No. 01 / 46

1s_1br_sq_side_split_bath_gr

Verified
1s_1br_sq_side_split_bath_gr · single-storey · Squarish · 1 bed · 1 bath

Squarish 1BR / 1 bath — shell is split vertically first (left = private: bedroom + bath, right = public: great room + kitchen), then each half splits horizontally (bedroom front / bath rear on the left; great room front / kitchen rear on the right). Bath reached only via the kitchen, never through the bedroom.

Definition

1s/1br/squarish/1s_1br_sq_side_split_bath_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

First 1BR entry in the squarish catalog. Floor band: roughly 24-36 sqm buildable — the natural footprint for a single-occupant or couple's starter home on a 60-72 sqm lot (typical socialized/economic PH subdivision cut) after setbacks.

Program: 1 bedroom, 1 common bath (no ensuite by design — a single ensuite bath would force guests to walk through the bedroom to use it, which the rest of the catalog's access rules already forbid for multi-bedroom units and which is just as poor practice here). The bath instead doors off the kitchen (per the reference '25 Sqm' plan -- bath and great_room sit in diagonal quadrants and never touch), so it works for the occupant and visitors alike without crossing the bedroom.

Layout intent (canonical form — brief.carport_side='right'): Front: [bedroom | great_room ] Rear: [bath | kitchen ]

Bedroom sits front-left with its only door opening onto the great_room. Bath sits directly behind it at rear-left, sharing its plumbing wall AND its door with the kitchen (rear-right). This is the smallest-footprint pattern in the 1BR catalog — a clean 2x2 room grid split by one straight vertical wall, no hallway, since a single bedroom and single bath don't need one to avoid cross-traffic.

Carport: modest 2.5 x 5.0 m single bay (vs. the 2BR catalog's 3.0 x 6.0 m) — sized for a compact car or motorcycle, reflecting the smaller lot this unit typically sits on. Declared as a setback element only; no building_void needed unless the specific lot's side setback is narrower than 2.5 m, in which case a void follows the same pattern as the 2BR sq topology.

When brief.carport_side='left', the runner mirrors the topology so bedroom/bath end up front-right/rear-right and kitchen sits rear-left.

BUGFIX (2026-07-16): left_anchored originally listed both 'bedroom' and 'bath', forcing both to the same left wall AND (via their separate required adjacencies to great_room and kitchen) implicitly forcing bedroom and bath to the exact same width -- combined with the solver's hardcoded kitchen/great_room front-to-rear stacking rule, this over-constrained the 4-room grid so no layout existed at ANY lot size (confirmed via CP-SAT infeasibility across a full sweep, 4-121 sqm floor). Fixed by dropping 'bath' from left_anchored -- bath's position is now driven purely by its own adjacencies (bath_door to great, wet_core to kitchen), same pattern the working 2BR squarish sibling already uses for its common bath. (Superseded by the STRAIGHT-CUT redesign below, which reinstates the double left-anchor now that the true root blocker is fixed.)

REDESIGN (2026-07-16): matched to a reference '25 Sqm' tiny-house floor plan (square-topology.png) at a true 5x5 m buildable envelope (9x9 m lot, standard 2 m setbacks -- NOT 1m setbacks, which fail PD 1096's 2.0 m window-yard minimum on the bedroom/bath). In that reference bath never reaches great_room's wall, so bath's door goes through kitchen exclusively; the old 'bath_access' door_host_group (default=great_room, alternate=kitchen) required bath to always reach great_room, which made 5x5 infeasible. Fixed by removing the bath<->great adjacency entirely and making bath<->kitchen kind='bath_door' directly.

STRAIGHT-CUT (2026-07-16): the envelope is now split by ONE straight vertical wall -- private column left (bedroom front / bath rear), public column right (great_room front / kitchen rear) -- via zone_split + match_widths [bedroom,bath] and [great,kitchen] + double left/right anchors. The true blocker for this layout was the solver's hard 'private total >= public total' area rule: the reference plan is public-heavy (bedroom 8.75 vs LDK 12.5 sqm), which no 1BR program can satisfy -- a single bedroom can't outweigh the LDK the way a multi-bedroom wing does. Disabled per-topology with the new private_area_floor: false flag (also skips the 55/45 soft bias). kitchen is mechanical_vent (its only exterior wall is the rear back-door wall). The compact profile's bedroom_standard set_max_area_sqm=20.0 uses the new cap-REPLACING knob (plain max_area_sqm can only lower the catalog's 12.0 ceiling for this type, never raise it). At 9x9 m (5x5 env): bedroom 2.6x3.5=9.1, bath 2.6x1.5=3.9, great 2.4x2.5=6.0, kitchen 2.4x2.5=6.0 -- 25.0/25 sqm tiled, zero snaps, COMPLIANT, 1 soft warning (intended bath->kitchen door). Sweep-verified square-lot range: 8.5x8.5 to 12x12 m (64 sqm floor -- already past the sensible 1BR ceiling; the 2BR band starts at 45 sqm floor per floor-area-per-br, so larger lots should be offered 2BR instead). 12.5x12.5 is achievable if ever needed by adding aspect_overrides {"bath": 2.5} (verified) -- the binding constraint there is bath's 2:1 aspect cap limiting the private column's width.

RESTRICTED TO COMPACT SIZES (2026-07-20): this topology's own briefs/test_sweep/ fixtures are now compact-only (8.5x8.5 and the near-square-1.15 9.77x8.5 point; med/max removed). Larger lots are expected to use the sibling LDK topology 1s_1br_sq_side_split_bath_ld instead (full living/dining/kitchen split, feasible 10x10-12x12) rather than this counter-divider great_room design stretched past its compact scale -- same pattern as the narrow 1BR gr/ld siblings. This topology itself still solves fine up to 12x12 (unchanged, no code removed), it's just no longer the recommended choice there.

Key adjacencies

  • bedroom ↔ bath — bedroom (front) and bath (rear) stack vertically along the left wall — shared wall only, no door between them. Keeps the bath from being accessible only through the bedroom, per the locked no-bedroom-through-bath / no-bath-through-bedroom rule.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • bedroom ↔ great — bedroom's only door opens east into the great_room — the one occupant's sole route in and out. Sized 0.9 m (vs the 0.8 m generic bedroom_to_public floor) to give a slightly more generous door for the single, larger bedroom this topology carries.bedroom_to_public
  • bath ↔ kitchen — bath's only door opens onto the kitchen — bath and kitchen sit rear-left/rear-right across the straight zone-split seam, sharing the plumbing wall AND the door. Bath never touches great_room (they sit in diagonal quadrants), so it's reached via the kitchen — still guest-accessible without walking through the bedroom.bath_door
  • kitchen ↔ great — great_room + kitchen form the open LDK (gr style). counter_divider: a 0.6 m dining counter (breakfast bar) renders along this open edge on the kitchen side, with 2 stools on the great_room side and a >= 0.9 m walk-through gap — dining happens at the counter in this compact plan. min_shared_wall_m raised 1.5 -> 2.1 (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass) so the counter always fits the realized edge.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (ultra_compact_sq_side_split_bath_gr, compact_sq_side_split_bath_gr).
  • match_widths — solver forces matching widths within each group: bedroom, bath; great, kitchen.
  • private_area_floor: false — the hard private-area-must-exceed-public rule is disabled (a single bedroom can't outweigh a full LDK).

Bath reached only via the kitchen, never through the bedroom.",

  "rooms": [
    {
      "id": "bedroom",
      "type": "bedroom_standard",
      "zone": "private",
      "size_priority": "other_bedrooms"
    },

      "id": "bath",
      "type": "common_bath",

      "size_priority": "service_and_baths"

      "zone": "public",
      "size_priority": "public_LDK",
      "mechanical_vent": true

  "adjacencies": [
    {
      "a": "bedroom",
      "b": "bath",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 1.2,
      "kind": "bath_to_bedroom_wall",
      "note": "bedroom (front) and bath (rear) stack vertically along the left wall \u2014 shared wall only, no door between them. Keeps the bath from being accessible only through the bedroom, per the locked no-bedroom-through-bath / no-bath-through-bedroom rule."
    },
    {
      "a": "bedroom",
      "b": "great",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 0.9,
      "kind": "bedroom_to_public",
      "note": "bedroom's only door opens east into the great_room \u2014 the one occupant's sole route in and out. Sized 0.9 m (vs the 0.8 m generic bedroom_to_public floor) to give a slightly more generous door for the single, larger bedroom this topology carries."
    },

    },

      "a": "kitchen",
      "b": "great",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 2.1,

      "counter_divider": true,
      "note": "great_room + kitchen form the open LDK (gr style). counter_divider: a 0.6 m dining counter (breakfast bar) renders along this open edge on the kitchen side, with 2 stools on the great_room side and a >= 0.9 m walk-through gap \u2014 dining happens at the counter in this compact plan. min_shared_wall_m raised 1.5 -> 2.1 (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass) so the counter always fits the realized edge."

  "lot_adjustment_profiles": [
    {
      "name": "ultra_compact_sq_side_split_bath_gr",
      "when": {
        "buildable_area_lt_sqm": 30
      },
      "auto_apply": {
        "kitchen": {
          "min_least_dim_m": 1.8
        },
        "common_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 2.8,
          "max_area_sqm": 4.5
        },
        "bedroom_standard": {
          "max_area_sqm": 9.5
        }
      },
      "rationale": "At true 5x5 m envelopes (~25 sqm), the bedroom's top priority weight lets it balloon past 11 sqm, crowding kitchen down to its bare hard minimum and leaving no room for great_room to absorb any freed space. Capping bedroom at 9.5 sqm (close to the reference '25 Sqm' tiny-house layout's 8.75 sqm bedroom) lets snap_gaps() redistribute the freed area into great_room instead. This cap is WRONG for larger lots (it blocked the 40-90 sqm range entirely when tested at buildable_area_lt_sqm=100) so it's scoped to only this ultra-compact tier, below the broader compact_sq_side_split_bath_gr profile's 100 sqm threshold."
    },
    {
      "name": "compact_sq_side_split_bath_gr",
      "when": {
        "buildable_area_lt_sqm": 100
      },
      "auto_apply": {
        "kitchen": {
          "min_least_dim_m": 1.8
        },
        "common_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 2.8,
          "max_area_sqm": 4.5
        },
        "bedroom_standard": {
          "set_max_area_sqm": 20.0
        }
      },
      "rationale": "1BR footprints run smaller than the 2BR catalog's compact band, so the threshold drops to 40 sqm buildable -- widened to 100 sqm (2026-07-16) so the common_bath max_area_sqm cap covers the whole practical 1BR range (matches the 2BR baths_ds_ld precedent's 100 sqm threshold). Kitchen floors at 1.8 m least-dim for a usable single-run galley; bath floors at 2.8 sqm and caps at 4.5 sqm (matches the room_catalog's soft preferred_area_sqm upper bound) -- without the cap, snap_gaps() was dumping leftover envelope area into the bath uncapped (e.g. 10.8 sqm on a 10x10 m lot, ~30% of the whole house) since no other room absorbed the gap on some lot sizes. Below 30 sqm, the ultra_compact_sq_side_split_bath_gr profile above matches first and additionally caps bedroom_standard tighter (9.5 sqm). bedroom_standard set_max_area_sqm=20.0 (2026-07-16): restores the headroom this room had when it was typed master_bedroom (catalog ceiling 20.0 vs bedroom_standard's 12.0). Uses set_max_area_sqm -- the cap-REPLACING knob added for exactly this case -- because plain max_area_sqm can only lower a type's catalog ceiling, never raise it."
    }
  ],
  "setback_elements": [
    {
      "type": "carport",
      "location": "side_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "width_m": 2.5,
      "depth_m": 5.0
    },
    {
      "type": "dirty_kitchen",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "behind": "kitchen"
    },
    {
      "type": "service_area",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false
    }
  ],
  "building_voids": [],

Test brief

test/1s_1br_9x9_sq_side_split_bath_gr_ncp.json

1BR / 1 bath squarish smoke test, no carport. 9x9 lot gives a true 5x5 envelope = 25 m2 floor, reproducing the reference '25 Sqm' tiny-house layout (square-topology.png): one straight vertical cut, bedroom+bath column left, great+kitchen column right, 25.0/25 tiled.

Lot 9×9 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 5×5 m buildable envelope — 25 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot9 × 9 m (81 m²)
Buildable envelope5 × 5 m (25 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested1
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

single common bath · bath door opens into kitchen, not great_room · open-plan great room + kitchen · no carport

1s_1br_sq_side_split_bath_ld

Verified
1s_1br_sq_side_split_bath_ld · single-storey · Squarish · 1 bed · 1 bath

Squarish 1BR / 1 bath, LD style — PROOF-OF-CONCEPT sibling of 1s_1br_sq_side_split_bath_gr: same private column (bedroom front / bath rear, left), but the public column splits into separate living / dining / kitchen (stacked front-to-rear) instead of one great_room + kitchen.

Definition

1s/1br/squarish/1s_1br_sq_side_split_bath_ld.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

PROOF-OF-CONCEPT topology -- created 2026-07-20 to answer a feasibility question (does a true living/dining/kitchen split work for a squarish 1BR program, or is the counter_divider great_room approach the only thing that fits). NOT yet swept for min/med/max, NOT yet added to briefs/test/, NOT yet in the published artol-topologies catalog or CLAUDE.md. If it proves out, treat this file as a draft to be tuned (lot_adjustment_profiles especially) before it graduates to a real catalog entry.

Direct sibling of 1s_1br_sq_side_split_bath_gr: identical private column (bedroom front-left / bath rear-left). The public column swaps great_room + kitchen (2-room open-plan) for living + dining + kitchen (3-room stack, living front / dining mid / kitchen rear), following the same wiring pattern as the working 1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_ds_ld sibling: living/dining/kitchen are NOT width-matched to each other (per that precedent, which lets the public column's rooms vary in width rather than over-constraining a 3-way match) -- only right_anchored on kitchen, not living/dining, for the same over-constrained-anchors reason documented across this catalog's 3BR fixes.

Key adjacencies

  • bedroom ↔ bath — bedroom (front) and bath (rear) stack vertically along the left wall -- shared wall only, no door between them, same as the gr sibling.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • bedroom ↔ living — bedroom's only door opens east into living -- the arrival room, same role great_room played in the gr sibling.bedroom_to_public
  • bath ↔ kitchen — bath's only door opens onto kitchen, same as the gr sibling -- both are rear_anchored so they align at the rear wall regardless of the public column now stacking 3 rooms instead of 2.bath_door
  • living ↔ dining — living and dining flow as one open public space -- no wall (ld style, matches the working 2BR _ld precedent).open_plan
  • dining ↔ kitchen — dining flows into kitchen -- open pass-through (PH practice, matches the working 2BR _ld precedent).open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_sq_side_split_bath_ld).
  • match_widths — solver forces matching widths within each group: bedroom, bath.
  • private_area_floor: false — the hard private-area-must-exceed-public rule is disabled (a single bedroom can't outweigh a full LDK).

Test brief

test/1s_1br_11x11_sq_side_split_bath_ld_ncp.json

1BR / 1 bath squarish LDK smoke test, no carport. 11x11 lot gives a 7x7 envelope = 49 m2 floor -- the LDK sibling of 1s_1br_sq_side_split_bath_gr, which stays compact-only (min is always the gr topology's job). Separate living/dining/kitchen instead of a counter-divider great_room; feasible 10x10-12x12, this is the smaller (med) end of that range.

Lot 11×11 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 7×7 m buildable envelope — 49 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot11 × 11 m (121 m²)
Buildable envelope7 × 7 m (49 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested1
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

single common bath · bath door opens into kitchen, not living · separate living, dining, and kitchen rooms · no carport

1s_1br_nw_front_back_split_bath_gr

Verified
1s_1br_nw_front_back_split_bath_gr · single-storey · Narrow · 1 bed · 1 bath

Narrow-shell 1BR / 1 bath, FRONT-BACK SPLIT — public front band (great room left with the entry, galley kitchen along the right wall) and private rear band (bedroom left, bath right). Kitchen and great room sit side-by-side with a dining counter (breakfast bar) dividing them; kitchen does NOT touch the rear wall — the bath stacks behind it on the plumbing line. Based on the narrow-fp-01 reference plan (4 x 6 m unit).

Definition

1s/1br/narrow/1s_1br_nw_front_back_split_bath_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

Second 1BR entry in the narrow catalog, and the first FRONT-BACK SPLIT narrow topology. Modeled on the narrow-fp-01 reference plan (a 4.00 x 6.00 m unit, 24 sqm): public front band with the entry into the great room (Sala de Estar/Jantar) and a galley kitchen (Cozinha) along the right wall; private rear band with the bedroom (Quarto, ~3.45 m deep) left and the bath (Banheiro, ~1.80 m deep) right. Dining happens at a counter between kitchen and great room (counter_divider with 2 stools), not at a table.

Layout intent (front at bottom): Rear: [BEDROOM | BATH ] Front: [GREAT ROOM ][|||| KITCHEN] (|||| = dining counter + stools) [porch/entry]

Canonical fit: 8 x 10 m lot with standard 2 m setbacks -> exactly the reference's 4 x 6 m envelope (shell ratio 0.67, 'deep' category).

Global-rule overrides this topology depends on (all topology-scoped, zero effect on the rest of the catalog): - kitchen_rear_pin: false — the bath stacks BEHIND the kitchen, so the hardcoded kitchen-touches-rear-wall pin is impossible here. The zone_split horizontal escape can't be used instead: the front/rear seam is STEPPED (bedroom ~3.45 deep vs bath ~1.8; kitchen ~4.2 vs great ~2.55), and zone_split needs one straight split line. This flag was added for exactly this topology (2026-07-16). - ldk_horizontal: true — kitchen and great room are side-by-side; the default LDK must-stack rule and the kitchen-right-half symmetry break both contradict that. - private_area_floor: false — single bedroom (~8.6 sqm) can never outweigh great+kitchen (~12+ sqm); same rationale as the squarish 1BR sibling. - aspect_overrides kitchen 3.2 — the reference galley runs ~1.4 x 4.2 (3:1), past the catalog's 2.5:1 kitchen cap. - kitchen mechanical_vent — the galley's exterior walls are the front (entry side) and right; on tight envelopes the window may not reach the 10% floor, and the open counter edge borrows the great room's light anyway.

match_widths [bedroom,great] + [kitchen,bath] gives one straight vertical seam between the left and right columns (the reference has a slightly stepped seam — bath wider than kitchen — but the clean grid is within ~15 cm of the reference proportions and far more robust to solve).

Dirty kitchen (2026-07-22): kitchen's rear wall is interior (shared with bath), so the default rear-behind-kitchen service-door placement is impossible here. Declared setback_elements[dirty_kitchen].location = 'side_setback' instead — puts the service pocket, and the kitchen's exterior service door, on kitchen's side wall (right, non-carport side). Opt-in via brief.dirty_kitchen = true. service_area stays available in the rear setback behind the bath (shares the plumbing line).

No fallback declared — the squarish 1BR sibling targets a different shell category, so a shell-mismatch fallback would be misleading.

Key adjacencies

  • great ↔ bedroom — bedroom (rear-left) and great room (front-left) stack in the left column — shared wall only, NO door (2026-07-16 revision: the door moved to the kitchen seam so the great room's rear wall stays solid/furnishable). Bedroom keeps this public adjacency for the validator's bedroom-access rule.bedroom_to_public_wall
  • bedroom ↔ kitchen — bedroom's only door — opens west-to-east into the kitchen aisle at the rear end of the galley (2026-07-16 revision, per client direction: entering the bedroom from the kitchen side frees the great room's full wall run for furniture). 1.2 m floor = 0.9 m clear door + frames.bedroom_door
  • great ↔ kitchen — great room and galley kitchen sit SIDE-BY-SIDE in the front band (vertical seam) — open plan with a dining counter (breakfast bar) along the shared edge. counter_side: 'great' puts the band INSIDE the great room (kitchen keeps its full 1.5 m aisle), with the 2 stools beside it in the great room. min_shared_wall_m 2.1 = 2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass-through. Requires ldk_horizontal (the default kitchen/great must-stack rule would forbid this arrangement).open_plan
  • bedroom ↔ bath — bedroom and bath share the rear band side-by-side — shared wall only, no door, per the locked no-bath-through-bedroom rule.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • bath ↔ kitchen — bath (rear-right) stacks directly behind the kitchen (front-right) on one plumbing line — shared wet wall AND the bath's only door, opening south into the kitchen aisle. Same guest-accessible-without-crossing-the-bedroom pattern as the squarish 1BR sibling.bath_door

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_nw_front_back_split_bath_gr).
  • match_widths — solver forces matching widths within each group: bedroom, great; kitchen, bath.
  • private_area_floor: false — the hard private-area-must-exceed-public rule is disabled (a single bedroom can't outweigh a full LDK).
  • aspect_overrides — per-room aspect-ratio (w:h) floor for kitchen (3.2:1).
  • kitchen_rear_pin: false — overrides the solver's default kitchen-rear-wall placement rule.
  • ldk_horizontal: true — disables the solver's default vertical LDK-stacking rules so great_room/kitchen can sit side-by-side.

      "covered": false,
      "behind": "kitchen",
      "note": "kitchen's rear wall is interior (bath stacks behind it), so there's no rear wall to put a service door on. Kitchen's only other exterior wall is its side wall (right, non-carport side) \u2014 dirty kitchen moves there instead, same side_setback pattern as 1s_2br_nw_front_back_split_bath. Gives the kitchen an exterior service door (2026-07-22, user request)."
    },
    {
      "type": "service_area",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false
    }
  ],
  "building_voids": [],
  "notes": [
    "Second 1BR entry in the narrow catalog, and the first FRONT-BACK SPLIT narrow",
    "topology. Modeled on the narrow-fp-01 reference plan (a 4.00 x 6.00 m unit,",
    "24 sqm): public front band with the entry into the great room (Sala de",
    "Estar/Jantar) and a galley kitchen (Cozinha) along the right wall; private",
    "rear band with the bedroom (Quarto, ~3.45 m deep) left and the bath",
    "(Banheiro, ~1.80 m deep) right. Dining happens at a counter between kitchen",
    "and great room (counter_divider with 2 stools), not at a table.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (front at bottom):",
    "  Rear:  [BEDROOM        | BATH   ]",
    "  Front: [GREAT ROOM ][|||| KITCHEN]   (|||| = dining counter + stools)",
    "         [porch/entry]",
    "",
    "Canonical fit: 8 x 10 m lot with standard 2 m setbacks -> exactly the",
    "reference's 4 x 6 m envelope (shell ratio 0.67, 'deep' category).",
    "",
    "Global-rule overrides this topology depends on (all topology-scoped,",
    "zero effect on the rest of the catalog):",
    "  - kitchen_rear_pin: false \u2014 the bath stacks BEHIND the kitchen, so the",
    "    hardcoded kitchen-touches-rear-wall pin is impossible here. The",
    "    zone_split horizontal escape can't be used instead: the front/rear",
    "    seam is STEPPED (bedroom ~3.45 deep vs bath ~1.8; kitchen ~4.2 vs",
    "    great ~2.55), and zone_split needs one straight split line. This flag",
    "    was added for exactly this topology (2026-07-16).",
    "  - ldk_horizontal: true \u2014 kitchen and great room are side-by-side; the",
    "    default LDK must-stack rule and the kitchen-right-half symmetry break",
    "    both contradict that.",
    "  - private_area_floor: false \u2014 single bedroom (~8.6 sqm) can never",
    "    outweigh great+kitchen (~12+ sqm); same rationale as the squarish",
    "    1BR sibling.",
    "  - aspect_overrides kitchen 3.2 \u2014 the reference galley runs ~1.4 x 4.2",
    "    (3:1), past the catalog's 2.5:1 kitchen cap.",
    "  - kitchen mechanical_vent \u2014 the galley's exterior walls are the front",
    "    (entry side) and right; on tight envelopes the window may not reach",
    "    the 10% floor, and the open counter edge borrows the great room's",
    "    light anyway.",
    "",
    "match_widths [bedroom,great] + [kitchen,bath] gives one straight vertical",
    "seam between the left and right columns (the reference has a slightly",
    "stepped seam \u2014 bath wider than kitchen \u2014 but the clean grid is within",
    "~15 cm of the reference proportions and far more robust to solve).",
    "",
    "Dirty kitchen (2026-07-22): kitchen's rear wall is interior (shared with",
    "bath), so the default rear-behind-kitchen service-door placement is",
    "impossible here. Declared setback_elements[dirty_kitchen].location =",
    "'side_setback' instead \u2014 puts the service pocket, and the kitchen's",
    "exterior service door, on kitchen's side wall (right, non-carport side).",
    "Opt-in via brief.dirty_kitchen = true. service_area stays available in",
    "the rear setback behind the bath (shares the plumbing line).",
    "",
    "No fallback declared \u2014 the squarish 1BR sibling targets a different",
    "shell category, so a shell-mismatch fallback would be misleading."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/1s_1br_8x10_nw_front_back_split_bath_gr_ncp.json

1BR / 1 bath narrow front-back-split smoke test, no carport. 8x10 lot with standard 2 m setbacks gives exactly the narrow-fp-01 reference plan's 4x6 m envelope (24 m2): bedroom+bath rear band, great room + galley kitchen front band with dining-counter divider and entry into the great room. dirty_kitchen on — kitchen's rear wall is interior (bath behind it), so its exterior service door is on the side wall instead.

Lot 8×10 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 4×6 m buildable envelope — 24 m² floor, requesting no carport.

No. 04 / 46

1s_1br_nw_front_back_split_bath_ld

Verified
1s_1br_nw_front_back_split_bath_ld · single-storey · Narrow · 1 bed · 1 bath

Narrow-shell 1BR / 1 bath, LD style -- sibling of 1s_1br_nw_front_back_split_bath_gr for lots too big to stay compact: full-width living room across the front (hosts entry), dining and kitchen side-by-side in a mid band behind it, bedroom and bath side-by-side in the rear band (same private-band layout as the gr sibling).

Definition

1s/1br/narrow/1s_1br_nw_front_back_split_bath_ld.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

Sibling of 1s_1br_nw_front_back_split_bath_gr, created 2026-07-20 for lots too big to stay compact -- the gr sibling is now deliberately restricted to its canonical 8x10 (see its own sweep fixture notes). This one trades the gr sibling's counter-divider great_room for a real 3-band public sequence:

Front (full width): LIVING ROOM (entry) Mid: [DINING | KITCHEN] Rear: [BEDROOM | BATH ]

living is double-anchored (left AND right) so it always spans the full envelope width, the same mechanism the front_rear_bath_gr sibling uses to pin its full-width bedroom. Behind it the building reverts to the gr sibling's familiar two-column structure: dining stacks where great used to be (left column, above bedroom), kitchen and bath are UNCHANGED from the gr sibling (right column, kitchen mid above bath rear, one wet-plumbing line).

bedroom's door now opens into dining (its direct front neighbor) rather than reaching across to kitchen the way the gr sibling's does -- that routing was specifically to keep the gr sibling's great_room wall clear for furniture, which doesn't apply here since dining is a separate room.

kitchen_rear_pin: false and ldk_horizontal: true carried over from the gr sibling for the same structural reasons (bath stacks behind kitchen, not the rear wall; dining/kitchen are side-by-side, not vertically stacked).

Kitchen exterior door (2026-07-22): kitchen's only exterior wall is its side wall (right, non-carport side; front is interior against dining/living, rear is interior against bath). No dirty_kitchen setback element is declared here, so no visible service-yard box is drawn -- but the door-generator's shared default (brief.kitchen_back_door, catalog-wide default true) now searches rear THEN side walls for any topology, so the kitchen still gets a plain exterior door on that side wall automatically. See the sibling gr topology for the alternative (a declared side-setback dirty_kitchen, which also draws the box) if a visible service pocket is wanted here too.

No lot_adjustment_profiles yet -- untuned, pending real feasibility testing at its intended med/max sizes (9x11, 10x12).

Key adjacencies

  • living ↔ dining — living spans the full width; dining sits directly behind its left half -- open mouth into dining, no wall (ld style).open_plan
  • living ↔ kitchen — living's other open mouth, into kitchen behind its right half. Two separate open-plan seams along one continuous rear wall of the living room (same pattern as a hallway opening onto two rooms elsewhere in this catalog).open_plan
  • dining ↔ kitchen — dining and kitchen sit side-by-side in the mid band -- open pass-through, no wall (PH practice, matches the working _ld precedents).open_plan
  • dining ↔ bedroom — bedroom's only door -- opens into dining, its direct front neighbor in the same (left) column. Simpler than the gr sibling's kitchen-side door since dining is right there; no furniture-wall reason to route it further.bedroom_door
  • bedroom ↔ bath — bedroom and bath share the rear band side-by-side -- shared wall only, no door, per the locked no-bath-through-bedroom rule.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • bath ↔ kitchen — bath (rear-right) stacks directly behind kitchen (mid-right) on one plumbing line -- shared wet wall AND the bath's only door, same wet-stack pattern as the gr sibling.bath_door

Notable overrides

  • match_widths — solver forces matching widths within each group: bedroom, dining; kitchen, bath.
  • private_area_floor: false — the hard private-area-must-exceed-public rule is disabled (a single bedroom can't outweigh a full LDK).
  • kitchen_rear_pin: false — overrides the solver's default kitchen-rear-wall placement rule.
  • ldk_horizontal: true — disables the solver's default vertical LDK-stacking rules so great_room/kitchen can sit side-by-side.

Test brief

test/1s_1br_9x11_nw_front_back_split_bath_ld_ncp.json

1BR / 1 bath narrow LDK smoke test, no carport. 9x11 lot gives a 5x7 envelope = 35 m2 floor -- the LDK sibling of 1s_1br_nw_front_back_split_bath_gr, which stays compact-only (min is always the gr topology's job). Full-width living room at the front, dining + kitchen side-by-side behind it. This is the smaller (med) end of its 9x11-10x12 feasible range and runs tight -- bedroom and dining both sit close to their PD 1096 hard floors -- but solves COMPLIANT with no hard errors.

Lot 9×11 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 5×7 m buildable envelope — 35 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot9 × 11 m (99 m²)
Buildable envelope5 × 7 m (35 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested1
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

single common bath · bath door opens into kitchen · full-width living room hosting the entry · separate dining and kitchen side-by-side behind living · no carport

No. 05 / 46

1s_1br_nw_front_rear_bath_gr

Verified
1s_1br_nw_front_rear_bath_gr · single-storey · Narrow · 1 bed · 1 bath

Narrow-shell 1BR / 1 bath, FRONT-REAR — the bedroom owns the full-width rear band; in front of it, a full-depth great room column (hosts the entry) runs beside a wet column with the kitchen above the bath on one plumbing wall (bath at the front corner, door off the great room). Dining at a counter on the kitchen/great seam. Based on the narrow-fp-02 reference plan (3.2 x 7.0 m micro-unit).

Definition

1s/1br/narrow/1s_1br_nw_front_rear_bath_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

Third 1BR entry in the narrow catalog, and the first use of the locked 'front_rear' strategy token: the bedroom owns the FULL-WIDTH rear band, and everything else sits in front of it. Modeled on the narrow-fp-02 reference plan (a 3.2 x 7.0 m / 22.4 sqm Brazilian micro-unit): bedroom across the rear with a service/laundry area behind it, living in the middle with a counter-attached table, kitchen run along one side wall, bath at the front corner, entry on the front wall beside the bath.

Layout intent (front at bottom, canonical 4 x 8 m envelope): Rear: [BEDROOM 4.0 x ~2.4 (full width) ] Middle: [KITCHEN 1.5x~3.6 | GREAT ROOM (cont.) ] Front: [BATH 1.5x~2.0 | GREAT ~2.5 full-depth] [porch/entry]

Canonical fit: 8 x 12 m lot with standard 2 m setbacks -> 4 x 8 m envelope (ratio 0.5, 'super_deep' category, 32 sqm floor). Roomier than the 24-25 sqm tiny-house set — the ~14 sqm great room clears the couch+TV+dining furnishability band, though dining stays at the counter per the reference.

Global-rule overrides (all topology-scoped): - kitchen_rear_pin: false — the bedroom owns the rear wall; the kitchen sits mid-column with the bath in front of it. Same flag as the front_back_split sibling. Kitchen has no exterior rear wall, but the shared kitchen-door generator (2026-07-22) searches rear then side walls, so brief.kitchen_back_door (default true) still gets the kitchen a plain exterior door on its side (left) wall automatically. - ldk_horizontal: true — kitchen beside the great room (vertical seam), and the kitchen sits in the LEFT half (symmetry break would flip it). - private_area_floor: false — bedroom ~9.4 sqm vs great+kitchen ~19.6. - aspect_overrides great 2.3 — the full-depth great column runs ~2.5 x 5.65 (2.26:1), past the strict 1.8 cap (its width stays under the 3.0 m chunky-relax threshold).

No zone_split: the full-width bedroom crosses both columns, so no single straight private/public line exists — the two front_to_rear_stacks ([bath, kitchen, bedroom] and [great, bedroom]) plus side anchors pin the structure instead. The bedroom is anchored to BOTH side walls, which forces its full-width span; with the 9.5 sqm cap that pins the rear band's depth at ~2.4 m.

Wet stack: bath and kitchen share one vertical plumbing wall; the rear service_area (laundry, per the reference's washer) sits in the rear setback behind the bedroom. No dirty kitchen declared — the kitchen has no rear exposure, so a rear-setback dirty kitchen box wouldn't make sense; it still gets a plain exterior service door via the side-wall fallback above, just no visible service-yard box.

Bath door opens into the GREAT room (as in the reference) — no bath-into-kitchen sanitary warning.

No fallback declared — the other narrow 1BR topologies have different structural patterns (corridor shotgun, stepped front-back split) rather than being relaxations of this one.

Key adjacencies

  • great ↔ bedroom — bedroom's only door — opens south from the full-width rear band into the great room column. 1.2 m floor = 0.9 m clear door + frames.bedroom_to_public
  • kitchen ↔ bedroom — the kitchen's rear edge meets the bedroom band's underside on the left column — geometry-only shared wall, no door. Keeps the seam between the front zone and the rear band continuous.wall_only
  • bath ↔ kitchen — bath (front) and kitchen (above it) stack in the left column on one plumbing wall — shared wall only, no door.wet_core
  • bath ↔ great — bath's only door — opens east into the great room near the entry, matching the reference plan. No bath-into-kitchen sanitary trade-off in this topology.bath_door
  • kitchen ↔ great — kitchen and great room sit SIDE-BY-SIDE (vertical seam) — open plan with a dining counter along the shared edge, band + 2 stools inside the great room (counter_side) so the kitchen keeps its full work aisle; mirrors the reference plan's counter-attached table. min_shared_wall_m 2.1 = 2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass. Requires ldk_horizontal.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_nw_front_rear_bath_gr).
  • match_widths — solver forces matching widths within each group: bath, kitchen.
  • private_area_floor: false — the hard private-area-must-exceed-public rule is disabled (a single bedroom can't outweigh a full LDK).
  • aspect_overrides — per-room aspect-ratio (w:h) floor for great (2.3:1).
  • kitchen_rear_pin: false — overrides the solver's default kitchen-rear-wall placement rule.
  • ldk_horizontal: true — disables the solver's default vertical LDK-stacking rules so great_room/kitchen can sit side-by-side.
1s_1br_nw_front_rear_bath_gr.json 192 lines · 7.5 KB
Download

        "buildable_area_lt_sqm": 45

        "common_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 2.6,
          "max_area_sqm": 4.5

        }
      },
      "rationale": "Compact super-deep unit (canonical 4x8 m envelope, 32 sqm \u2014 reference plan is 22.4). Same tier values as the other 1BR topologies: bath floors at 2.6 sqm and caps at 4.5 so snap_gaps can't balloon it; bedroom caps at 9.5 \u2014 with the full 4 m width anchored to both side walls, that pins the rear band's depth at ~2.4 m and pushes the freed depth into the great room."
    }
  ],
  "setback_elements": [
    {
      "type": "service_area",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false
    }
  ],
  "building_voids": [],
  "notes": [
    "Third 1BR entry in the narrow catalog, and the first use of the locked",
    "'front_rear' strategy token: the bedroom owns the FULL-WIDTH rear band,",
    "and everything else sits in front of it. Modeled on the narrow-fp-02",
    "reference plan (a 3.2 x 7.0 m / 22.4 sqm Brazilian micro-unit): bedroom",
    "across the rear with a service/laundry area behind it, living in the",
    "middle with a counter-attached table, kitchen run along one side wall,",
    "bath at the front corner, entry on the front wall beside the bath.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (front at bottom, canonical 4 x 8 m envelope):",
    "  Rear:   [BEDROOM  4.0 x ~2.4  (full width)      ]",
    "  Middle: [KITCHEN 1.5x~3.6 | GREAT ROOM (cont.)  ]",
    "  Front:  [BATH    1.5x~2.0 | GREAT ~2.5 full-depth]",
    "                               [porch/entry]",
    "",
    "Canonical fit: 8 x 12 m lot with standard 2 m setbacks -> 4 x 8 m envelope",
    "(ratio 0.5, 'super_deep' category, 32 sqm floor). Roomier than the 24-25",
    "sqm tiny-house set \u2014 the ~14 sqm great room clears the couch+TV+dining",
    "furnishability band, though dining stays at the counter per the reference.",
    "",
    "Global-rule overrides (all topology-scoped):",
    "  - kitchen_rear_pin: false \u2014 the bedroom owns the rear wall; the kitchen",
    "    sits mid-column with the bath in front of it. Same flag as the",
    "    front_back_split sibling. Kitchen has no exterior rear wall, but the",
    "    shared kitchen-door generator (2026-07-22) searches rear then side",
    "    walls, so brief.kitchen_back_door (default true) still gets the",
    "    kitchen a plain exterior door on its side (left) wall automatically.",
    "  - ldk_horizontal: true \u2014 kitchen beside the great room (vertical seam),",
    "    and the kitchen sits in the LEFT half (symmetry break would flip it).",
    "  - private_area_floor: false \u2014 bedroom ~9.4 sqm vs great+kitchen ~19.6.",
    "  - aspect_overrides great 2.3 \u2014 the full-depth great column runs",
    "    ~2.5 x 5.65 (2.26:1), past the strict 1.8 cap (its width stays under",
    "    the 3.0 m chunky-relax threshold).",
    "",
    "No zone_split: the full-width bedroom crosses both columns, so no single",
    "straight private/public line exists \u2014 the two front_to_rear_stacks",
    "([bath, kitchen, bedroom] and [great, bedroom]) plus side anchors pin the",
    "structure instead. The bedroom is anchored to BOTH side walls, which",
    "forces its full-width span; with the 9.5 sqm cap that pins the rear",
    "band's depth at ~2.4 m.",
    "",
    "Wet stack: bath and kitchen share one vertical plumbing wall; the rear",
    "service_area (laundry, per the reference's washer) sits in the rear",
    "setback behind the bedroom. No dirty kitchen declared \u2014 the kitchen has",
    "no rear exposure, so a rear-setback dirty kitchen box wouldn't make",
    "sense; it still gets a plain exterior service door via the side-wall",
    "fallback above, just no visible service-yard box.",
    "",
    "Bath door opens into the GREAT room (as in the reference) \u2014 no",
    "bath-into-kitchen sanitary warning.",
    "",
    "No fallback declared \u2014 the other narrow 1BR topologies have different",
    "structural patterns (corridor shotgun, stepped front-back split) rather",
    "than being relaxations of this one."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/1s_1br_8x10_nw_front_rear_bath_gr_ncp.json

1BR / 1 bath narrow front-rear smoke test, no carport. 8x10 lot with standard 2 m setbacks gives a 4x6 m envelope (24 m2, ratio 0.67, deep) — the narrow-fp-02 reference plan scaled to its own 22.4 m2 footprint: full-width bedroom across the rear, full-depth great room column with the entry and counter dining, wet column with the kitchen above the bath at the front corner. kitchen has no exterior rear wall, but the shared kitchen-door generator now searches side walls too, so it still gets a plain exterior door on its side (left) wall.

Lot 8×10 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 4×6 m buildable envelope — 24 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot8 × 10 m (80 m²)
Buildable envelope4 × 6 m (24 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested1
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

bedroom spanning the full width at the rear · bath at the front corner with its door off the great room · kitchen above the bath on one plumbing wall · counter dining between kitchen and great room · no carport

1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_gr

Verified
1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_gr · single-storey · Wide · 1 bed · 1 bath

Wide-shell 1BR / 1 bath, SIDE SPLIT — full-depth bedroom column on one side; the other side splits front/back into a great room (front, hosts entry) and a rear service band with the bath and kitchen side-by-side. The T&B sits BETWEEN the bedroom and the kitchen, buffering the bedroom from kitchen noise; its door opens from the kitchen aisle. Sibling of 1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_gr (same 6x4 m program, wet rooms in a rear band instead of an end column).

Definition

1s/1br/wide/1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

Third 1BR entry in the wide catalog; sibling of 1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_gr (same ~24 sqm program on the same canonical 10x8 lot / 6x4 m envelope) with a different service arrangement: instead of a wet END COLUMN, the bath and kitchen form a side-by-side REAR BAND above the great room, with the bath between the bedroom and the kitchen.

Layout intent (front at bottom, canonical form — kitchen on the right per the catalog's symmetry convention; a left-mirrored brief flips the whole plan, giving the kitchen|bath|bedroom reading order): Rear: [BEDROOM (cont.) | BATH | KITCHEN] Front: [BEDROOM | GREAT ROOM ] [porch/entry]

Bath access (2026-07-16 revision): the bath door opens from the KITCHEN side (a fixed door on the plumbing wall) — freeing the great room's entire rear-wall left segment, its prime furniture wall (the front wall carries the entry + windows). This accepts the bath_door_into_kitchen sanitary soft warning, like the squarish and narrow siblings. A door_host group was tried and abandoned: Pass 1a's auto-scorer always flips the door back to the great room (front-door-spine circulation overlap, +4.0 weight, dwarfs freed-wall minus sanitary), so only a fixed door holds this choice.

This is the LEAST override-hungry 1BR topology: kitchen+great stack front-to-rear (default LDK rule holds), kitchen touches the rear wall (rear-pin, dirty kitchen, and back door conventions all intact), and the kitchen sits in the right half (symmetry break satisfied). Only private_area_floor: false is needed (bedroom ~9 sqm vs great+kitchen ~12 sqm — same 1BR rationale as all siblings).

Geometry note: the rear band's uniform depth needs no explicit constraint — bath<->great and kitchen<->great must both touch, and with both rear- anchored that forces bath.depth == kitchen.depth automatically.

Dining is at the counter (counter_divider, counter_side great), on the HORIZONTAL kitchen/great seam — first counter use on a N/S wall in the wide catalog. The 2.1 m min_shared_wall doubles as the kitchen's width floor, leaving the bath ~1.6 m of band width beside it.

No fallback declared. Not a mirror-image variant of split_wing — the service arrangement (band vs column), bath position (buffering vs front), and LDK orientation (stacked vs side-by-side) all differ.

Key adjacencies

  • bedroom ↔ great — bedroom's only door — opens east into the great room across the column seam. 1.2 m floor = 0.9 m clear door + frames.bedroom_to_public
  • bedroom ↔ bath — bath sits directly beside the bedroom in the rear band — shared wall only, no door, per the locked no-bath-through-bedroom rule. The bath is deliberately BETWEEN the bedroom and the kitchen, buffering the bedroom from kitchen noise/smells.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • bath ↔ kitchen — bath's only door — opens east into the kitchen aisle, on the shared plumbing wall (2026-07-16 revision, per client direction): routing the bath through the kitchen frees the great room's entire rear-wall left segment for furniture (the prime TV/console wall) and consolidates all service circulation in one pocket. Accepts the bath_door_into_kitchen sanitary soft warning, same trade-off as the squarish and narrow siblings. NOTE: authored as a FIXED door, not a door_host_group — with a group, Pass 1a's auto-scorer always flips the door back to the great room (the front-door spine's +4.0 circulation-overlap weight dwarfs the freed-wall bonus minus the sanitary penalty), so a group cannot hold the kitchen as the effective default.bath_door
  • bath ↔ great — solid wall — no door (the bath doors off the kitchen instead, keeping this stretch of the great room's rear wall furnishable). This adjacency also pins the rear band's depth: bath and kitchen must BOTH touch the great room's rear edge, which forces them to equal depth with no extra constraint.wall_only
  • kitchen ↔ great — kitchen (rear) and great room (front) STACK — the catalog's default LDK arrangement, no ldk_horizontal needed. Open plan with a dining counter along the horizontal seam, band + 2 stools inside the great room (counter_side) so the kitchen keeps its full work aisle. min_shared_wall_m 2.1 = 2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass; also floors the kitchen's width at 2.1 m.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_wd_side_split_bath_gr).
  • private_area_floor: false — the hard private-area-must-exceed-public rule is disabled (a single bedroom can't outweigh a full LDK).

    "Geometry note: the rear band's uniform depth needs no explicit constraint \u2014",
    "bath<->great and kitchen<->great must both touch, and with both rear-",
    "anchored that forces bath.depth == kitchen.depth automatically.",
    "",
    "Dining is at the counter (counter_divider, counter_side great), on the",
    "HORIZONTAL kitchen/great seam \u2014 first counter use on a N/S wall in the",
    "wide catalog. The 2.1 m min_shared_wall doubles as the kitchen's width",
    "floor, leaving the bath ~1.6 m of band width beside it.",
    "",
    "No fallback declared. Not a mirror-image variant of split_wing \u2014 the",
    "service arrangement (band vs column), bath position (buffering vs front),",
    "and LDK orientation (stacked vs side-by-side) all differ."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/1s_1br_10x8_wd_side_split_bath_gr_ncp.json

1BR / 1 bath wide side-split smoke test, no carport. 10x8 lot with standard 2 m setbacks gives a 6x4 m envelope (24 m2, ratio 1.5): full-depth bedroom column, rear service band with bath and kitchen side-by-side (bath between bedroom and kitchen as an acoustic buffer), great room in front with the entry and counter dining on the horizontal kitchen seam.

Lot 10×8 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 6×4 m buildable envelope — 24 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot10 × 8 m (80 m²)
Buildable envelope6 × 4 m (24 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested1
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

bath and kitchen side-by-side in a rear band on one plumbing wall · bath between bedroom and kitchen · great room in front of the service band with counter dining · no carport

1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_ld

Verified
1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_ld · single-storey · Wide · 1 bed · 1 bath

Wide-shell 1BR / 1 bath, LD style -- sibling of 1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_gr: full-depth bedroom column on one side; the other side keeps the gr sibling's exact 2-band depth (front public band / rear bath+kitchen band), splitting the front band into living (hosts entry, left) + dining (right) side-by-side instead of one great_room. Dining stacks front-to-rear with kitchen, living stacks front-to-rear with bath -- no extra depth-band needed versus the gr sibling.

Definition

1s/1br/wide/1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_ld.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

Sibling of 1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_gr, created 2026-07-20 -- second attempt at a wide LDK variant. The first attempt (living/dining STACKED as two full- width bands in front of kitchen+bath) needed a genuinely bigger lot (true min ~12.5x10, 51 m2) than even the round-number max (12x9). This version avoids adding a third depth-band entirely: living and dining sit SIDE-BY- SIDE in the same front band the gr sibling already uses, each stacking front-to-rear with its own rear-band neighbor (living->bath, dining- >kitchen) via match_widths, giving a clean 2x2 grid on the public side.

Layout intent (front at bottom): Rear: [BEDROOM (cont.) | BATH | KITCHEN] Front: [BEDROOM (cont.) | LIVING | DINING ] [porch/entry]

dining stacks directly with kitchen (satisfies the default kitchen+dining- must-stack rule) but living sits BESIDE dining, not in front of both dining and kitchen -- requires ldk_horizontal: true. Without it, the solver's default LDK rule (solver.py ~line 699) hard-requires living.y_end <= dining.y (living strictly in front of dining) whenever living_room + dining_room + kitchen all exist, which directly contradicts a side-by-side living/dining seam -- infeasible at ANY lot size, confirmed by probing an absurdly oversized 25x20 m lot and still getting INFEASIBLE. Diagnosed by reading the rule in solver.py rather than guessing from symptoms.

FEASIBILITY (2026-07-20): infeasible at min (10x8) -- min stays the gr sibling's job by design, this topology is med/max only. Feasible from just above 10x8 (10.25x8.2 already solves) through 11x8 (med) and 12x9 (max). living gets mechanical_vent: true -- at 12x9 specifically it came back with 0.00 m2 window area (living's only exterior wall is the front, and the entry door apparently ate the whole segment); same accepted opt-out pattern kitchen already uses everywhere in this catalog.

No lot_adjustment_profiles yet -- untuned, pending real feasibility testing.

Key adjacencies

  • bedroom ↔ living — bedroom's only door -- opens east into living across the column seam, same role great played in the gr sibling.bedroom_to_public
  • bedroom ↔ bath — bath sits directly beside the bedroom in the rear band -- shared wall only, no door. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • living ↔ dining — living and dining sit side-by-side in the front band -- open plan, no wall.open_plan
  • living ↔ bath — living (front-left-of-right-side) stacks front-to-rear with bath (rear-left-of-right-side) -- shared wall only, no door. Bath's access stays via kitchen.wall_only
  • dining ↔ kitchen — dining stacks front-to-rear with kitchen -- open pass-through.open_plan
  • bath ↔ kitchen — bath's only door -- opens into kitchen on the shared plumbing wall. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bath_door

Notable overrides

  • match_widths — solver forces matching widths within each group: living, bath; dining, kitchen.
  • private_area_floor: false — the hard private-area-must-exceed-public rule is disabled (a single bedroom can't outweigh a full LDK).
  • ldk_horizontal: true — disables the solver's default vertical LDK-stacking rules so great_room/kitchen can sit side-by-side.

Test brief

test/1s_1br_11x8_wd_side_split_bath_ld_ncp.json

1BR / 1 bath wide LDK smoke test, no carport. 11x8 lot gives a 7x4 envelope = 28 m2 floor -- the LDK sibling of 1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_gr, which stays compact-only (min is always the gr topology's job; this ldk sibling is infeasible at the gr topology's 10x8 canonical). Full-depth bedroom column; living + dining side-by-side in the front band, bath + kitchen side-by-side in the rear band -- living stacks with bath, dining stacks with kitchen. Needed ldk_horizontal: true and mechanical_vent: true on living (window-area warning at some sizes).

Lot 11×8 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 7×4 m buildable envelope — 28 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot11 × 8 m (88 m²)
Buildable envelope7 × 4 m (28 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested1
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

single common bath · bath door opens into kitchen · living and dining side-by-side, hosting the entry · no carport

1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_gr

Verified
1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_gr · single-storey · Wide · 1 bed · 1 bath

Wide-shell 1BR / 1 bath, SPLIT WING — wet rooms clustered in the west column (bath front, kitchen rear on one plumbing wall), full-depth great room in the middle hosting the entry, full-depth bedroom in the quiet east column. Kitchen and great room sit side-by-side with a dining counter on the great-room side. Based on the wide-fp-01 reference plan (12.5 x 4.35 m unit), restructured for a 6 x 4 m envelope.

Definition

1s/1br/wide/1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

Second 1BR entry in the wide catalog, originally filling the small-lot gap left by 1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr (which didn't solve below ~13.3 x 9.5 m lots). That topology was removed (2026-07-20) -- a hall doesn't make sense for a single bedroom, and its true feasibility floor sat inside the 2BR/1bath floor band anyway. This entry now stands as this catalog's own compact wide 1BR case, alongside the sibling 1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_gr. Modeled on the wide-fp-01 reference plan (a 12.5 x 4.35 m German modular unit): wet rooms clustered at one end, bedroom at the opposite quiet end, shared living in the middle with the entry. The reference's 2.87 aspect can't scale to a wide-shell lot directly, so the left end is restructured: the reference's full-depth bath + adjacent kitchen run become a stacked wet COLUMN (bath front, kitchen rear) on one plumbing wall.

Layout intent (front at bottom, canonical 6 x 4 m envelope): Rear: [KITCHEN | GREAT (cont.) | BEDROOM (cont.)] Front: [BATH | GREAT ROOM | BEDROOM ] [porch/entry]

Canonical fit: 10 x 8 m lot with standard 2 m setbacks -> 6 x 4 m envelope (ratio 1.5, 'wide' shell, 24 sqm floor). Completes the matched ~24-25 sqm tiny-house set across all three shells (squarish 5x5, deep 4x6, wide 6x4).

Global-rule overrides (all topology-scoped): - ldk_horizontal: true — kitchen sits BESIDE the great room (vertical seam); the default kitchen/great must-stack rule and the kitchen- right-half symmetry break (kitchen is west here) both contradict that. - private_area_floor: false — single bedroom (~9 sqm) can never outweigh great + kitchen (~12 sqm); same rationale as the 1BR siblings. - aspect_overrides great 2.1 / bedroom 2.0 — at 6 m width the middle and east columns run ~2.2 m wide x 4.0 deep (~1.8-1.9:1), right at the strict 1.8 catalog cap; the overrides give the solver working slack instead of a knife-edge width juggle. - kitchen_rear_pin stays DEFAULT (true): kitchen touches the rear wall, so the dirty-kitchen and kitchen-back-door conventions keep working — unlike the narrow front-back-split sibling.

Dining is at the counter (counter_divider, counter_side great — band and stools inside the great room, kitchen keeps its full aisle): the reference plan's 4-seat table needs a ~13-16 sqm living zone, which a 24 sqm program cannot afford; counter dining is the honest compact substitute.

Bath door opens into the GREAT room, not the kitchen — this is the only 1BR topology with no bath-into-kitchen sanitary soft warning.

No fallback declared — the other 1BR topologies target different shell categories, so a shell-mismatch fallback would be misleading.

Key adjacencies

  • bath ↔ kitchen — bath (front-west) and kitchen (rear-west) stack in the west column on one plumbing wall — shared wall only, no door. Mirrors the reference plan's wet-rooms-at-one-end clustering.wet_core
  • bath ↔ great — bath's only door — opens east into the great room near the entry. Guest-accessible without crossing the bedroom OR the kitchen (no bath-into-kitchen sanitary trade-off in this topology, unlike the squarish/narrow 1BR siblings).bath_door
  • kitchen ↔ great — kitchen and great room sit SIDE-BY-SIDE (vertical seam) — open plan with a dining counter along the shared edge, band + 2 stools inside the great room (counter_side) so the kitchen keeps its full aisle. min_shared_wall_m 2.1 = 2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass; also forces kitchen depth >= 2.1 m. Requires ldk_horizontal.open_plan
  • bedroom ↔ great — bedroom's only door — opens west into the great room. Full-depth seam between the middle and east columns. door_placement: high_corner (2026-07-20) hinges it at the REAR end of the seam (near the kitchen/counter), away from the front entry door -- previously defaulted to the front (low) end, clustering both doors' swings right next to the entry.bedroom_to_public

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_wd_split_wing_bath_gr).
  • match_widths — solver forces matching widths within each group: bath, kitchen.
  • private_area_floor: false — the hard private-area-must-exceed-public rule is disabled (a single bedroom can't outweigh a full LDK).
  • aspect_overrides — per-room aspect-ratio (w:h) floor for great (2.1:1), bedroom (2.0:1).
  • ldk_horizontal: true — disables the solver's default vertical LDK-stacking rules so great_room/kitchen can sit side-by-side.

      "kind": "bedroom_to_public",
      "door_placement": "high_corner",
      "note": "bedroom's only door \u2014 opens west into the great room. Full-depth seam between the middle and east columns. door_placement: high_corner (2026-07-20) hinges it at the REAR end of the seam (near the kitchen/counter), away from the front entry door -- previously defaulted to the front (low) end, clustering both doors' swings right next to the entry."
    }
  ],
  "soft_proximities": [],
  "entry_point": "great",
  "zone_split": {
    "axis": "vertical",
    "private_side": "right",
    "private_rooms": [
      "bedroom"
    ],
    "public_rooms": [
      "great",
      "kitchen",
      "bath"
    ]
  },
  "private_area_floor": false,
  "ldk_horizontal": true,
  "aspect_overrides": {
    "great": 2.1,
    "bedroom": 2.0
  },
  "match_bedroom_widths": false,
  "match_widths": [
    [
      "bath",
      "kitchen"
    ]
  ],
  "left_anchored": [
    "bath",
    "kitchen"
  ],
  "right_anchored": [
    "bedroom"
  ],
  "rear_anchored": [
    "kitchen"
  ],
  "front_to_rear_stacks": [
    [
      "bath",
      "kitchen"
    ]
  ],
  "lot_adjustment_profiles": [
    {
      "name": "compact_wd_split_wing_bath_gr",
      "when": {
        "buildable_area_lt_sqm": 40
      },
      "auto_apply": {
        "common_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 2.6,
          "max_area_sqm": 4.5
        },
        "bedroom_standard": {
          "max_area_sqm": 9.5
        }
      },
      "rationale": "Ultra-compact wide unit (canonical 6x4 m envelope, 24 sqm). Bath floors at 2.6 sqm (client soft minimum) and caps at 4.5 so snap_gaps can't balloon it; bedroom caps at 9.5 so freed area flows to the great room. Kitchen gets no min_least floor \u2014 the west column runs ~1.5-1.6 m wide, at the catalog's 1.5 m hard minimum."
    }
  ],
  "setback_elements": [
    {
      "type": "dirty_kitchen",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "behind": "kitchen"
    },
    {
      "type": "service_area",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false
    }
  ],
  "building_voids": [],
  "notes": [
    "Second 1BR entry in the wide catalog, originally filling the small-lot gap",
    "left by 1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr (which didn't solve below ~13.3 x",
    "9.5 m lots). That topology was removed (2026-07-20) -- a hall doesn't make",
    "sense for a single bedroom, and its true feasibility floor sat inside the",
    "2BR/1bath floor band anyway. This entry now stands as this catalog's own",
    "compact wide 1BR case, alongside the sibling 1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_gr.",
    "Modeled on the wide-fp-01 reference plan (a 12.5 x 4.35 m German modular unit):",
    "wet rooms clustered at one end, bedroom at the opposite quiet end, shared",
    "living in the middle with the entry. The reference's 2.87 aspect can't scale",
    "to a wide-shell lot directly, so the left end is restructured: the reference's",
    "full-depth bath + adjacent kitchen run become a stacked wet COLUMN (bath",
    "front, kitchen rear) on one plumbing wall.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (front at bottom, canonical 6 x 4 m envelope):",
    "  Rear:  [KITCHEN | GREAT (cont.) | BEDROOM (cont.)]",
    "  Front: [BATH    | GREAT ROOM    | BEDROOM        ]",
    "                     [porch/entry]",
    "",
    "Canonical fit: 10 x 8 m lot with standard 2 m setbacks -> 6 x 4 m envelope",
    "(ratio 1.5, 'wide' shell, 24 sqm floor). Completes the matched ~24-25 sqm",
    "tiny-house set across all three shells (squarish 5x5, deep 4x6, wide 6x4).",
    "",
    "Global-rule overrides (all topology-scoped):",
    "  - ldk_horizontal: true \u2014 kitchen sits BESIDE the great room (vertical",
    "    seam); the default kitchen/great must-stack rule and the kitchen-",
    "    right-half symmetry break (kitchen is west here) both contradict that.",
    "  - private_area_floor: false \u2014 single bedroom (~9 sqm) can never outweigh",
    "    great + kitchen (~12 sqm); same rationale as the 1BR siblings.",
    "  - aspect_overrides great 2.1 / bedroom 2.0 \u2014 at 6 m width the middle and",
    "    east columns run ~2.2 m wide x 4.0 deep (~1.8-1.9:1), right at the",
    "    strict 1.8 catalog cap; the overrides give the solver working slack",
    "    instead of a knife-edge width juggle.",
    "  - kitchen_rear_pin stays DEFAULT (true): kitchen touches the rear wall,",
    "    so the dirty-kitchen and kitchen-back-door conventions keep working \u2014",
    "    unlike the narrow front-back-split sibling.",
    "",
    "Dining is at the counter (counter_divider, counter_side great \u2014 band and",
    "stools inside the great room, kitchen keeps its full aisle): the reference",
    "plan's 4-seat table needs a ~13-16 sqm living zone, which a 24 sqm program",
    "cannot afford; counter dining is the honest compact substitute.",
    "",
    "Bath door opens into the GREAT room, not the kitchen \u2014 this is the only",
    "1BR topology with no bath-into-kitchen sanitary soft warning.",
    "",
    "No fallback declared \u2014 the other 1BR topologies target different shell",
    "categories, so a shell-mismatch fallback would be misleading."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/1s_1br_10x8_wd_split_wing_bath_gr_ncp.json

1BR / 1 bath wide split-wing smoke test, no carport. 10x8 lot with standard 2 m setbacks gives a 6x4 m envelope (24 m2, ratio 1.5) — the wide-fp-01 reference plan restructured: wet column west (bath front, kitchen rear), full-depth great room center with the entry and counter dining, full-depth bedroom east.

Lot 10×8 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 6×4 m buildable envelope — 24 m² floor, requesting no carport.

No. 09 / 46

1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_ld

Verified
1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_ld · single-storey · Wide · 1 bed · 1 bath

Wide-shell 1BR / 1 bath, LD style -- sibling of 1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_gr: same 3-column layout (west wet column, middle public column, east bedroom column), but the middle column's great_room splits into living (front, hosts entry) + dining (rear) stacked, mirroring the west column's own bath (front) + kitchen (rear) stack. Dining ends up side-by-side with kitchen (both rear row), living side-by-side with bath (both front row).

Definition

1s/1br/wide/1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_ld.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

Sibling of 1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_gr, created 2026-07-20 to answer whether an LDK split works for med/max wide lots if dining stacks front- to-rear behind living (as opposed to the side_split_bath_ld sibling's side-by-side living/dining, which needed ldk_horizontal to bypass a hardcoded solver conflict). Here living/dining occupy the same front/rear row split the west wet column already uses, so kitchen ends up beside dining (both rear row) and bath beside living (both front row) -- ldk_horizontal is kept regardless, since the gr sibling already needs it for the kitchen-on-the-west symmetry-break override, which incidentally also skips the living/dining/kitchen default-stacking rule entirely.

Layout intent (front at bottom): Rear: [KITCHEN | DINING | BEDROOM (cont.)] Front: [BATH | LIVING | BEDROOM ] [porch/entry]

FEASIBILITY (2026-07-20): infeasible at min (10x8) -- min stays the gr sibling's job by design, this topology is med/max only, same split as its side_split_bath_ld cousin. Feasible at both 11x8 (med) and 12x9 (max). living needed mechanical_vent: true -- at 11x8 it came back with 0.00 m2 window area (living's only exterior wall is the front, entry door apparently consumed the whole segment); same accepted opt-out pattern kitchen already uses everywhere in this catalog, and the same fix side_split_bath_ld needed (there it hit at max instead of med -- which specific size trips the window check isn't consistent, so the opt-out is applied unconditionally rather than size-gated).

No lot_adjustment_profiles yet -- untuned, both sizes solve clean without.

Key adjacencies

  • bath ↔ kitchen — bath (front-west) and kitchen (rear-west) stack in the west column on one plumbing wall -- shared wall only, no door. Unchanged from the gr sibling.wet_core
  • bath ↔ living — bath's only door -- opens east into living near the entry, same role great played in the gr sibling. No bath-into-kitchen sanitary trade-off, same as the gr sibling.bath_door
  • living ↔ dining — living and dining stack front-to-rear in the middle column -- open mouth, no wall (ld style). Mirrors the west column's own bath/kitchen stack.open_plan
  • dining ↔ kitchen — dining and kitchen sit side-by-side (both in the rear row, middle vs west column) -- open pass-through, no wall.open_plan
  • bedroom ↔ living — bedroom's only door -- opens west into living. door_placement: high_corner (2026-07-20, matching the gr sibling's fix) hinges it at the REAR end of the living/bedroom seam (toward dining), away from the front entry door -- previously defaulted to the front (low) end, clustering both doors' swings right next to the entry.bedroom_to_public
  • bedroom ↔ dining — bedroom is full-depth, so its west wall also borders dining (rear) -- shared wall only, no door.wall_only

Notable overrides

  • match_widths — solver forces matching widths within each group: bath, kitchen; living, dining.
  • private_area_floor: false — the hard private-area-must-exceed-public rule is disabled (a single bedroom can't outweigh a full LDK).
  • aspect_overrides — per-room aspect-ratio (w:h) floor for bedroom (2.0:1).
  • ldk_horizontal: true — disables the solver's default vertical LDK-stacking rules so great_room/kitchen can sit side-by-side.

Test brief

test/1s_1br_11x8_wd_split_wing_bath_ld_ncp.json

1BR / 1 bath wide LDK smoke test, no carport. 11x8 lot gives a 7x4 envelope = 28 m2 floor -- the LDK sibling of 1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_gr, which stays compact-only (min is always the gr topology's job; this ldk sibling is infeasible at the gr topology's 10x8 canonical). West wet column (bath front/kitchen rear) unchanged from the gr sibling; middle column now stacks living (front, hosts entry) + dining (rear) instead of one great_room, mirroring the west column's own front/rear split -- dining ends up beside kitchen, living beside bath. Bedroom's door is hinged at the rear end (door_placement: high_corner), matching the gr sibling's fix.

Lot 11×8 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 7×4 m buildable envelope — 28 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot11 × 8 m (88 m²)
Buildable envelope7 × 4 m (28 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested1
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

single common bath · bath door opens into living, not kitchen · living and dining stacked front-to-rear, hosting the entry · no carport

No. 10 / 46

1s_2br_sq_side_split_bath_ld

Verified
1s_2br_sq_side_split_bath_ld · single-storey · Squarish · 2 bed · 1 bath

Squarish 2BR / 1 bath, LD style -- standard front-left, master rear-left, one common bath at the rear next to kitchen, public column splits into living (front) + dining (mid) + kitchen (rear), all stacked front-to-rear -- the solver's default LDK arrangement, no ldk_horizontal needed. Deliberately public-heavy zone ratio: 45% private (bedrooms) / 55% public (LDK + bath).

Definition

1s/2br/squarish/1s_2br_sq_side_split_bath_ld.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

Created 2026-07-20 as an LDK split (living/dining/kitchen stacked front-to-rear, the solver's default LDK arrangement) of 1s_2br_sq_side_split_bath_gr -- gr sibling deleted the same day, this topology replaces it. Master repositioned to the rear the same day (standard now front-left, master rear-left) -- adjacencies keep each bedroom's original wall-width value (standard's wider 1.2 m clearance floor, master's 0.8 m) but reassigned to whichever public room now occupies that geometric role, same mechanism as the cl_gr->cl_ld swap. Single common bath stays rear-anchored next to kitchen (no separate bath band -- this is the 1-bath program, unlike the 2-bath cl_gr/cl_ld pair which has a full-depth ensuite+common middle band); common was never wired to a bedroom directly, so its own adjacencies (dining + kitchen wet-core) are untouched by the reposition.

Layout intent (canonical form -- brief.carport_side='right'): Front: [standard | living ] Rear: [master | common | dining->kitchen ]

standard's door opens into living (front, mirrors its old ->great role). master's door opens into dining (rear, mirrors its old ->great role). common's public door goes to dining, matching its proximity to master (now the rear bedroom). The kitchen<->common wet-core alternate host is unchanged.

Published/canonical minimum: 10x10 (chosen deliberately per an earlier session request, kept as-is through the master reposition). The reposition actually tightened the solver's true floor further, to ~9.65x9.65 (9.6x9.6 infeasible, 9.65x9.65 solves -- confirmed by bisection); 10x10 is no longer the bare minimum but is kept as the published one since nothing asked for it to be re-tuned down again. vs the gr sibling's 10.5x10.5. Unlike the cl_gr->cl_ld swap, this is NOT a clean 0-warning win -- both window_area_habitable (kitchen, small sizes only) and bath_door_into_kitchen (the door-host auto-scorer picks kitchen over the group's dining default at every size) persist here exactly as they did in the gr sibling at comparable sizes. The win is purely the smaller minimum lot, not warning count. Re-verified after the master reposition at the canonical sizes -- 10x10 and 10.5x10.5 unchanged (2 warn), 12x12 unchanged (1 warn); 11x11 briefly picked up a second window_area_habitable warning after the reposition alone, but the follow-up 45/55 zone-ratio change below pulled it back to 1 warn at 11x11 -- see that paragraph.

45/55 zone ratio (same day, second follow-up): user asked for the private column (master+standard) at 45% of the private+public total and the public side (living+dining+kitchen+common bath) at 55% -- opposite of the solver's catalog-wide default (55/45 favoring private, with a hard floor of private>=public that would have made 45% private outright infeasible). This needed a real solver.py change, not just a topology flag: generalized the previously-hardcoded 50% hard-floor / 55% soft-target into two new configurable Topology fields, zone_ratio_private_floor_pct and zone_ratio_private_target_pct (defaults 50.0/55.0 reproduce every other topology's behavior byte-for-byte -- verified via full regression before touching this topology's own values). Set here to floor=40.0 / target=45.0. Also added a zone_balance_rooms override so common counts toward the public side (it's zone:'service', normally neutral/excluded from the ratio entirely) -- the user's request explicitly included the bath on the 55% side. Verified achieved ratio lands 44-46.5% private / 53.5-56% public across 9.7x9.7 through 13x13 -- e.g. exactly 45.0%/ 55.0% at 10x10 and 12x12. Side effects: true floor tightened slightly further to ~9.6x9.6 (10x10 stays published minimum); 11x11 dropped back to 1 warning (window_area_habitable resolved itself under the new ratio, see paragraph above); 10x10's baseline SVG refreshed (warn/sugg counts unchanged: 2 warn, 6 sugg, but room proportions shifted).

Practical ceiling matches the locked 2BR/1bath floor-area knee (~13x13 / 81 m2) -- solves through 13x13, goes non-monotonic above that (same fixed max_area_sqm room-cap behavior documented for the gr sibling in the squarish 2BR lot-size sweep, not a regression introduced here).

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ standard — master and standard stack vertically along the left wall -- standard at the front (street side), master at the rear. Shared wall only, no door between them. Repositioned 2026-07-20 (master moved to the rear).bedroom_to_bedroom_wall
  • standard ↔ living — standard's door -- opens into living, its front-most public neighbor. Keeps the wider 1.2 m floor for door-swing clearance (same value standard has always used, now paired with living instead of dining after the 2026-07-20 reposition).bedroom_to_public
  • master ↔ dining — master's door -- opens into dining, its rear-most public neighbor. Keeps master's original 0.8 m floor, now paired with dining instead of living after the 2026-07-20 reposition (master moved to the rear).bedroom_to_public
  • common ↔ dining — common's public door -- opens into dining (its nearest public neighbor at the rear), matching its proximity to master (rear bedroom as of the 2026-07-20 reposition). DEFAULT host of the 'common_access' door-host group; a brief door_host: {"common": "kitchen"} moves the door to the kitchen wall instead, leaving this wall solid. Takes over the role common<->great played in the gr sibling.bath_door
  • common ↔ kitchen — shared plumbing wall -- kitchen and common bath stack rear-right; bath west of kitchen. ALTERNATE host of the 'common_access' group: door placed here when shared wall >= 1.40 m (clear 0.70 + frame 0.20 + guard 0.50); otherwise falls back to dining. Unchanged from the gr sibling.wet_core
  • living ↔ dining — living and dining stack front-to-rear -- open mouth, no wall (ld style).open_plan
  • dining ↔ kitchen — dining and kitchen stack front-to-rear -- open pass-through, matches the solver's default kitchen+dining-must-stack rule directly.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_sq_side_split_bath_ld).
  • door_host_group — at least one door's host wall is chosen by the solver's auto-scorer (or overridden per-brief via door_host), not fixed in the topology.
  • zone_balance_rooms — custom room grouping overrides the default public/private area split (private: master, standard; public: living, dining, kitchen, common).

    "Created 2026-07-20 as an LDK split (living/dining/kitchen stacked",

    "",

    "session request, kept as-is through the master reposition). The",

    "~9.65x9.65 (9.6x9.6 infeasible, 9.65x9.65 solves -- confirmed by",
    "bisection); 10x10 is no longer the bare minimum but is kept as the",
    "published one since nothing asked for it to be re-tuned down again.",
    "vs the gr sibling's 10.5x10.5. Unlike the cl_gr->cl_ld swap, this is",
    "NOT a clean 0-warning win -- both window_area_habitable (kitchen, small",
    "sizes only) and bath_door_into_kitchen (the door-host auto-scorer picks",

    "purely the smaller minimum lot, not warning count. Re-verified after",
