    "unchanged (2 warn), 12x12 unchanged (1 warn); 11x11 briefly picked up a",
    "second window_area_habitable warning after the reposition alone, but",
    "the follow-up 45/55 zone-ratio change below pulled it back to 1 warn",
    "at 11x11 -- see that paragraph.",
    "",
    "45/55 zone ratio (same day, second follow-up): user asked for the",
    "private column (master+standard) at 45% of the private+public total",
    "and the public side (living+dining+kitchen+common bath) at 55% --",
    "opposite of the solver's catalog-wide default (55/45 favoring",
    "private, with a hard floor of private>=public that would have made",
    "45% private outright infeasible). This needed a real solver.py change,",
    "not just a topology flag: generalized the previously-hardcoded 50%",
    "hard-floor / 55% soft-target into two new configurable Topology",
    "fields, zone_ratio_private_floor_pct and zone_ratio_private_target_pct",
    "(defaults 50.0/55.0 reproduce every other topology's behavior",
    "byte-for-byte -- verified via full regression before touching this",
    "topology's own values). Set here to floor=40.0 / target=45.0. Also",
    "added a zone_balance_rooms override so common counts toward the",
    "public side (it's zone:'service', normally neutral/excluded from the",
    "ratio entirely) -- the user's request explicitly included the bath",
    "on the 55% side. Verified achieved ratio lands 44-46.5% private /",
    "53.5-56% public across 9.7x9.7 through 13x13 -- e.g. exactly 45.0%/",
    "55.0% at 10x10 and 12x12. Side effects: true floor tightened slightly",
    "further to ~9.6x9.6 (10x10 stays published minimum); 11x11 dropped",
    "back to 1 warning (window_area_habitable resolved itself under the",
    "new ratio, see paragraph above); 10x10's baseline SVG refreshed",
    "(warn/sugg counts unchanged: 2 warn, 6 sugg, but room proportions",
    "shifted).",
    "",
    "Practical ceiling matches the locked 2BR/1bath floor-area knee (~13x13",
    "/ 81 m2) -- solves through 13x13, goes non-monotonic above that (same",
    "fixed max_area_sqm room-cap behavior documented for the gr sibling in",
    "the squarish 2BR lot-size sweep, not a regression introduced here)."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/1s_2br_10x10_sq_side_split_bath_ld_ncp.json

2BR / 1 bath LDK smoke test, no carport. 10x10 lot gives a 6x6 envelope = 36 m2 floor -- the empirical minimum feasible size for this topology (9.9x9.9 is infeasible). Single common bath sits at the rear next to kitchen (no ensuite, no separate bath band). Public side is a real living/dining/kitchen split (not open-plan great_room) -- living, dining, and kitchen stack front-to-rear in one column, the solver's default LDK arrangement.

Lot 10×10 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 6×6 m buildable envelope — 36 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot10 × 10 m (100 m²)
Buildable envelope6 × 6 m (36 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

one common bath · common bath publicly accessible from dining · separate living, dining, and kitchen rooms stacked front-to-rear · no carport

No. 11 / 46

1s_2br_sq_side_split_bath_pwd_gr

Verified
1s_2br_sq_side_split_bath_pwd_gr · single-storey · Squarish · 2 bed · 2 bath

Squarish 2BR / 1 common bath + powder room — master front-left, standard rear-left, rear band right side has common bath then powder room then kitchen, open-plan great room takes the front-right. Powder room doors into the great room.

Definition

1s/2br/squarish/1s_2br_sq_side_split_bath_pwd_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

Squarish 2BR topology with ONE common bath + ONE powder room (half-bath). bath_token: 'bath_pwd' — extends the locked naming convention for single common bath + powder room.

Layout intent (canonical form — brief.carport_side='right'): Front: [master | great_room ] Rear: [standard | common | powder_room | kitchen ]

The rear band has FOUR rooms west-to-east: standard (left) | common (centre-left) | powder_room (centre-right) | kitchen (right)

Plumbing train: common — powder_room — kitchen all share rear wet walls, collapsing three service points onto one rear riser.

Doors from the great room's north (rear) wall: - Common bath door (south face of common overlaps great's north face) - Powder room door (south face of powder_room overlaps great's north face) Both doors open directly into the great room — guest-accessible without passing through the private zone.

Recommended lot: 14×12 or similar (buildable ≥ 10×8 = 80 sqm). The four-room rear band + private ≥ public zone floor means lots wider than ~11 m buildable become infeasible (public zone dominates). Tested at 14×12 (buildable 10×8).

Carport and dirty_kitchen behave identically to sq_side_split_bath_gr.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ standard — master (front) and standard (rear) stack vertically along the left wall. Shared wall only, no door.bedroom_to_bedroom_wall
  • master ↔ great — master's east wall faces great room — hosts master's bedroom door.bedroom_to_public
  • standard ↔ great — standard's east wall overlaps great room's west wall — hosts standard's door. 1.2 m floor gives 0.8 m clear door + 0.15 m frame each side.bedroom_to_public
  • standard ↔ common — common is just east of standard at the rear; shared wall only, no door.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • common ↔ great — common's south wall faces great room — door into great for shared access by both bedrooms.bath_door
  • common ↔ powder_room — common and powder_room share a plumbing wall — powder_room is east of common in the rear band. Stacks both wet rooms for a single rear service riser.wet_core
  • powder_room ↔ great — powder_room's south wall faces great room — door into great for guest access. Powder room sits in the rear band east of common, so its south face touches the great room's north face.bath_door
  • powder_room ↔ kitchen — powder_room and kitchen share a plumbing wall — kitchen is east of powder_room, right-anchored. Extends the rear wet-stack: common — powder_room — kitchen.wet_core
  • kitchen ↔ great — great_room + kitchen form the open LDK (gr style).open_plan

      "kind": "wet_core",
      "note": "powder_room and kitchen share a plumbing wall \u2014 kitchen is east of powder_room, right-anchored. Extends the rear wet-stack: common \u2014 powder_room \u2014 kitchen."
    },
    {
      "a": "kitchen",
      "b": "great",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 1.5,
      "kind": "open_plan",
      "note": "great_room + kitchen form the open LDK (gr style)."
    }

  "notes": [
    "Squarish 2BR topology with ONE common bath + ONE powder room (half-bath).",
    "bath_token: 'bath_pwd' \u2014 extends the locked naming convention for single common bath + powder room.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (canonical form \u2014 brief.carport_side='right'):",
    "  Front: [master         | great_room                          ]",
    "  Rear:  [standard | common | powder_room | kitchen            ]",
    "",
    "The rear band has FOUR rooms west-to-east:",
    "  standard (left) | common (centre-left) | powder_room (centre-right) | kitchen (right)",
    "",
    "Plumbing train: common \u2014 powder_room \u2014 kitchen all share rear wet walls,",
    "collapsing three service points onto one rear riser.",
    "",
    "Doors from the great room's north (rear) wall:",
    "  - Common bath door (south face of common overlaps great's north face)",
    "  - Powder room door (south face of powder_room overlaps great's north face)",
    "Both doors open directly into the great room \u2014 guest-accessible without",
    "passing through the private zone.",
    "",
    "Recommended lot: 14\u00d712 or similar (buildable \u2265 10\u00d78 = 80 sqm). The four-room",
    "rear band + private \u2265 public zone floor means lots wider than ~11 m buildable",
    "become infeasible (public zone dominates). Tested at 14\u00d712 (buildable 10\u00d78).",
    "",
    "Carport and dirty_kitchen behave identically to sq_side_split_bath_gr."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/1s_2br_16x12_sq_side_split_bath_pwd_gr_ncp.json

2BR / 1 common bath + powder room — 14x12 lot gives a 10x8 buildable = 80 sqm. Powder room (half-bath) is guest-accessible from the great room alongside the common bath. Rear band: standard | common | powder_room | kitchen. Open-plan great room + kitchen (gr style). No carport — clean rectangle.

Lot 14×12 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 10×8 m buildable envelope — 80 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot14 × 12 m (168 m²)
Buildable envelope10 × 8 m (80 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

single common bath shared between bedrooms · powder room (half-bath) accessible from great room · open-plan great room + kitchen · no carport

No. 12 / 46

1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_ld

Verified
1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_ld · single-storey · Squarish · 2 bed · 2 bath

Squarish 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED with mid-band HALL, LD style — bedrooms and the common T&B all open onto a hallway tucked into the middle band between master (front) and standard (rear). Hall has an open mouth east into dining. Public side is a real living/dining/kitchen split stacked front-to-rear, not a combined great room.

Definition

1s/2br/squarish/1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_ld.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

Created 2026-07-20 as an LDK split (living/dining/kitchen stacked front-to-rear, the solver's default LDK arrangement) of 1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr -- gr sibling deleted the same day, this topology replaces it. Private column + hall structure entirely unchanged: master front / standard rear, ensuite+common wet core and a hallway both tucked into the middle band, bedroom and bath doors all open onto the hall (never directly onto the public side).

Layout intent (canonical, brief.carport_side='right'): Front: [master | living ] Mid: [ensuite | common | HALL | dining ] Rear: [standard | kitchen ]

Only ONE adjacency changed role: hall's open-mouth connection moves from great_room to dining (not living) -- the hall sits in the MIDDLE band between master and standard, so its wall has to align with whichever public room occupies that same middle row. Verified this empirically: hall->living tested directly and is infeasible even at 11x11 (wrong row), hall->dining solves cleanly. living gets hosts_entry instead of great (front-most public room, same role).

Published minimum: 10x10 (true floor ~9.9x9.9, confirmed by bisection). This is WORSE than the gr sibling's 9.5x9.5 floor -- unlike cl_gr->cl_ld (smaller floor) and bath_gr->bath_ld (same floor after re-tuning), the LDK split here costs about 0.4 m of minimum lot size. Tried loosening the compact-shell profile's auto_apply floors (kitchen 1.8->1.6, hallway 0.95->0.9) to chase parity with gr; true floor stayed at 9.9x9.9 regardless, so the gap is a genuine structural cost of dining's width needing to satisfy both the hall's mouth AND its own living/kitchen stacking, not a tunable threshold. Kept the profile at its original (gr) values since loosening didn't help.

At 10x10 and 11x11/12x12 (the published sweep sizes), warning counts are IDENTICAL to the gr sibling at the same sizes (1 warn tiered_preferred_dropped at 10x10, 0 warn at 11x11 and up) -- suggestion counts run slightly higher (more distinct rooms to flag), cosmetic only.

fallback_topology unchanged from the gr sibling -- still points to 1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_ld.json (confirmed dormant, never triggered by any existing brief, same as before).

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is master's exclusive — interior door from master, west side of the bath block. Unchanged from the gr sibling.ensuite_door
  • ensuite ↔ common — shared plumbing wall — wet stacks in one bay. Unchanged from the gr sibling.wet_core
  • common ↔ standard — common's north face touches standard — bath block is the acoustic+privacy buffer between bedrooms. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • master ↔ hall — master door opens north into the hall — 1.0 m floor = 0.8 m clear + 2x 0.10 m frame stops. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bedroom_door
  • common ↔ hall — common bath door opens east into the hall — 0.8 m bath leaf + frame. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bath_door
  • standard ↔ hall — standard door opens south into the hall — 1.0 m = 0.8 m clear + frame. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bedroom_door
  • hall ↔ dining — hall's east wall is an open mouth into dining (no door — codebase rule: hallway<->public is always open). dining, not living, because the hall sits in the MIDDLE band between master and standard -- its wall has to align with whichever public room occupies that same middle row. Tested hall->living directly: infeasible even at 11x11 (wrong row). Takes over the role hall<->great played in the gr sibling.open_plan
  • living ↔ dining — living and dining stack front-to-rear -- open mouth, no wall (ld style).open_plan
  • dining ↔ kitchen — dining and kitchen stack front-to-rear -- open pass-through, matches the solver's default kitchen+dining-must-stack rule directly.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_ld).
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • match_bath_widths — solver forces the bath rooms' widths to match.
  • bedroom_band_fills_width — the bedroom band is forced to fill the full envelope width.
  • fallback_topology — if the solver reports infeasibility on the brief's lot, retries with 1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_ld.

Test brief

test/1s_2br_10x10_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_ld_ncp.json

2BR / 2 baths clustered, mid-band hall, LDK smoke test, no carport. 10x10 lot gives a 6x6 envelope = 36 m2 floor -- the published minimum for this topology (true floor is ~9.9x9.9, confirmed by bisection; 9.9x9.9 also solves but 10x10 is the round-number published minimum, matching the bath_ld precedent). Master ensuite + common bath share a wet-core plumbing wall; a hallway is tucked into the same middle band between master (front) and standard (rear) -- all bedroom and bath doors open onto the hall, never directly onto the public side. Public side is a real living/dining/kitchen split stacked front-to-rear -- the hall's open mouth connects to dining (the middle-row public room), not living.

Lot 10×10 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 6×6 m buildable envelope — 36 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot10 × 10 m (100 m²)
Buildable envelope6 × 6 m (36 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

master ensuite · common bath accessible from the hall · clustered baths (ensuite + common share a plumbing wall) · mid-band hallway between master and standard · separate living, dining, and kitchen rooms stacked front-to-rear · no carport

Validator result

Warningpreferred_apply adjustments did not fit '1s/2br/squarish/1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_ld.json' on this shell — relaxed to base auto_apply (e.g., baths held to hard minimum instead of preferred-low) (tiered_preferred_dropped)
SuggestionR-1 front setback 2.00 m is below the IRR Rule VIII Table VIII.2 baseline of 4.5 m (LGU zoning may permit smaller — verify locally) (setback_below_irr_baseline)
Suggestioncommon_bath 'common' has no exterior wall — must use mechanical ventilation (mark room.mechanical_vent=True) (bath_needs_mech_vent)

1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_ld

Verified
1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_ld · single-storey · Squarish · 2 bed · 2 bath

Squarish 2BR / 2 baths, LD style -- private column (standard front / ensuite+common middle band / master rear), public column splits into living (front) + dining (mid) + kitchen (rear), all stacked front-to-rear -- the solver's default LDK arrangement, no ldk_horizontal needed.

Definition

1s/2br/squarish/1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_ld.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

Created 2026-07-20 as an LDK split (living/dining/kitchen stacked front-to-rear, the solver's default LDK arrangement) of this bath- cluster program. Originally a sibling of great_room-style 1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_gr, which this topology proved strictly better than (smaller true minimum -- 10.5x10.5 vs 11x11 -- and clean 0-warning solves at every tested size), so cl_gr was deleted the same day and this became the sole topology for the program. Private column: standard front, ensuite+common middle band, master rear.

Layout intent (canonical form -- brief.carport_side='right'): Front: [standard | living ] Mid: [ensuite | common | dining ] Rear: [master | kitchen ]

master's and standard's doors go to whichever public room occupies that geometric role: standard->living (front), master->dining (dining sits where a great room's rear portion would reach into master's zone). common's public door goes to living, matching standard's proximity ('standard can use it without going through master').

No lot_adjustment_profiles -- untuned, not needed at the verified canonical sizes (true min 10.5x10.5; 0-warning solves at 11x11 and 12x12).

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is master's exclusive -- door opens from master, no other way in. Unchanged from the gr sibling.ensuite_door
  • ensuite ↔ common — shared plumbing wall between the two baths -- keeps all wet stacks in one bay. Unchanged from the gr sibling.wet_core
  • common ↔ standard — common touches standard's south face -- the bath block acts as the acoustic buffer between bedrooms. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • standard ↔ living — standard's door -- opens into living, its front-most public neighbor. Takes over the role standard<->great played in the gr sibling.bedroom_to_public
  • master ↔ dining — master's door -- opens into dining, its rear-most public neighbor (dining sits where great_room's rear portion used to reach into master's zone). Takes over the role master<->great played in the gr sibling.bedroom_to_public
  • common ↔ living — common is publicly accessible via living (used by standard and guests). Takes over the role common<->great played in the gr sibling.bath_door
  • living ↔ dining — living and dining stack front-to-rear -- open mouth, no wall (ld style).open_plan
  • dining ↔ kitchen — dining and kitchen stack front-to-rear -- open pass-through, matches the solver's default kitchen+dining-must-stack rule directly.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • match_bath_widths — solver forces the bath rooms' widths to match.
  • bedroom_band_fills_width — the bedroom band is forced to fill the full envelope width.

Test brief

test/1s_2br_10.5x10.5_sq_side_split_baths_cl_ld_ncp.json

2BR / 2 baths clustered LDK smoke test, no carport. 10.5x10.5 lot gives a 6.5x6.5 envelope = 42.25 m2 floor -- the empirical minimum feasible size for this topology (10x10 is infeasible). Master ensuite + common bath share a wet-core plumbing wall; bath block sits between standard (front) and master (rear). Public side is a real living/dining/kitchen split (not open-plan great_room) -- living, dining, and kitchen stack front-to-rear in one column, the solver's default LDK arrangement.

Lot 10.5×10.5 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 6.5×6.5 m buildable envelope — 42.25 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot10.5 × 10.5 m (110.25 m²)
Buildable envelope6.5 × 6.5 m (42.25 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

master ensuite · common bath publicly accessible from living · clustered baths (ensuite + common share a plumbing wall) · separate living, dining, and kitchen rooms stacked front-to-rear · no carport

No. 14 / 46

1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_ds_ld

Verified
1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_ds_ld · single-storey · Squarish · 2 bed · 2 bath

Squarish 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED — ensuite sandwiched between master and standard in the private column; common T&B at rear-middle, accessible from dining (public) and standard (private). LDK is separate living + dining + kitchen, stacked front-to-rear.

Definition

1s/2br/squarish/1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_ds_ld.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

v2 catalog topology #4 — 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED. The two baths are NOT clustered — they sit in different bands of the building. The ensuite is sandwiched between master (front) and standard (rear) IN the private column, so the private wing reads as [master, ensuite, standard] front- to-rear. The common T&B sits at the rear-MIDDLE, between standard (west) and kitchen (east), serving as both the bedroom-side bath (standard's interior door) and the public-side bath (door to dining for guest use).

LDK is the 'ld' style: separate living + dining + kitchen stacked front- to-rear in the public column. Living hosts the entry. The LDK rule that kitchen can sit side-by-side with dining only when living is in front of both is satisfied here trivially because we explicitly stack the three.

Layout intent (canonical, brief.carport_side='right'): Rear: [standard | T&B | kitchen ] Mid: [ensuite | dining ] Front: [master | living ]

Private/public split: master + ensuite + standard on the LEFT, living + dining + kitchen on the RIGHT. Common T&B is zone='service' — it is geometrically on the public-side x range (east of standard) but not constrained by the zone_split. Floor band: ~60-80 m² (similar to the clustered_baths_gr band; the distributed bath block adds slightly less circulation overhead than a hall variant but more than a single bath).

Carport: 3.0 m x 6.0 m at the front-right (Item 2 default). The 1 m x 4 m exceeding the side+front setbacks becomes a building_void carved from the LDK column's front-right corner — same L-shape pattern as the clustered_baths topologies. Brief.carport_side='none' strips the void; brief.carport_side='left' mirrors the topology.

ACOUSTIC NOTE: when ensuite_alcove_joins_master triggers (bedroom column wider than ~3.0 m), the L-extension's north edge butts directly against standard's south edge — a short bedroom-to-bedroom wall without the ensuite acting as an acoustic buffer there. PH practice mitigates by placing master's closet (or standard's closet) against this segment. This is a future fixture-placement concern, not a topology constraint.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is master's exclusive — door opens north from masterensuite_door
  • ensuite ↔ standard — ensuite north wall touches standard south — no door, acoustic+privacy bufferbath_to_bedroom_wall
  • common ↔ kitchen — shared plumbing wall between common bath and kitchen — wet stacks in one rear bay. ALTERNATE host of the 'common_access' group: door placed here only when shared wall >= 1.5 m; falls back to dining on compact lots.wet_core
  • common ↔ dining — common is publicly accessible — door on common south wall to dining. DEFAULT host of the 'common_access' door-host group. NOTE: standard↔common bath_door adjacency was removed (2026-06-05) to make this topology swap-friendly. Common bath is now reached only via dining (publicly), not from standard's bedroom.bath_door
  • master ↔ dining — master door east into dining — placed on the L-extension (rect2) east wall, hinged at the HIGH (north) end via the bedroom stack-bias. This clusters master's door right next to standard's door at the bedroom-wing boundary.bedroom_to_public
  • standard ↔ dining — standard door east into dining (rear of the LDK column, in front of kitchen). 1.1 m floor = 0.8 m clear panel + 2x 0.1 m frame stops + 0.1 m leeway. Half-step compromise between the bare-minimum 1.0 m (which puts the door right at the corner) and the 1.2 m used in clustered topologies — gives standard's door breathing room without making the 11x11 NCP lot infeasible.bedroom_to_public
  • living ↔ dining — living and dining flow as one open public space — no wall (ld style)open_plan
  • dining ↔ kitchen — dining flows into kitchen — open pass-through, no wall (PH practice)open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_sq_side_split_baths_ds_ld).
  • door_host_group — at least one door's host wall is chosen by the solver's auto-scorer (or overridden per-brief via door_host), not fixed in the topology.
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • ensuite_alcove_joins_master — the strip beside a narrower ensuite is folded into master as an L-shaped alcove.

      "living",
      "dining",
      "kitchen"
    ]
  },
  "match_bedroom_widths": true,
  "ensuite_alcove_joins_master": true,
  "left_anchored": [
    "master",
    "ensuite",
    "standard"
  ],
  "right_anchored": [
    "kitchen"
  ],
  "rear_anchored": [
    "standard",
    "common",
    "kitchen"
  ],
  "front_to_rear_stacks": [
    [
      "master",
      "ensuite",
      "standard"
    ],
    [
      "living",
      "dining",
      "kitchen"
    ]
  ],
  "lot_adjustment_profiles": [
    {
      "name": "compact_sq_side_split_baths_ds_ld",
      "when": {
        "buildable_area_lt_sqm": 100
      },
      "auto_apply": {
        "kitchen": {
          "min_least_dim_m": 1.5
        },
        "ensuite_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 2.5,
          "max_area_sqm": 4.5
        },
        "common_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 3.0,
          "max_area_sqm": 4.5
        },
        "master_bedroom": {
          "max_area_sqm": 18.0
        }
      },
      "preferred_apply": {
        "ensuite_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 3.0
        },
        "kitchen": {
          "min_least_dim_m": 2.0
        }
      },
      "rationale": "Bath area is the HIGHEST priority soft constraint for this topology \u2014 both baths floor at 3.0 m\u00b2 preferred-low in auto_apply (always applied) so the ensuite reads consistently at preferred across NCP and carport variants. Without this, the carport variants drop the tier and ensuite collapses to its 0.9 m corridor minimum, while NCP variants keep their preferred ensuite. To make room for the bath floors on tight carport lots, kitchen's auto-floor is at the rules.json hard min (1.5 m); the preferred 2.0 m galley sits in preferred_apply and may relax. Master is hard-capped at 18 m\u00b2 area + 4.5 m greatest dim so its priority-4 weight doesn't steal middle-band depth from ensuite."
    }
  ],
  "setback_elements": [
    {
      "type": "carport",
      "location": "side_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "width_m": 3.0,
      "depth_m": 6.0
    },
    {
      "type": "dirty_kitchen",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "behind": "kitchen"
    },
    {
      "type": "service_area",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false
    },
    {
      "type": "lanai",
      "location": "side_setback",
      "covered": false
    }
  ],
  "building_voids": [
    {
      "id": "carport_cut",
      "location": "front_right",
      "width_m": 1.0,
      "depth_m": 4.0,
      "consumed_by": "carport"
    }
  ],
  "notes": [
    "v2 catalog topology #4 \u2014 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED. The two baths are NOT",
    "clustered \u2014 they sit in different bands of the building. The ensuite is",
    "sandwiched between master (front) and standard (rear) IN the private",
    "column, so the private wing reads as [master, ensuite, standard] front-",
    "to-rear. The common T&B sits at the rear-MIDDLE, between standard (west)",
    "and kitchen (east), serving as both the bedroom-side bath (standard's",
    "interior door) and the public-side bath (door to dining for guest use).",
    "",
    "LDK is the 'ld' style: separate living + dining + kitchen stacked front-",
    "to-rear in the public column. Living hosts the entry. The LDK rule that",
    "kitchen can sit side-by-side with dining only when living is in front of",
    "both is satisfied here trivially because we explicitly stack the three.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (canonical, brief.carport_side='right'):",
    "  Rear:  [standard         | T&B | kitchen                            ]",
    "  Mid:   [ensuite           | dining                                  ]",
    "  Front: [master            | living                                  ]",
    "",
    "Private/public split: master + ensuite + standard on the LEFT, living +",
    "dining + kitchen on the RIGHT. Common T&B is zone='service' \u2014 it is",
    "geometrically on the public-side x range (east of standard) but not",
    "constrained by the zone_split. Floor band: ~60-80 m\u00b2 (similar to the",
    "clustered_baths_gr band; the distributed bath block adds slightly less",
    "circulation overhead than a hall variant but more than a single bath).",
    "",
    "Carport: 3.0 m x 6.0 m at the front-right (Item 2 default). The 1 m x",
    "4 m exceeding the side+front setbacks becomes a building_void carved",
    "from the LDK column's front-right corner \u2014 same L-shape pattern as the",
    "clustered_baths topologies. Brief.carport_side='none' strips the",
    "void; brief.carport_side='left' mirrors the topology.",
    "",
    "ACOUSTIC NOTE: when ensuite_alcove_joins_master triggers (bedroom column",
    "wider than ~3.0 m), the L-extension's north edge butts directly against",
    "standard's south edge \u2014 a short bedroom-to-bedroom wall without the",
    "ensuite acting as an acoustic buffer there. PH practice mitigates by",
    "placing master's closet (or standard's closet) against this segment.",
    "This is a future fixture-placement concern, not a topology constraint."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/1s_2br_11x11_sq_side_split_baths_ds_ld_ncp.json

2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED — SMALL, NO CARPORT. Ensuite between master and standard; common T&B at rear-middle; separate L + D + K stacked. 11 × 11 lot → envelope 7 × 7 = 49 m² floor (clean rectangle, no L-cut). At the topology's lower limit — most rooms hit their hard minimums on this shell.

Lot 11×11 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 7×7 m buildable envelope — 49 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot11 × 11 m (121 m²)
Buildable envelope7 × 7 m (49 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

master ensuite · common bath accessible from dining (publicly) and standard (privately) · distributed baths (ensuite in private column, common at rear-middle) · separate living + dining + kitchen (ld style) · no carport

No. 15 / 46

2s_2br_sq_rear_stair_bath_gr

Verified
2s_2br_sq_rear_stair_bath_gr · 2-storey · Squarish · 2 bed · 2 bath

Squarish-shell 2-STOREY 2BR / 2 baths, REAR-CORNER STAIR, hall-less ground floor — open great room across the full front (entry, dining folds in with a counter on the kitchen edge), kitchen + T&B in a rear wet bay, straight stair in the rear corner boarding off the great room. Upstairs, the squarish signature: BOTH bedrooms side-by-side at the front with street-facing windows, landing hall behind them, second T&B at the rear near the wet stack. Canonical 10x10 lot (6x6 buildable per floor, 72 m² gross — inside the 2BR/2-bath 65-80 m² band).

Definition

2s/2br/squarish/2s_2br_sq_rear_stair_bath_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

2-STOREY squarish catalog topology #1 — REAR-CORNER STAIR, hall-less GF (authored 2026-07-19 per MULTISTOREY_V2_DESIGN.md and the multistorey-topology-authoring checklist; first 2s topology outside the narrow shell).

Why this parti is the SQUARISH take on a 2-storey 2BR: the ~6 m buildable width fits TWO bedrooms side-by-side across the front — both get street-facing windows — which the 4 m narrow shell can never do (its 2s pair stacks master front / br2 rear instead). The extra width also feeds a proper great room (~6 m wide) and roomier baths than the narrow sibling. Canonical 10x10 lot = 6x6 buildable x 2 floors = 72 m² gross, landing INSIDE the locked 2BR/2-bath 65-80 m² floor band that the narrow 2s pair (48-64 m²) undershoots.

Canonical layout (10 x 10 lot, 2 m setbacks, 6 x 6 buildable per floor): GF: Front: [great room (entry, full width, counter at rear edge)] Rear: [stair | T&B | kitchen ] kitchen rear-pinned 2F: Front: [br2 | master ] side-by-side Mid: [stairwell | hall2 landing ] Rear: [stairwell | T&B | master closet ] Stair: straight, 0.9-1.2 m wide, >= 3.5 m run, 15 risers @ 200 mm / 250 mm treads, left-anchored into the rear corner; boards off the great room, arrives at hall2 (stair_boarding / stair_arrival hold the two mouths to opposite ends of the run).

Structure/override choices (each per an established convention): - zone_split is a 2F-ONLY horizontal partition pinning both bedrooms to the front band with bath2 behind — the side-by-side-bedrooms signature, stated as one split line instead of anchor lists (the 3BR catalog's over-constrained-anchors trap; only the stair is anchored, single-member list, the proven-safe pattern). - zone_balance_rooms counts the whole upper floor minus stairwell as the private wing — same reasoning as the narrow hall-less sibling: bedrooms-alone can't outweigh a full-width GF great room + kitchen. - Hall-less GF: at 36 m²/floor the great room is the circulation (stair boards off it, bath1 doors into it) per the compact convention; a _hall sibling for larger squarish lots (with fallback_below_buildable_sqm routing back here) is future work. - soft proximity bath2->stairwell pulls the upper bath toward the rear corner, near the GF wet bay, to shorten the vertical plumbing run (cross-floor wet-stack alignment is still soft-only in v2).

Naming note: the `bath` token undercounts the program (two common baths, one per floor) — kept consistent with the 2s narrow siblings pending the 2s naming-convention pass.

Key adjacencies

  • great ↔ kitchen — GF: kitchen opens directly off the great room — dining counter on the shared edge (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass = 2.1 m min); band + stools live on the great-room side to keep the galley aisle clear. Render-only (counter_divider) — briefs may set dining_counter: false to suppress it without touching this topology.open_plan
  • great ↔ bath1 — GF: T&B doors into the great room — hall-less GF, the great room IS the circulation; door-placement scoring keeps it off the entry sightlinebath_door
  • great ↔ stair — GF: open stair boards straight off the great room; the boarding constraint holds this edge to the flight's bottom endstair_boarding
  • kitchen ↔ bath1 — GF: T&B shares a plumbing wall with the kitchen — single GF wet bay at the rear; kitchen keeps its rear service doorwet_core
  • stair ↔ stairwell — VERTICAL circulation edge: flight and stairwell opening occupy the identical rectangle on their floors (rect-equality in the solver, graph connectivity for validation)stair_vertical
  • stairwell ↔ hall2 — 2F: the landing hall must meet the stairwell at the TOP end of the flight — step off into circulation, never a bedroom wallstair_arrival
  • hall2 ↔ master — 2F: master door off the landing hallbedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ br2 — 2F: second bedroom door off the landing hallbedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ bath2 — 2F: second T&B doors off the landing hallbath_door

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (stair_run_geometry).
  • zone_balance_rooms — custom room grouping overrides the default public/private area split (private: master, br2, bath2, hall2; public: great, kitchen).
  • aspect_overrides — per-room aspect-ratio (w:h) floor for stairs (4.0:1).
  • Two-storey: GF flight and 2F stairwell pinned to the identical rectangle (stair_vertical); ascent direction forced via stair_boarding/stair_arrival so the flight can't top out against a wall.

'public_rooms' here is the mechanical 'other side of the split' list, not a zoning claim; hall2 and stairwell float."
  },

      "name": "stair_run_geometry",
      "when": {
        "buildable_area_lt_sqm": 99999
      },
      "auto_apply": {
        "stairs": {
          "min_greatest_dim_m": 3.5,
          "max_least_dim_m": 1.2
        },
        "common_bath": {
          "max_greatest_dim_m": 2.6,
          "max_area_sqm": 3.9
        }
      },
      "rationale": "ALWAYS-ON (the 99999 trigger): a straight stair's run is riser math the solver cannot derive \u2014 3.0 m floor-to-floor at 15 x 200 mm risers needs 14 x 250 mm treads = 3.5 m. min_greatest_dim_m pins the run; max_least_dim_m 1.2 keeps the flight a flight. The common_bath caps stop post-snap gap-filling from stretching a bath into a corridor (post-snap aspect is unchecked); 2.6 m / 3.9 m\u00b2 is roomier than the narrow 2s sibling's 2.5 / 2.5 because this 36 m\u00b2-per-floor shell can afford proper baths."
    }
  ],

      "great",

      "great",

    ],
    [
      "great",
      "stair"
    ]
  ],

      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false
    }
  ],

    "multistorey-topology-authoring checklist; first 2s topology outside the",

    "width fits TWO bedrooms side-by-side across the front \u2014 both get",
    "street-facing windows \u2014 which the 4 m narrow shell can never do (its 2s",
    "pair stacks master front / br2 rear instead). The extra width also feeds",
    "a proper great room (~6 m wide) and roomier baths than the narrow",
    "sibling. Canonical 10x10 lot = 6x6 buildable x 2 floors = 72 m\u00b2 gross,",
    "landing INSIDE the locked 2BR/2-bath 65-80 m\u00b2 floor band that the",
    "narrow 2s pair (48-64 m\u00b2) undershoots.",
    "",
    "Canonical layout (10 x 10 lot, 2 m setbacks, 6 x 6 buildable per floor):",
    "  GF:  Front: [great room (entry, full width, counter at rear edge)]",
    "       Rear:  [stair | T&B | kitchen              ]  kitchen rear-pinned",
    "  2F:  Front: [br2        | master               ]  side-by-side",
    "       Mid:   [stairwell | hall2 landing         ]",
    "       Rear:  [stairwell | T&B | master closet   ]",
    "Stair: straight, 0.9-1.2 m wide, >= 3.5 m run, 15 risers @ 200 mm /",
    "250 mm treads, left-anchored into the rear corner; boards off the great",
    "room, arrives at hall2 (stair_boarding / stair_arrival hold the two",
    "mouths to opposite ends of the run).",
    "",
    "Structure/override choices (each per an established convention):",
    "  - zone_split is a 2F-ONLY horizontal partition pinning both bedrooms",
    "    to the front band with bath2 behind \u2014 the side-by-side-bedrooms",
    "    signature, stated as one split line instead of anchor lists (the",
    "    3BR catalog's over-constrained-anchors trap; only the stair is",
    "    anchored, single-member list, the proven-safe pattern).",
    "  - zone_balance_rooms counts the whole upper floor minus stairwell as",
    "    the private wing \u2014 same reasoning as the narrow hall-less sibling:",
    "    bedrooms-alone can't outweigh a full-width GF great room + kitchen.",
    "  - Hall-less GF: at 36 m\u00b2/floor the great room is the circulation",
    "    (stair boards off it, bath1 doors into it) per the compact",
    "    convention; a _hall sibling for larger squarish lots (with",
    "    fallback_below_buildable_sqm routing back here) is future work.",
    "  - soft proximity bath2->stairwell pulls the upper bath toward the",
    "    rear corner, near the GF wet bay, to shorten the vertical plumbing",
    "    run (cross-floor wet-stack alignment is still soft-only in v2).",
    "",
    "Naming note: the `bath` token undercounts the program (two common",
    "baths, one per floor) \u2014 kept consistent with the 2s narrow siblings",
    "pending the 2s naming-convention pass."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/2s_2br_10x10_sq_rear_stair_bath_gr_ncp.json

2-STOREY 2BR / 2 bath squarish rear-corner-stair smoke test on the canonical 10x10 lot, no carport. 6x6 envelope per floor (36 m² footprint, 72 m² gross) — inside the locked 2BR/2-bath 65-80 m² band that the narrow 2s pair undershoots. Exercises the squarish 2s signature: both bedrooms side-by-side at the front with street-facing windows (2F-only zone_split), hall-less GF with the great room as circulation, dining counter on the great/kitchen edge, rear-corner stair with the bath2->stairwell wet-stack soft proximity, and the composite GROUND FLOOR | SECOND FLOOR SVG.

Lot 10×10 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 6×6 m buildable envelope — 36 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot10 × 10 m (100 m²)
Buildable envelope6 × 6 m (36 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

both bedrooms upstairs, side-by-side, street-facing windows · hall-less ground floor — great room is the circulation · dining counter between great room and kitchen · straight stair in the rear corner off the great room · one T&B per floor, upper bath near the wet stack · no carport

No. 16 / 46

1s_2br_nw_front_back_split_bath

Not regression-tested
1s_2br_nw_front_back_split_bath · single-storey · Narrow · 2 bed · 1 bath

Narrow-shell 2BR / 1 bath, FRONT-BACK split, no courtyard, no hall — great_room + kitchen occupy the front, master + standard + common bath sit side by side across the rear, each dooring directly across the boundary wall. Revised from an earlier courtyard version at the user's request: the open-air light well is removed and the boundary simply closes up.

Not yet regression-tested. No test brief in briefs/test/ currently exercises this topology through the solver (or every brief that references it falls back to a different topology before solving). Static structural check only — load_topology() loads cleanly — every room ID is unique, every adjacency references a real room, and every habitable room is reachable from the entry point. No test brief, rendered output, or solver-derived room sizing exists to show for this entry; nothing below is fabricated to fill the gap.

Definition

1s/2br/narrow/1s_2br_nw_front_back_split_bath.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

Narrow-shell catalog topology — front-back split, revised at the user's request to remove the open-air courtyard from the original concept. Without the courtyard as a buffer, the public/private boundary simply closes into a single wall, and every private room doors directly across it into great_room — same circulation idiom as 1s_2br_sq_front_back_split_baths_ds_lk, minus the ensuite (single common bath here, matching the simpler 1-bath program used across all three narrow options).

Layout intent (canonical form, front/street at the bottom): Rear (private): [master | standard | common (wet-core w/ kitchen)] Front (public): [great_room (entry) | kitchen ]

IMPORTANT FEASIBILITY CAVEAT: this is the least natural fit of the three narrow options. Options 1 and 2 both stack rooms front-to-rear, which leans into a narrow lot's real advantage — depth is abundant, width is scarce. This option does the opposite: it needs master, standard, AND common bath to fit side by side across ONE row of width. On a true 6 m-frontage lot that's very tight (three rooms in maybe 4.5-5 m of buildable width). Recommend treating this as the option that wants the WIDER end of 'narrow' (8 m+ frontage) rather than the narrowest lots — or treat it as a candidate for a future 2-story version, where the rear band's footprint only has to hold one bedroom per floor instead of two side by side.

Removing the courtyard also removes its light/ventilation benefit for the middle of the house — worth keeping in mind if the eventual buildable envelope puts common bath or an interior wall far from both the front and rear exterior walls.

No carport declared — side setbacks on a narrow lot rarely have room for one; see 1s_2br_nw_front_carport_spine_bath.json for the front-loaded carport pattern, which could be adapted here too.

No fallback declared yet — first draft.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ standard — master and standard sit side by side across the rear band — shared wall only, no door.bedroom_to_bedroom_wall
  • standard ↔ common — common bath sits at the far end of the rear band, next to standard — shared wall only, no door.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • master ↔ great — master's front wall = great's rear wall at the boundary — direct door, no hall.bedroom_to_public
  • standard ↔ great — standard's front wall overlaps great's rear wall — direct door. 1.2 m floor gives 0.8 m clear door + frame each side.bedroom_to_public
  • common ↔ great — common bath's door opens into great — DEFAULT host of the 'common_access' door-host group. Both bedrooms reach it by stepping out into the public zone and back in, same idiom as squarish's side_split_bath_gr, rotated 90 degrees for the narrow shell.bath_door
  • common ↔ kitchen — shared plumbing wall — common bath sits behind kitchen's edge. ALTERNATE host of 'common_access': door placed here instead of great when this wall >= 1.5 m.wet_core
  • great ↔ kitchen — great_room + kitchen form one open public band at the front — no separate dining room in this variant.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_nw_front_back_split_bath).
  • door_host_group — at least one door's host wall is chosen by the solver's auto-scorer (or overridden per-brief via door_host), not fixed in the topology.

No. 17 / 46

1s_2br_nw_front_carport_spine_bath

Not regression-tested
1s_2br_nw_front_carport_spine_bath · single-storey · Narrow · 2 bed · 1 bath

Narrow-shell 2BR / 1 bath, FRONT CARPORT + PUBLIC SPINE — a full-width covered carport sits ahead of the house at the street. Behind it, great_room forms a public spine running back to the kitchen. A short hall (only as deep as the three rooms it serves, not the whole house) branches off the spine to reach master, common bath, and standard.

Not yet regression-tested. No test brief in briefs/test/ currently exercises this topology through the solver (or every brief that references it falls back to a different topology before solving). Static structural check only — load_topology() loads cleanly — every room ID is unique, every adjacency references a real room, and every habitable room is reachable from the entry point. No test brief, rendered output, or solver-derived room sizing exists to show for this entry; nothing below is fabricated to fill the gap.

Definition

1s/2br/narrow/1s_2br_nw_front_carport_spine_bath.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

Narrow-shell catalog topology — front-loaded carport variant. Solves two narrow-lot problems at once: (1) no side-setback width for a carport, and (2) a full-depth corridor is expensive on a lot that's already width-starved.

Layout intent (canonical, bedroom wing on the right): [CARPORT, full width, at street] -> [GREAT (entry)] -> [KITCHEN | (hall -> MASTER -> COMMON -> STANDARD)] front / street -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------> rear

Reuses the same 'reach the bath by walking through the public zone' idiom as squarish's side_split_bath_gr family, but the public zone doing the connecting is the great_room+kitchen spine, not a great_room flanked by two bedroom columns. The bedroom wing branches off that spine through a SHORT hall — only as deep as the three rooms it serves, not the whole house depth. That's the space-saving difference from 1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall, which pays the corridor's area cost across the entire house length.

Common bath is deliberately placed at the REAR of the wing, next to kitchen, so it can share a wet-stack wall with kitchen — same plumbing-efficiency convention used everywhere else in the catalog. Standard ends up rear-most; master, being closer to the spine, gets the shorter walk to the entry.

Requires a bit more buildable width than the side-corridor option, since the bedroom wing and the kitchen have to sit side by side for at least part of the house's depth (not fully single-file). Best suited to the wider end of 'narrow' — roughly 7-8 m frontage rather than 6 m.

No fallback declared yet — first draft.

Key adjacencies

  • great ↔ kitchen — great_room + kitchen form one open LDK, kitchen at the rear — the spine's public half.open_plan
  • great ↔ hall — hall branches off the spine — open mouth, no door, codebase rule: hallway↔public is always open-plan. This hall only serves the three-room bedroom wing, NOT the whole house depth (unlike 1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall).open_plan
  • hall ↔ master — master's only door — opens onto the short wing hall. Master sits closest to the spine, first in the wing.bedroom_door
  • master ↔ common — master and common bath sit next to each other in the wing — shared wall only, no door.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • hall ↔ common — common bath's door opens onto the wing hall.bath_door
  • common ↔ kitchen — common bath sits at the wing's rear edge, directly beside kitchen (the spine's other rear-facing room) — shared plumbing wall, same wet-stack convention as the rest of the catalog. This is the payoff for putting common at the REAR of the wing instead of the front.wet_core
  • common ↔ standard — standard sits at the rear-most end of the wing, past common bath — shared wall only, no door.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • hall ↔ standard — standard's only door — opens onto the wing hall, same as master. Reached without passing through master or common.bedroom_door

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_nw_front_carport_spine_bath).
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.

      "preferred_apply": {
        "kitchen": {
          "min_least_dim_m": 2.0
        }
      },
      "rationale": "Because the hall here is short (wing-only, ~2-2.5 m of depth instead of the full house), it's capped at 3.0 sqm rather than floored aggressively \u2014 the space savings vs. the side-corridor variant is the whole point of this layout, so the solver shouldn't let the hall creep back up to full-length proportions."
    }
  ],
  "setback_elements": [
    {
      "type": "carport",
      "location": "front_setback",
      "covered": true,
      "width_m": 5.0,
      "depth_m": 5.5,
      "note": "full-width, front-loaded \u2014 narrow lots rarely have side-setback room for a carport, so it sits ahead of the house at the street instead of beside it. Width matches the lot's buildable width rather than a fixed 3.0 m single-bay."
    },
    {
      "type": "dirty_kitchen",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "behind": "kitchen"
    },
    {
      "type": "service_area",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false
    }
  ],
  "building_voids": [],
  "notes": [
    "Narrow-shell catalog topology \u2014 front-loaded carport variant. Solves two narrow-lot problems at once: (1) no side-setback width for a carport, and (2) a full-depth corridor is expensive on a lot that's already width-starved.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (canonical, bedroom wing on the right):",
    "  [CARPORT, full width, at street] -> [GREAT (entry)] -> [KITCHEN | (hall -> MASTER -> COMMON -> STANDARD)]",
    "  front / street  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------->  rear",
    "",
    "Reuses the same 'reach the bath by walking through the public zone' idiom as squarish's side_split_bath_gr family, but the public zone doing the connecting is the great_room+kitchen spine, not a great_room flanked by two bedroom columns. The bedroom wing branches off that spine through a SHORT hall \u2014 only as deep as the three rooms it serves, not the whole house depth. That's the space-saving difference from 1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall, which pays the corridor's area cost across the entire house length.",
    "",
    "Common bath is deliberately placed at the REAR of the wing, next to kitchen, so it can share a wet-stack wall with kitchen \u2014 same plumbing-efficiency convention used everywhere else in the catalog. Standard ends up rear-most; master, being closer to the spine, gets the shorter walk to the entry.",
    "",
    "Requires a bit more buildable width than the side-corridor option, since the bedroom wing and the kitchen have to sit side by side for at least part of the house's depth (not fully single-file). Best suited to the wider end of 'narrow' \u2014 roughly 7-8 m frontage rather than 6 m.",
    "",
    "No fallback declared yet \u2014 first draft."
  ]
}
← Back to catalog
No. 18 / 46

1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall

Not regression-tested
1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall · single-storey · Narrow · 2 bed · 1 bath

Narrow-shell 2BR / 1 bath, SIDE-CORRIDOR SHOTGUN — a single hallway runs the full depth of the house along one side; great_room, standard, master, common bath, and kitchen bud off it one after another from front (street) to rear, in that order. No room is ever reached through another room.

Not yet regression-tested. No test brief in briefs/test/ currently exercises this topology through the solver (or every brief that references it falls back to a different topology before solving). Static structural check only — load_topology() loads cleanly — every room ID is unique, every adjacency references a real room, and every habitable room is reachable from the entry point. No test brief, rendered output, or solver-derived room sizing exists to show for this entry; nothing below is fabricated to fill the gap.

Definition

1s/2br/narrow/1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

Narrow-shell catalog topology — first entry for the 'narrow' target_shell (frontage ~6-8 m, depth ~15-20 m, roughly 1:2.2-1:3). Every squarish/wide topology splits the lot into two columns side by side; a narrow lot usually doesn't have the width budget for that, so this reuses the vertical-split idiom's DOORS and RULES but arranges the rooms in a single front-to-rear column instead, served by one corridor running the full depth along one side.

Layout intent (canonical, corridor on the right): [GREAT (entry) | hall] — [STANDARD | hall] — [MASTER | hall] — [COMMON | hall] — [KITCHEN | hall] front / street ----------------------------------------------------------> rear

This is deliberately the most conservative of the three narrow options: it reuses every door/hall idiom already validated in the squarish 'cl_hall_gr' family (bedroom_door, bath_door, hallway-always-open) rather than inventing anything new — just stretched lengthwise instead of applied as a mid-band strip.

Trade-off: the corridor runs the ENTIRE depth of the house, so its area cost is paid once per room rather than once total. On the narrowest lots this can eat a meaningful share of the buildable width. See 1s_2br_nw_front_carport_spine_bath.json for a variant that shrinks the hall to just the bedroom wing.

No carport declared — NCP form. Narrow lots rarely have side-setback room for a carport; a front-loaded covered parking bay can be added by trimming great_room's front depth and adding a building_void, similar to the front-carport option.

Side setbacks: assumes at least one side approaches a firewall/party-wall condition (PD 1096) with no window openings on that face — the corridor side is a reasonable place to put that wall, since a corridor doesn't need windows the way a bedroom does.

No fallback declared yet — first draft, not yet validated against a smaller-lot fallback.

Key adjacencies

  • great ↔ hall — hall's front end opens into great_room — no door, codebase rule: hallway↔public is always open-plan. This is also where the main entry sits.open_plan
  • great ↔ standard — great's rear wall = standard's front wall — geometry-only, NO door. Standard's actual access is the hall; this keeps the building a clean rectangle at the seam.bedroom_to_public_wall
  • standard ↔ hall — standard's only door — opens onto the corridor.bedroom_door
  • standard ↔ master — standard and master stack directly front-to-rear along the room column — shared wall only, no door.bedroom_to_bedroom_wall
  • master ↔ hall — master's only door — opens onto the corridor. Master sits closer to the front than the rear pair (common bath + kitchen), same front-of-bedroom-wing convention used throughout the squarish/wide catalog.bedroom_door
  • master ↔ common — master and common bath sit next in the sequence — shared wall only, no door. Acts as the acoustic buffer between the bedroom pair and the wet rooms at the rear.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • common ↔ hall — common bath's only door — opens onto the corridor. Both bedrooms reach it by walking the corridor, never through each other.bath_door
  • common ↔ kitchen — shared plumbing wall — common bath and kitchen cluster at the rear end of the house, same wet-stack convention used in every other topology in the catalog.wet_core
  • kitchen ↔ hall — corridor's rear end opens into kitchen — no door. Kitchen is the terminal room of the shotgun sequence.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall).
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.

No. 19 / 46

1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_hall

Verified
1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_hall · single-storey · Narrow · 2 bed · 2 bath

Narrow-shell 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, SIDE-CORRIDOR — master spans the full width at the rear-most band, with an ensuite tucked directly below it. Common bath sits beside the ensuite, standard bedroom occupies the full width below that. A hall runs down the right side (from master's bottom-right corner to the great room) serving master, common bath, and standard. Kitchen and great_room sit side by side at the front. Two-bath sibling of 1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall.

Definition

1s/2br/narrow/1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_hall.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

Narrow-shell catalog topology — built directly from a hand-sketched concept. Two-bath sibling of 1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall: same side-corridor shotgun logic (master, standard, and common bath each door onto a single hall running down one side), with an ensuite added as master's exclusive private bath, tucked directly below master and sharing a wet-core wall with common bath.

Layout intent (canonical, front/street at the bottom, hall on the right): Rear: MASTER BR (full width) Mid-rear: ENSUITE | COMMON T&B | (hall starts here) Mid: STANDARD BR (full width, minus hall) | hall (cont.) Front: KITCHEN | GREAT ROOM | (hall opens into great)

Master has two doors: a private one into ensuite, and a public one into the top of the hall — same two-door pattern already used for master in 1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr, just applied to a single-column narrow layout instead of a mid-band hall.

Deliberate trade-off vs. the rest of the catalog: kitchen and common bath do NOT share a wet-core wall here — they're geometrically separated by the full-width standard bedroom in between, since this design stays faithful to the sketch's room order rather than re-ordering purely for plumbing efficiency (contrast with 1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall, which was reordered specifically to put common bath next to kitchen). Two separate plumbing runs instead of one consolidated wet stack.

Master's row (top) has no hall notch — it spans the full building width, since the hall only begins at the seam between master and ensuite/common. This means master is WIDER than standard, which sits beside the hall for its whole depth. match_bedroom_widths is false for this reason.

No carport declared — same as the rest of the narrow catalog; see 1s_2br_nw_front_carport_spine_bath.json for the front-loaded carport pattern if one is needed here.

No fallback declared yet — first draft, not yet validated against a smaller-lot fallback.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is master's exclusive — sits directly below master's left portion. Door opens down from master's rear-band floor into ensuite.ensuite_door
  • master ↔ hall — master's public route — a second door at master's bottom-right corner, opening into the top of the hall. Master has two doors: one private (ensuite), one public (hall), same pattern used in 1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr.bedroom_door
  • ensuite ↔ common — ensuite and common bath share a plumbing wall, side by side directly under master — consolidates both wet stacks into one bay. No door — ensuite stays master-exclusive.wet_core
  • common ↔ hall — common bath's only door — opens onto the hall on its east wall.bath_door
  • ensuite ↔ standard — ensuite's south wall touches standard's north wall — shared wall only, no door. Acoustic buffer, same idiom as the squarish 'ds' distributed-bath topologies.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • common ↔ standard — common bath's south wall also touches standard's north wall — shared wall only, no door.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • standard ↔ hall — standard's only door — opens onto the hall, same wall the hall runs down.bedroom_door
  • standard ↔ kitchen — standard's south wall = kitchen's north wall — geometry-only, NO door. Standard's real door is to the hall; this just keeps the outline flush.bedroom_to_public_wall
  • standard ↔ great — standard's south wall also runs along great_room's north wall further east — geometry-only, NO door, same reasoning as standard↔kitchen.bedroom_to_public_wall
  • hall ↔ great — hall's bottom end opens into great_room — no door, codebase rule: hallway↔public is always open-plan. This is the hall's only connection to the front public band.open_plan
  • kitchen ↔ great — kitchen and great_room sit side by side at the front, open-plan — classic gr-style LDK.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_hall).
  • ldk_horizontal: true — disables the solver's default vertical LDK-stacking rules so great_room/kitchen can sit side-by-side.

      "kitchen",
      "great"
    ]
  },

  ],

  ],

  "lot_adjustment_profiles": [
    {
      "name": "compact_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_hall",
      "when": {
        "buildable_area_lt_sqm": 85
      },
      "auto_apply": {
        "hallway": {
          "min_least_dim_m": 0.95
        },
        "kitchen": {
          "min_least_dim_m": 1.8
        },
        "ensuite_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 2.2
        },
        "common_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 3.0
        },
        "master_bedroom": {
          "max_area_sqm": 18.0
        }
      },
      "preferred_apply": {
        "ensuite_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 2.5
        },
        "kitchen": {
          "min_least_dim_m": 2.0
        }
      },
      "rationale": "Adding an ensuite on top of the single-bath side-corridor pattern (1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall) means master's row now has to fit master + ensuite + common + hall side by side within the same scarce width, so ensuite floors low (2.2 sqm auto, 2.5 preferred) to leave room for the hall and common. Hall's min stays pinned tight (0.95 m); no max cap here (unlike the 1-bath sibling) since hall is a continuous corridor running the full depth from great_room past standard and common (ending at master's front wall, where master's door onto it sits) rather than one fixed-width slot, so it needs headroom for whatever width the standard/ensuite/common split leaves it."
    }
  ],
  "setback_elements": [
    {
      "type": "dirty_kitchen",
      "location": "side_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "behind": "kitchen",
      "note": "kitchen sits at the front here (street side), same consequence as the other front-band-kitchen topologies \u2014 no rear wall to extend a service tail from, so dirty kitchen moves to kitchen's side setback instead of rear_setback."
    },
    {
      "type": "service_area",
      "location": "side_setback",
      "covered": false
    }
  ],
  "building_voids": [],
  "notes": [
    "Narrow-shell catalog topology \u2014 built directly from a hand-sketched concept. Two-bath sibling of 1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall: same side-corridor shotgun logic (master, standard, and common bath each door onto a single hall running down one side), with an ensuite added as master's exclusive private bath, tucked directly below master and sharing a wet-core wall with common bath.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (canonical, front/street at the bottom, hall on the right):",
    "  Rear:   MASTER BR (full width)",
    "  Mid-rear: ENSUITE | COMMON T&B | (hall starts here)",
    "  Mid:    STANDARD BR (full width, minus hall) | hall (cont.)",
    "  Front:  KITCHEN | GREAT ROOM | (hall opens into great)",
    "",
    "Master has two doors: a private one into ensuite, and a public one into the top of the hall \u2014 same two-door pattern already used for master in 1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr, just applied to a single-column narrow layout instead of a mid-band hall.",
    "",
    "Deliberate trade-off vs. the rest of the catalog: kitchen and common bath do NOT share a wet-core wall here \u2014 they're geometrically separated by the full-width standard bedroom in between, since this design stays faithful to the sketch's room order rather than re-ordering purely for plumbing efficiency (contrast with 1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall, which was reordered specifically to put common bath next to kitchen). Two separate plumbing runs instead of one consolidated wet stack.",
    "",
    "Master's row (top) has no hall notch \u2014 it spans the full building width, since the hall only begins at the seam between master and ensuite/common. This means master is WIDER than standard, which sits beside the hall for its whole depth. match_bedroom_widths is false for this reason.",
    "",
    "No carport declared \u2014 same as the rest of the narrow catalog; see 1s_2br_nw_front_carport_spine_bath.json for the front-loaded carport pattern if one is needed here.",
    "",
    "No fallback declared yet \u2014 first draft, not yet validated against a smaller-lot fallback."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/1s_2br_8x17_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_hall_ncp.json

Narrow-shell 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, SIDE-CORRIDOR — 8x17 lot, NO CARPORT. A single hallway serves master, common bath, and standard; master (full width at the rear) reaches the hall via a small notch beside it (hall_notch_joins_master), not by the hall running the full house depth. Kitchen and great_room sit side by side at the front.

Lot 8×17 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 4×13 m buildable envelope — 52 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot8 × 17 m (136 m²)
Buildable envelope4 × 13 m (52 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

master ensuite, master's private door is to ensuite · master's second door reaches the hall via a small notch on its right side · standard and common bath both door onto the hall · kitchen and great_room side by side at the front, no hall column between them · no carport

1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_rear_kitchen

Verified
1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_rear_kitchen · single-storey · Narrow · 2 bed · 2 bath

Narrow-shell 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, SIDE-CORRIDOR, kitchen at the rear — two parallel front-to-rear columns rather than great_room-full-width-plus-rows. Left column (front to rear): common bath, standard bedroom, master bedroom, ensuite. Right column: great_room, hall, kitchen. Common bath doors directly into great_room (its front-column neighbor) instead of the hall; standard and master both door onto the hall from across the column boundary.

Definition

1s/2br/narrow/1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_rear_kitchen.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

Narrow-shell catalog topology, built from a dimensioned sketch (6.0 x 10.0 m footprint, 60 sqm). The original sketch described a 'great_room full width at front, three rows behind it' layout, but that structure is not realizable by this solver's rectangle model: it requires great_room, hall, AND standard to be mutually adjacent (a 3-way triangle) while common bath ALSO needs to reach both great_room and standard — three separate rooms all needing to border the same pair of side-by-side rooms is a geometric impossibility (only two rooms can flank a pair from either side). The realized design keeps every declared room-to-room adjacency but resolves them as two parallel front-to-rear columns instead — see the diagnostic trail for the full derivation.

Actual layout (canonical, front/street at the bottom, hall on the right): LEFT COLUMN (front to rear): COMMON T&B -> STANDARD BR -> MASTER BR -> ENSUITE RIGHT COLUMN (front to rear): GREAT ROOM -> HALL -> KITCHEN

Common bath and great_room are both the front-most room in their column, so common's door opens directly into great_room across the column boundary — same precedent as 1s_2br_sq_side_split_bath_gr's common-bath-to-great_room door. Standard and master each reach the hall the same way, across the column boundary at their respective rows. Kitchen sits at the REAR, sharing a wet-core wall with ensuite (also rear-most, other column), and reaching the rest of the house only through the hall — it does not border great_room directly, same long-walk trade-off as 1s_2br_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall. This also means the classic rear_setback dirty-kitchen pattern applies cleanly, unlike the front-band-kitchen narrow variants.

Master has a single public door (to hall) plus its private ensuite door — same two-door pattern used throughout the catalog wherever an ensuite coexists with hall access.

6.0 x 10.0 m building footprint (1:1.67) is narrower than squarish but shallower than the 1:2.2-1:3 ratio the rest of the narrow catalog assumes — this one tests the shallow end of 'narrow' rather than the deep end.

No carport declared — consistent with the rest of the narrow catalog. No fallback declared yet — first draft.

Key adjacencies

  • ensuite ↔ kitchen — ensuite (left column, rear-most) and kitchen (right column, rear-most) sit side by side across the column boundary, sharing a plumbing wall — the one wet-stack consolidation this layout achieves. No door; ensuite stays master-exclusive.wet_core
  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is master's exclusive — sits directly behind master in the left column (common, standard, master, ensuite front-to-rear). Door opens from master's rear wall into ensuite.ensuite_door
  • kitchen ↔ hall — kitchen sits directly behind hall in the right column (great, hall, kitchen front-to-rear) — no door, same 'public room open to hall' treatment used for great_room elsewhere in the catalog. Kitchen's only connection to the rest of the house is via the hall; it does not border great_room directly.open_plan
  • master ↔ hall — master's only public door — opens onto the hall across the column boundary (left column's 3rd room aligns with the right column's 2nd room).bedroom_door
  • master ↔ standard — master and standard are consecutive in the left column (standard directly in front of master) — shared wall only, no door.bedroom_to_bedroom_wall
  • common ↔ standard — common and standard are consecutive in the left column (common front-most, standard behind it) — shared wall only, no door.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • standard ↔ hall — standard's only door — opens onto the hall across the column boundary.bedroom_door
  • common ↔ great — common bath's only door — opens directly into great_room, its front-column neighbor across the boundary (both are the front-most room in their column). Same precedent as common bath dooring into great_room in 1s_2br_sq_side_split_bath_gr.bath_door
  • standard ↔ great — great_room is tall enough to also reach across into standard's row (one row behind common) — geometry-only, NO door. Standard's real door is to the hall.bedroom_to_public_wall
  • hall ↔ great — hall sits directly behind great_room in the right column — no door, codebase rule: hallway↔public is always open-plan.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_rear_kitchen).

2s_2br_nw_side_spine_stair_bath

Verified
2s_2br_nw_side_spine_stair_bath · 2-storey · Narrow · 2 bed · 2 bath

Narrow-shell 2-STOREY 2BR / 2 baths, SIDE-SPINE STAIR, HALL-LESS ground floor — the compact sibling of the _hall variant. The open great room (entry, dining folds in) is itself the GF circulation: the stair boards straight off it, the T&B doors into it, and the kitchen opens directly behind it. Second floor keeps its landing hall (bedrooms always door off circulation). Fits an 8x10 lot (4x6 m buildable per floor).

Definition

2s/2br/narrow/2s_2br_nw_side_spine_stair_bath.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

2-STOREY narrow catalog topology — SIDE-SPINE STAIR, HALL-LESS GF (authored 2026-07-19 as the compact sibling of 2s_2br_nw_side_spine_stair_bath_hall, which declares this file as its fallback_topology with fallback_below_buildable_sqm: 28 — shells under 28 m²/floor auto-route here; see MULTISTOREY_V2_DESIGN.md).

Rationale: on a 24 m² floor (8x10 lot) a GF hall costs ~3.7 m² — 15% of the floor — purely for circulation on an already open plan. Dropping it hands that area to the great room and kitchen, and NEWLY enables the dining counter: great_room and kitchen share a wall only in this parti (the hall sibling's bath band separates them).

Canonical layout (8 x 10 lot, 2 m setbacks, 4 x 6 buildable per floor): GF: Front: [great room (entry, full width, counter at rear edge)] Rear: [stair | T&B | kitchen galley ] kitchen rear-pinned 2F: Front: [master (full width) ] Mid: [stairwell | hall2 landing | T&B ] Rear: [stairwell | br2 ] Stair: straight, left wall, 0.9 m wide, >= 3.5 m run, 15 risers @ 200 mm / 250 mm treads; boards off the great room, arrives at hall2 (stair_boarding / stair_arrival hold the two mouths to opposite ends).

Solver note: zone_balance_rooms counts the WHOLE upper floor (minus stairwell) as the private wing. Without it this topology is provably infeasible: the great room is forced full-width (it must reach both the stair-boarding overlap and the 2.1 m kitchen counter edge) at >= 2.22 m depth (aspect), and the kitchen is forced to 2.1 x 3.5 (rear-pinned + touching great), so GF public >= ~16.3 m² while master+br2 alone top out at ~15.1 on a 24 m² floor — the default bedrooms-only private tally can never win. Counting the 2F wing whole (bath + landing included) is the architecturally truthful 2-storey reading. The _hall sibling passes without this override only because its hall eats 3.7 m² OUT of the public tally.

Trade-offs vs the _hall sibling (deliberate, per 2026-07-19 decision): - bath1 door opens onto the great room (visible from the sala) — standard for compact PH plans, mitigated by door-corner scoring. - stair lands in the living space (noise) instead of a corridor. - +~3.7 m² of usable area and a real dining counter in exchange.

Naming note: the `bath` token undercounts the program (two common baths, one per floor) — kept consistent with the _hall sibling pending the 2s naming-convention pass.

Key adjacencies

  • great ↔ kitchen — GF: kitchen opens directly off the great room — dining counter on the shared edge (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass = 2.1 m min); band + stools live on the great-room side to keep the galley aisle clear. Render-only (counter_divider) — briefs may set dining_counter: false to suppress it without touching this topology.open_plan
  • great ↔ bath1 — GF: T&B doors into the great room — the privacy trade-off of dropping the hall; door-placement scoring pushes it to the least-exposed cornerbath_door
  • great ↔ stair — GF: open stair boards straight off the great room (the classic compact PH 2-storey move); the great room IS the circulation, and the boarding constraint holds this edge to the flight's bottom endstair_boarding
  • kitchen ↔ bath1 — GF: T&B shares a plumbing wall with the kitchen — single GF wet bay; kitchen keeps its rear service doorwet_core
  • stair ↔ stairwell — VERTICAL circulation edge: flight and stairwell opening occupy the identical rectangle on their floors (rect-equality in the solver, graph connectivity for validation)stair_vertical
  • stairwell ↔ hall2 — 2F: the landing hall must meet the stairwell at the TOP end of the flight — step off into circulation, never a bedroom wallstair_arrival
  • hall2 ↔ master — 2F: master door off the landing hallbedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ br2 — 2F: rear bedroom door off the landing hallbedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ bath2 — 2F: second T&B doors off the landing hallbath_door

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (stair_run_geometry).
  • zone_balance_rooms — custom room grouping overrides the default public/private area split (private: master, br2, bath2, hall2; public: great, kitchen).
  • aspect_overrides — per-room aspect-ratio (w:h) floor for stairs (4.0:1).
  • Two-storey: GF flight and 2F stairwell pinned to the identical rectangle (stair_vertical); ascent direction forced via stair_boarding/stair_arrival so the flight can't top out against a wall.

Test brief

test/2s_2br_8x10_nw_side_spine_stair_bath_ncp.json

2-STOREY 2BR / 2 bath HALL-LESS side-spine-stair smoke test — the compact sibling selected DIRECTLY, on its canonical 8x10 lot, no carport. 4x6 envelope per floor. The great room is the GF circulation: stair boards off it (left wall), T&B doors into it, kitchen opens behind it with a dining counter on the shared 2.1 m edge and an L-pantry alcove claimed from the dead strip behind the bath. Also exercises zone_balance_rooms (whole upper floor counts as the private wing) and the common_bath shape caps.

Lot 8×10 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 4×6 m buildable envelope — 24 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot8 × 10 m (80 m²)
Buildable envelope4 × 6 m (24 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

hall-less ground floor — great room is the circulation · open stair off the great room, left wall · dining counter between great room and kitchen · both bedrooms upstairs off the landing hall · one T&B per floor · no carport
