No. 22 / 46

2s_2br_nw_side_spine_stair_bath_hall

Verified
2s_2br_nw_side_spine_stair_bath_hall · 2-storey · Narrow · 2 bed · 2 bath

Narrow-shell 2-STOREY 2BR / 2 baths, SIDE-SPINE STAIR — straight stair hugs the left wall as the circulation spine on both floors. Ground floor: open great room (entry, dining folds in — gr style) at front, kitchen at rear, common T&B between them off the hall. Second floor: master at front, standard bedroom at rear, second T&B off the landing hall. Stairwell occupies the identical rectangle on both floors. The hall earns its keep from ~8x11 lots up; below 28 m²/floor buildable the runner auto-routes to the hall-less compact sibling (fallback_below_buildable_sqm).

Definition

2s/2br/narrow/2s_2br_nw_side_spine_stair_bath_hall.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

2-STOREY narrow catalog topology #1 — SIDE-SPINE STAIR (Variant A of the 2026-07-18 four-variant reference set). Converted 2026-07-18 from a fully- dimensioned reference spec (fixed coordinates on an 8 x 12 lot) into the topology-v0.1 adjacency-graph schema; the original spec is preserved in the session scratchpad and wherever it was drafted.

RUNNABLE since 2026-07-18 — first topology on the multi-storey v2 pipeline (MULTISTOREY_V2_DESIGN.md): one joint CP-SAT solve with a no-overlap group per storey, `storeys: 2` + per-room `storey: 1|2` consumed by the loader, the kind='stair_vertical' adjacency enforcing identical stair/stairwell rectangles across floors, per-floor validate/snap/archplan post-passes, and a side-by-side composite SVG (GROUND FLOOR | SECOND FLOOR). The always-on 'stair_run_geometry' profile pins the straight run at 3.5 m (riser math the solver cannot derive); stairs are frozen during gap-snapping so the flights stay identical (design decision D6). Cross-floor wet-stack proximity is a known v2 gap — see design doc, out of scope this phase.

Landing fix + gr conversion + 8x10 target (2026-07-18, later same day): - `stair_boarding` / `stair_arrival` adjacency kinds (with the solver's per-pair ascent decision) now guarantee boarding and arrival at OPPOSITE ends of the run — the reference drawing had the flight topping out against br2's wall with the upstairs hall at the wrong end. The solver resolves it by ascending REAR-to-FRONT: board at the flight's rear end off the hall, arrive at hall2's front-band landing serving master / bath2 / br2. - Ground floor converted lk -> gr: the reference's separate living + dining could never fit the 8x10 target lot (GF hard minimums 6+6+3+1.2+1.5+2.6 = 26.3 m² > the 24 m² 4x6 buildable), so dining folds into one open great_room. No dining counter is possible in this parti — great_room and kitchen never share a wall (the bath band sits between) — so dining furniture lives in the great room. - Verified COMPLIANT at 8x10 (canonical, 100% coverage both floors) and 8x12 (roomier; the old canonical).

Canonical layout (8 x 10 lot, 2 m setbacks, 4 x 6 buildable per floor): GF: Front: [great room (entry, full width) ] Mid: [stair | hall | T&B ] Rear: [stair | hall | kitchen ] kitchen rear-pinned 2F: Front: [master (full width) ] Mid: [stairwell | hall2 landing | T&B ] Rear: [stairwell | br2 ] Stair: straight, left wall, 0.9 m wide, >= 3.5 m run, 15 risers @ 200 mm (= PD 1096 hard max, zero margin) / 250 mm treads, ascending rear-to- front per the solver's landing decision. Stairwell rectangle identical on both floors (enforced, not just verified).

Conversion audit findings against the reference spec (2026-07-18): - Reference dining was 5.6 m² — BELOW the 6.0 m² dining_room hard minimum (moot after the gr conversion, kept for the record). - The spec claims 'fully_stacked' plumbing but its own coordinates put the 2F bath ~1.5 m FORWARD of the GF bath (same east x-band, zero vertical overlap; kitchen has br2 above). Converted as-drawn; a v2 wet-stack proximity term should pull bath2 over bath1. - The drawn GF 'Hall'+'Passage' slivers (1.08 / 1.56 m²) and 2F 'Hall'+'Hall 2' (0.90 / 1.95 m²) are each merged into ONE hallway room per floor — separately they'd violate the 1.5 m² hallway minimum; merged they pass. - No carport (reference classification carport: false) — no carport setback element or building void is declared; briefs for this topology should be ncp. Rear service/laundry yard kept as an opt-in service_area element. - Name/token note: the id's `bath` token undercounts the program (two common baths, one per floor — `baths_ds` territory under the locked 1s convention). Kept as-imported; renaming is a catalog decision for the 2s naming pass. - Gross floor area 64 m² (~57 net) sits below the locked 2BR/2-bath 45-65/65-80 m² bands, which were calibrated single-storey; the 2s floor-area bands are an open convention decision.

App-matcher note: surfaces in ai/match.py as a narrow 2BR/2-bath candidate and solves when selected. Its three sibling reference specs (front_stair_foyer, mid_core_ustair, rear_stair_understair) remain unconverted and list as 0-bedroom candidates that match nothing.

Key adjacencies

  • great ↔ hall — GF: hall's front mouth opens from the great room — no dooropen_plan
  • hall ↔ stair — GF: the hall must meet the flight at its BOTTOM end — the solver picks the ascent direction and holds boarding + arrival to opposite ends of the run (multi-storey v2 landing fix, 2026-07-18)stair_boarding
  • hall ↔ bath1 — GF: common T&B door opens off the hall's rear leg (the drawn 'Passage')bath_door
  • hall ↔ kitchen — GF: kitchen is reached from the hall's rear leg — dining and kitchen are NOT directly adjacent (bath band between them)open_plan
  • kitchen ↔ bath1 — GF: T&B backs onto the kitchen head wall (drawn shared edge 1.5 m) — single GF plumbing bay; kitchen keeps its rear service doorwet_core
  • stair ↔ stairwell — VERTICAL circulation edge, not a wall: stair (GF flight) and stairwell (2F opening) occupy the IDENTICAL rectangle on their respective floors. Present so the adjacency graph is connected (2F rooms reachable from entry). The v1 solver would wrongly read this as a same-floor shared wall — this topology must not be solved until multi-storey v2 interprets kind='stair_vertical' as a cross-floor rect-equality constraint.stair_vertical
  • stairwell ↔ hall2 — 2F: the upstairs hall must meet the stairwell at the TOP end of the flight — you step off the last tread into circulation, never against a bedroom wall (the reference drawing got this wrong: its hall flanked only the bottom of the opening)stair_arrival
  • hall2 ↔ master — 2F: master door on the hall's front edge (master holds the full-width front band)bedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ br2 — 2F: rear bedroom door on the hall's rear edgebedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ bath2 — 2F: second T&B door opens east off the hall (drawn shared edge 1.5 m)bath_door

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (stair_run_geometry).
  • aspect_overrides — per-room aspect-ratio (w:h) floor for stairs (4.0:1).
  • fallback_below_buildable_sqm / fallback_topology — below 28 m²/floor buildable area, the runner auto-routes to 2s_2br_nw_side_spine_stair_bath before attempting a solve.
  • Two-storey: GF flight and 2F stairwell pinned to the identical rectangle (stair_vertical); ascent direction forced via stair_boarding/stair_arrival so the flight can't top out against a wall.
2s_2br_nw_side_spine_stair_bath_hall.json 288 lines · 11.4 KB

  "notes": [
    "2-STOREY narrow catalog topology #1 \u2014 SIDE-SPINE STAIR (Variant A of the",
    "2026-07-18 four-variant reference set). Converted 2026-07-18 from a fully-",
    "dimensioned reference spec (fixed coordinates on an 8 x 12 lot) into the",
    "topology-v0.1 adjacency-graph schema; the original spec is preserved in",
    "the session scratchpad and wherever it was drafted.",
    "",
    "RUNNABLE since 2026-07-18 \u2014 first topology on the multi-storey v2",
    "pipeline (MULTISTOREY_V2_DESIGN.md): one joint CP-SAT solve with a",
    "no-overlap group per storey, `storeys: 2` + per-room `storey: 1|2`",
    "consumed by the loader, the kind='stair_vertical' adjacency enforcing",
    "identical stair/stairwell rectangles across floors, per-floor",
    "validate/snap/archplan post-passes, and a side-by-side composite SVG",
    "(GROUND FLOOR | SECOND FLOOR). The always-on 'stair_run_geometry'",
    "profile pins the straight run at 3.5 m (riser math the solver cannot",
    "derive); stairs are frozen during gap-snapping so the flights stay",
    "identical (design decision D6). Cross-floor wet-stack proximity is a",
    "known v2 gap \u2014 see design doc, out of scope this phase.",
    "",
    "Landing fix + gr conversion + 8x10 target (2026-07-18, later same day):",
    "  - `stair_boarding` / `stair_arrival` adjacency kinds (with the",
    "    solver's per-pair ascent decision) now guarantee boarding and",
    "    arrival at OPPOSITE ends of the run \u2014 the reference drawing had the",
    "    flight topping out against br2's wall with the upstairs hall at the",
    "    wrong end. The solver resolves it by ascending REAR-to-FRONT:",
    "    board at the flight's rear end off the hall, arrive at hall2's",
    "    front-band landing serving master / bath2 / br2.",
    "  - Ground floor converted lk -> gr: the reference's separate living +",
    "    dining could never fit the 8x10 target lot (GF hard minimums",
    "    6+6+3+1.2+1.5+2.6 = 26.3 m\u00b2 > the 24 m\u00b2 4x6 buildable), so dining",
    "    folds into one open great_room. No dining counter is possible in",
    "    this parti \u2014 great_room and kitchen never share a wall (the bath",
    "    band sits between) \u2014 so dining furniture lives in the great room.",
    "  - Verified COMPLIANT at 8x10 (canonical, 100% coverage both floors)",
    "    and 8x12 (roomier; the old canonical).",
    "",
    "Canonical layout (8 x 10 lot, 2 m setbacks, 4 x 6 buildable per floor):",
    "  GF:  Front: [great room (entry, full width)      ]",
    "       Mid:   [stair | hall | T&B                  ]",
    "       Rear:  [stair | hall | kitchen              ]  kitchen rear-pinned",
    "  2F:  Front: [master (full width)                 ]",
    "       Mid:   [stairwell | hall2 landing | T&B     ]",
    "       Rear:  [stairwell | br2                     ]",
    "Stair: straight, left wall, 0.9 m wide, >= 3.5 m run, 15 risers @ 200 mm",
    "(= PD 1096 hard max, zero margin) / 250 mm treads, ascending rear-to-",
    "front per the solver's landing decision. Stairwell rectangle identical",
    "on both floors (enforced, not just verified).",
    "",
    "Conversion audit findings against the reference spec (2026-07-18):",
    "  - Reference dining was 5.6 m\u00b2 \u2014 BELOW the 6.0 m\u00b2 dining_room hard",
    "    minimum (moot after the gr conversion, kept for the record).",
    "  - The spec claims 'fully_stacked' plumbing but its own coordinates",
    "    put the 2F bath ~1.5 m FORWARD of the GF bath (same east x-band,",
    "    zero vertical overlap; kitchen has br2 above). Converted as-drawn;",
    "    a v2 wet-stack proximity term should pull bath2 over bath1.",
    "  - The drawn GF 'Hall'+'Passage' slivers (1.08 / 1.56 m\u00b2) and 2F",
    "    'Hall'+'Hall 2' (0.90 / 1.95 m\u00b2) are each merged into ONE hallway",
    "    room per floor \u2014 separately they'd violate the 1.5 m\u00b2 hallway",
    "    minimum; merged they pass.",
    "  - No carport (reference classification carport: false) \u2014 no carport",
    "    setback element or building void is declared; briefs for this",
    "    topology should be ncp. Rear service/laundry yard kept as an",
    "    opt-in service_area element.",
    "  - Name/token note: the id's `bath` token undercounts the program",
    "    (two common baths, one per floor \u2014 `baths_ds` territory under the",
    "    locked 1s convention). Kept as-imported; renaming is a catalog",
    "    decision for the 2s naming pass.",
    "  - Gross floor area 64 m\u00b2 (~57 net) sits below the locked 2BR/2-bath",
    "    45-65/65-80 m\u00b2 bands, which were calibrated single-storey; the 2s",
    "    floor-area bands are an open convention decision.",
    "",
    "App-matcher note: surfaces in ai/match.py as a narrow 2BR/2-bath",
    "candidate and solves when selected. Its three sibling reference specs",
    "(front_stair_foyer, mid_core_ustair, rear_stair_understair) remain",
    "unconverted and list as 0-bedroom candidates that match nothing."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/2s_2br_8x12_nw_side_spine_stair_bath_hall_ncp.json

2-STOREY 2BR / 2 bath side-spine-stair with the GF HALL retained — 8x12 lot, no carport. 4x8 envelope per floor = 32 m², comfortably above the 28 m² compact-fallback threshold, so the hall variant runs as-is (no auto-switch): great room front, hall + stair spine mid-left, T&B and kitchen rear. Proves the hall variant still earns its keep on shells that can afford it.

Lot 8×12 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 4×8 m buildable envelope — 32 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot8 × 12 m (96 m²)
Buildable envelope4 × 8 m (32 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

ground-floor hall retained (shell above the compact threshold) · straight side-spine stair on the left wall · both bedrooms upstairs off the landing hall · one T&B per floor · no carport

No. 23 / 46

1s_2br_wd_front_back_split_bath_hall_ld

Verified
1s_2br_wd_front_back_split_bath_hall_ld · single-storey · Wide · 2 bed · 1 bath

Wide-shell 2BR / 1 bath, FRONT-BACK split, LD style with mid HALL notch — the entire front (street) half is a full LDK: living (left) | dining (middle) | kitchen (right), side by side. The entire rear half is private: master (left) | [hall notch in front, T&B behind] (middle) | standard (right). Master, standard, and the T&B all door into the central hall; the hall opens into the dining room. No ensuite.

Definition

1s/2br/wide/1s_2br_wd_front_back_split_bath_hall_ld.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

Created 2026-07-21 to spec: wide, 1-storey, single T&B (no ensuite), master + standard. Front-back split — the whole FRONT half is a full LDK (living | dining | kitchen, side by side), the whole REAR half is private (master | middle column | standard). Distinct from 1s_2br_wd_front_back_split_baths_ds_gr (that one is 2 baths, gr-style combined great room, and its bedrooms door straight across the band boundary with no hall). This one is single-bath, full-LDK, and uses a central HALL notch for circulation.

Layout intent (canonical form, matches solved geometry): Rear (private): [ MASTER | (hall / T&B) | STANDARD ] Front (public): [ LIVING | DINING | KITCHEN ] Middle column, front-to-rear: HALL (front, opens to dining) then T&B (rear).

The HALL is a notch in the middle column: it pokes forward to the front/rear band boundary and opens (open-plan mouth) into the dining room. master (left, full depth) and standard (right, full depth) each door into the hall from their side; the T&B sits behind the hall and doors into it from the rear. So master, standard, AND the T&B all reach the public side through the one hall — hallway is a valid bedroom-access room per the validator, which is why this works where a no-hall version failed (a bedroom can't be accessed only through the kitchen, and with the LDK split the right-side bedroom would otherwise sit above only the kitchen). match_widths pins hall width == T&B width so the middle column reads as one clean vertical band.

SOLVER REQUIREMENTS (same family as the other front-back split): (1) zone_split axis=horizontal, private_side=rear — one full-width wall divides the two bands; (2) ldk_horizontal=true — the LDK is side-by-side, not the solver's default front-to-rear stack, so its stacking rules must be disabled; (3) kitchen_rear_pin=false — kitchen is at the FRONT here, not the rear; (4) master left_anchored but NOT the public rooms crowding one edge (living is left_anchored, kitchen right_anchored — the ends only). claim_dead_strips=true because at larger sizes the ragged master/standard/middle-column widths leave interior pockets that living and the hall absorb as L-alcoves (0 dead space across the band).

FEASIBILITY: broad 0-warning band — buildable width ~7-13 m, depth >= ~5-6 m (lots ~11x10 / 12x9 up through ~17x12). Published minimum 12x10 (buildable 8x6). Upper-width limit ~buildable 13 (18x* infeasible) is the usual soft public-room aspect cap, past the 2BR floor-area band anyway. Sub-minimum lots route to the vertical-split single-bath hall topology (1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr) via fallback_topology.

PLUMBING NOTE: the T&B lands rear-middle, far from the front-right kitchen, so there's no shared wet-wall between them (unlike the vertical-split topologies where bath and kitchen share a rear wet-stack). The T&B runs independent plumbing to the rear wall. Accepted trade-off of the front-back split with a full LDK front — a real-build efficiency point, not a functional problem.

IMPORTANT deviation (shared with the other front-back split): because kitchen sits at the FRONT, the dirty kitchen + service area relocate to the kitchen-side (right) side_setback rather than a rear_setback.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ hall — master's east wall (front portion) has a door into the hall — master's only public access, via the hall. Master is the full-depth left room of the rear band.bedroom_door
  • standard ↔ hall — standard's west wall (front portion) has a door into the hall — standard's only public access. Standard is the full-depth right room of the rear band.bedroom_door
  • common ↔ hall — T&B's south (front) wall opens into the hall — the single bath's only door. T&B sits behind (north of) the hall in the middle column, so both bedrooms reach it via the hall.bath_door
  • master ↔ common — T&B's west wall touches master's east wall (rear portion) — shared wall only, no door. Acoustic buffer.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • standard ↔ common — T&B's east wall touches standard's west wall (rear portion) — shared wall only, no door.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • hall ↔ dining — hall's south wall is an open mouth into dining (no door — codebase rule: hallway<->public is always open-plan). The hall notch pokes down to the front/rear band boundary; dining is the middle public room directly in front of it. This is the sole public connection for the whole private band.open_plan
  • living ↔ dining — living and dining sit side by side in the front band — open plan, no wall (requires ldk_horizontal, since they're horizontal not stacked).open_plan
  • dining ↔ kitchen — dining and kitchen sit side by side — open pass-through. living | dining | kitchen form one continuous LDK across the front.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_wd_front_back_split_bath_hall_ld).
  • match_widths — solver forces matching widths within each group: hall, common.
  • kitchen_rear_pin: false — overrides the solver's default kitchen-rear-wall placement rule.
  • ldk_horizontal: true — disables the solver's default vertical LDK-stacking rules so great_room/kitchen can sit side-by-side.
  • fallback_topology — if the solver reports infeasibility on the brief's lot, retries with 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr.

      "id": "common",
      "type": "common_bath",
      "zone": "service",
      "size_priority": "service_and_baths"
    },
    {
      "id": "hall",
      "type": "hallway",
      "zone": "circulation",
      "size_priority": "service_and_baths"
    },
    {
      "id": "living",
      "type": "living_room",
      "zone": "public",
      "size_priority": "public_LDK",
      "hosts_entry": true
    },
    {
      "id": "dining",
      "type": "dining_room",
      "zone": "public",
      "size_priority": "public_LDK"
    },
    {
      "id": "kitchen",
      "type": "kitchen",
      "zone": "public",
      "size_priority": "public_LDK"
    }
  ],
  "adjacencies": [
    {
      "a": "master",
      "b": "hall",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 1.0,
      "kind": "bedroom_door",
      "note": "master's east wall (front portion) has a door into the hall \u2014 master's only public access, via the hall. Master is the full-depth left room of the rear band."
    },
    {
      "a": "standard",
      "b": "hall",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 1.0,
      "kind": "bedroom_door",
      "note": "standard's west wall (front portion) has a door into the hall \u2014 standard's only public access. Standard is the full-depth right room of the rear band."
    },
    {
      "a": "common",
      "b": "hall",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 1.0,
      "kind": "bath_door",
      "note": "T&B's south (front) wall opens into the hall \u2014 the single bath's only door. T&B sits behind (north of) the hall in the middle column, so both bedrooms reach it via the hall."
    },
    {
      "a": "master",
      "b": "common",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 0.8,
      "kind": "bath_to_bedroom_wall",
      "note": "T&B's west wall touches master's east wall (rear portion) \u2014 shared wall only, no door. Acoustic buffer."
    },
    {
      "a": "standard",
      "b": "common",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 0.8,
      "kind": "bath_to_bedroom_wall",
      "note": "T&B's east wall touches standard's west wall (rear portion) \u2014 shared wall only, no door."
    },
    {
      "a": "hall",
      "b": "dining",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 0.9,
      "kind": "open_plan",
      "note": "hall's south wall is an open mouth into dining (no door \u2014 codebase rule: hallway<->public is always open-plan). The hall notch pokes down to the front/rear band boundary; dining is the middle public room directly in front of it. This is the sole public connection for the whole private band."
    },
    {
      "a": "living",
      "b": "dining",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 1.5,
      "kind": "open_plan",
      "note": "living and dining sit side by side in the front band \u2014 open plan, no wall (requires ldk_horizontal, since they're horizontal not stacked)."
    },
    {
      "a": "dining",
      "b": "kitchen",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 1.2,
      "kind": "open_plan",
      "note": "dining and kitchen sit side by side \u2014 open pass-through. living | dining | kitchen form one continuous LDK across the front."
    }
  ],
  "soft_proximities": [],
  "entry_point": "living",
  "zone_split": {
    "axis": "horizontal",
    "private_side": "rear",
    "private_rooms": [
      "master",
      "standard",
      "common",
      "hall"
    ],
    "public_rooms": [
      "living",
      "dining",
      "kitchen"
    ]
  },
  "fallback_topology": "1s/2br/wide/1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr.json",
  "match_bedroom_widths": false,
  "match_widths": [
    [
      "hall",
      "common"
    ]
  ],
  "ldk_horizontal": true,
  "kitchen_rear_pin": false,
  "claim_dead_strips": true,
  "left_anchored": [
    "master",
    "living"
  ],
  "right_anchored": [
    "standard",
    "kitchen"
  ],
  "rear_anchored": [
    "master",
    "standard",
    "common"
  ],
  "front_to_rear_stacks": [
    [
      "living",
      "master"
    ],
    [
      "dining",
      "hall"
    ],
    [
      "kitchen",
      "standard"
    ],
    [
      "hall",
      "common"
    ]
  ],
  "lot_adjustment_profiles": [
    {
      "name": "compact_wd_front_back_split_bath_hall_ld",
      "when": {
        "buildable_area_lt_sqm": 90
      },
      "auto_apply": {
        "kitchen": {
          "min_least_dim_m": 1.8
        },
        "hallway": {
          "min_least_dim_m": 1.0
        },
        "common_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 3.0,
          "max_area_sqm": 4.5
        },
        "master_bedroom": {
          "max_area_sqm": 18.0
        }
      },
      "rationale": "Splitting the lot front-back halves the usable depth per band, so compact lots get tight \u2014 the private band has to fit master, the hall+T&B middle column, and standard side by side in one band of depth. auto_apply floors kitchen width and the T&B area, holds the hall at PH minimum corridor depth (1.0 m), and caps master at 18 m2 so its priority weight doesn't crowd the middle column or standard."
    }
  ],
  "setback_elements": [
    {
      "type": "dirty_kitchen",
      "location": "side_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "behind": "kitchen",
      "note": "kitchen sits at the FRONT (street) here, not the rear, so it has no rear wall to extend a service tail from \u2014 the dirty kitchen extends off kitchen's outer (right) side wall instead, same as the other front-back-split topology."
    },
    {
      "type": "service_area",
      "location": "side_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "note": "grouped with dirty_kitchen on the kitchen (right) side."
    },
    {
      "type": "lanai",
      "location": "side_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "note": "on the living (left) side, opposite the kitchen/service side."
    }
  ],
  "building_voids": [],
  "notes": [
    "Created 2026-07-21 to spec: wide, 1-storey, single T&B (no ensuite),",
    "master + standard. Front-back split \u2014 the whole FRONT half is a full LDK",
    "(living | dining | kitchen, side by side), the whole REAR half is private",
    "(master | middle column | standard). Distinct from",
    "1s_2br_wd_front_back_split_baths_ds_gr (that one is 2 baths, gr-style",
    "combined great room, and its bedrooms door straight across the band",
    "boundary with no hall). This one is single-bath, full-LDK, and uses a",
    "central HALL notch for circulation.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (canonical form, matches solved geometry):",
    "  Rear (private):  [ MASTER | (hall / T&B) | STANDARD ]",
    "  Front (public):  [ LIVING | DINING       | KITCHEN  ]",
    "  Middle column, front-to-rear: HALL (front, opens to dining) then T&B (rear).",
    "",
    "The HALL is a notch in the middle column: it pokes forward to the",
    "front/rear band boundary and opens (open-plan mouth) into the dining",
    "room. master (left, full depth) and standard (right, full depth) each",
    "door into the hall from their side; the T&B sits behind the hall and",
    "doors into it from the rear. So master, standard, AND the T&B all reach",
    "the public side through the one hall \u2014 hallway is a valid bedroom-access",
    "room per the validator, which is why this works where a no-hall version",
    "failed (a bedroom can't be accessed only through the kitchen, and with",
    "the LDK split the right-side bedroom would otherwise sit above only the",
    "kitchen). match_widths pins hall width == T&B width so the middle column",
    "reads as one clean vertical band.",
    "",
    "SOLVER REQUIREMENTS (same family as the other front-back split): (1)",
    "zone_split axis=horizontal, private_side=rear \u2014 one full-width wall",
    "divides the two bands; (2) ldk_horizontal=true \u2014 the LDK is side-by-side,",
    "not the solver's default front-to-rear stack, so its stacking rules must",
    "be disabled; (3) kitchen_rear_pin=false \u2014 kitchen is at the FRONT here,",
    "not the rear; (4) master left_anchored but NOT the public rooms crowding",
    "one edge (living is left_anchored, kitchen right_anchored \u2014 the ends",
    "only). claim_dead_strips=true because at larger sizes the ragged",
    "master/standard/middle-column widths leave interior pockets that living",
    "and the hall absorb as L-alcoves (0 dead space across the band).",
    "",
    "FEASIBILITY: broad 0-warning band \u2014 buildable width ~7-13 m, depth >= ~5-6",
    "m (lots ~11x10 / 12x9 up through ~17x12). Published minimum 12x10",
    "(buildable 8x6). Upper-width limit ~buildable 13 (18x* infeasible) is the",
    "usual soft public-room aspect cap, past the 2BR floor-area band anyway.",
    "Sub-minimum lots route to the vertical-split single-bath hall topology",
    "(1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr) via fallback_topology.",
    "",
    "PLUMBING NOTE: the T&B lands rear-middle, far from the front-right",
    "kitchen, so there's no shared wet-wall between them (unlike the",
    "vertical-split topologies where bath and kitchen share a rear wet-stack).",
    "The T&B runs independent plumbing to the rear wall. Accepted trade-off of",
    "the front-back split with a full LDK front \u2014 a real-build efficiency",
    "point, not a functional problem.",
    "",
    "IMPORTANT deviation (shared with the other front-back split): because",
    "kitchen sits at the FRONT, the dirty kitchen + service area relocate to",
    "the kitchen-side (right) side_setback rather than a rear_setback."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/1s_2br_12x10_wd_front_back_split_bath_hall_ld_ncp.json

2BR / 1 bath, FRONT-BACK split with central hall notch, full LDK front, no carport. 12x10 lot gives an 8x6 envelope = 48 m2 floor -- the published minimum (front-back split halves the depth per band, so it needs buildable depth ~5-6 m; sub-minimum lots route to the vertical-split hall sibling). The whole FRONT half is a full LDK: living (left) | dining (middle) | kitchen (right), side by side. The whole REAR half is private: master (left) | hall+T&B middle column | standard (right). Master, standard, and the single T&B all door into the central hall; the hall opens into dining. No ensuite.

Lot 12×10 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 8×6 m buildable envelope — 48 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot12 × 10 m (120 m²)
Buildable envelope8 × 6 m (48 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

one common bath (T&B) · central hallway serving both bedrooms and the T&B · front-back split: full LDK across the front, bedrooms across the rear · separate living, dining, and kitchen side by side · no carport

No. 24 / 46

1s_2br_wd_front_back_split_baths_ds_gr

Verified
1s_2br_wd_front_back_split_baths_ds_gr · single-storey · Wide · 2 bed · 2 bath

Wide-shell 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED, FRONT-BACK split, GR style — the entire front (street-facing) half is public: great_room + kitchen, open-plan (gr style, no separate dining room). The entire rear half is private, arranged as ONE row: master, ensuite, common bath, and standard bedroom side by side (in that order), each dooring directly across the public/private boundary wall except ensuite (master's exclusive, no public door) and common (which shares its wet wall with ensuite instead). No hallway.

Definition

1s/2br/wide/1s_2br_wd_front_back_split_baths_ds_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

Wide-shell catalog topology — 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED, FRONT-BACK split, GR style. Every other topology in the catalog splits the lot vertically (bedrooms on one side, LDK on the other, both running front-to-rear). This one splits it horizontally instead: the entire front half of the house is public, the entire rear half is private, and one wall spanning the full width separates them.

Revision history: v1 (2026-07-13) used a plain living_room instead of great_room and was squarish-only (misfiled under wide/) — infeasible everywhere due to a mathematical contradiction between zone_split and the solver's hardcoded kitchen-rear-wall pin. v2 (2026-07-14) switched to great_room + kitchen (gr style) and corrected target_shell to wide. v3 (2026-07-14, WORKING): three solver-level fixes made this topology solve for real, verified end-to-end (14x11, COMPLIANT, score 23.50, 0 warnings, no fallback): (1) the kitchen-rear pin is now skipped when zone_split explicitly declares kitchen as a front/public room; (2) a new topology.ldk_horizontal flag (set true below) disables the solver's hardcoded LDK vertical-stacking rules, which otherwise force kitchen+great_room to stack front-to-rear and directly contradict a horizontal (side-by-side) LDK; (3) great_room was removed from left_anchored (only master stays) — forcing BOTH master and great_room onto the exact same left edge locked great_room into a rigid stacked relationship with master that made it geometrically impossible to also reach standard's adjacency on the far right, regardless of lot size (confirmed infeasible up to 34x34 m before this fix). Full diagnostic trail in project memory: front-back-split-topology-solver-bug.

Layout intent (canonical form — brief.carport_side='left'), matches the actual solved geometry: Rear (private, one row): [master | ensuite | common | standard] Front (public, side-by-side): [great_room (hosts entry) | kitchen] One wall spans the FULL WIDTH, dividing the two bands.

No hallway. Master and standard each door directly across the front/rear boundary wall into great_room. Ensuite is master's exclusive (wet_core with common, door only from master). Common's only door faces great_room — same circulation idiom as 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr's non-hall siblings: both bedrooms reach the bath by stepping into the shared public room and back in.

Wet stack: ensuite and common share a plumbing wall (wet_core) in the middle of the row.

IMPORTANT deviation from the rest of the catalog: because kitchen sits at the FRONT of the house (street side) rather than the rear, the dirty kitchen and service area can't extend into a rear_setback the way they do in every vertical-split topology — there's no rear wall behind kitchen to extend from. They're relocated to the side_setback on kitchen's side instead. Carport is pushed to the opposite side (great room's side) to avoid crowding that same setback strip.

Floor band: sized against a 14x11 m lot (envelope 10x7 = 70 sqm, ratio 1.43, genuinely wide).

When brief.carport_side='right', the runner mirrors the topology: great_room/carport move right, kitchen/service move left.

Fallback: 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr — drops to the standard vertical-split single-bath hall topology on lots too shallow to support two full-width bands.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is master's exclusive — door on master's EAST wall. Ensuite and common cluster together in the middle of the row, between master and standard.ensuite_door
  • ensuite ↔ common — ensuite and common share a plumbing wall — the two-bath cluster in the middle of the rear row, between master and standard.wet_core
  • common ↔ standard — common bath's east wall = standard's west wall — shared wall only, no door (bedroom<->common-bath is always wall-only per the codebase's generic rule). Acoustic buffer between the bath cluster and standard.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • master ↔ great — master's south wall = great_room's north wall at the front/rear boundary — a real door, no hall. Master reaches the rest of the house only through this one door.bedroom_to_public
  • standard ↔ great — standard's south wall overlaps great_room's north wall — direct door. 1.2 m floor gives 0.8 m clear door + 0.15 m frame each side.bedroom_to_public
  • common ↔ great — common bath's only door — south wall opens into great_room. With ensuite (wet_core, no door) on one side and standard (wall-only) on the other, this is common's sole access, same idiom as the squarish side_split_bath_gr family: both bedrooms reach the bath by stepping out through their own door into the public band and back in.bath_door
  • great ↔ kitchen — great_room + kitchen form one open LDK (gr style) — consistent with every other wide-shell topology in the catalog. The solver's global LDK rule forces kitchen+great_room to stack front-to-rear (never side-by-side); the front_to_rear_stacks hint below pins great_room in front, kitchen behind.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_wd_front_back_split_baths_ds_gr).
  • ldk_horizontal: true — disables the solver's default vertical LDK-stacking rules so great_room/kitchen can sit side-by-side.
  • fallback_topology — if the solver reports infeasibility on the brief's lot, retries with 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr.

      "preferred_apply": {
        "ensuite_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 3.0
        },
        "kitchen": {
          "min_least_dim_m": 2.0
        }
      },
      "rationale": "Splitting the lot front-back halves the usable depth per band compared to the vertical-split topologies, so compact lots get tight fast \u2014 the whole private band has to fit master, ensuite, common, and standard side by side in one row of depth. auto_apply floors kitchen width and both bath areas so neither collapses to its hard minimum by default; master is capped at 18 sqm so its priority weight doesn't crowd the bath cluster or standard sharing its row. preferred_apply bumps ensuite and kitchen further where the envelope allows."
    }
  ],
  "setback_elements": [
    {
      "type": "carport",
      "location": "side_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "width_m": 3.0,
      "depth_m": 6.0,
      "note": "placed on the GREAT ROOM side (left), not the kitchen side \u2014 deviates from the catalog default of pairing carport with kitchen's side, because the dirty kitchen already claims the kitchen-side setback here (kitchen is at the front, not the rear, so it can't push its service tail into a rear setback like every other topology does)."
    },
    {
      "type": "dirty_kitchen",
      "location": "side_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "behind": "kitchen",
      "note": "moved from rear_setback to side_setback (kitchen-side/right) \u2014 a direct consequence of the front-back flip. Kitchen sits in the front band near the street in this topology, so it has no rear wall to extend a service tail from; the dirty kitchen instead extends off kitchen's outer side wall."
    },
    {
      "type": "service_area",
      "location": "side_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "note": "grouped with dirty_kitchen on the kitchen side for the same reason."
    },
    {
      "type": "lanai",
      "location": "side_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "note": "on the great room side, opposite the kitchen/service side."
    }
  ],
  "building_voids": [
    {
      "id": "carport_cut",
      "location": "front_left",
      "width_m": 1.0,
      "depth_m": 4.0,
      "consumed_by": "carport"
    }
  ],
  "notes": [
    "Wide-shell catalog topology \u2014 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED, FRONT-BACK split, GR style. Every other topology in the catalog splits the lot vertically (bedrooms on one side, LDK on the other, both running front-to-rear). This one splits it horizontally instead: the entire front half of the house is public, the entire rear half is private, and one wall spanning the full width separates them.",
    "",
    "Revision history: v1 (2026-07-13) used a plain living_room instead of great_room and was squarish-only (misfiled under wide/) \u2014 infeasible everywhere due to a mathematical contradiction between zone_split and the solver's hardcoded kitchen-rear-wall pin. v2 (2026-07-14) switched to great_room + kitchen (gr style) and corrected target_shell to wide. v3 (2026-07-14, WORKING): three solver-level fixes made this topology solve for real, verified end-to-end (14x11, COMPLIANT, score 23.50, 0 warnings, no fallback): (1) the kitchen-rear pin is now skipped when zone_split explicitly declares kitchen as a front/public room; (2) a new topology.ldk_horizontal flag (set true below) disables the solver's hardcoded LDK vertical-stacking rules, which otherwise force kitchen+great_room to stack front-to-rear and directly contradict a horizontal (side-by-side) LDK; (3) great_room was removed from left_anchored (only master stays) \u2014 forcing BOTH master and great_room onto the exact same left edge locked great_room into a rigid stacked relationship with master that made it geometrically impossible to also reach standard's adjacency on the far right, regardless of lot size (confirmed infeasible up to 34x34 m before this fix). Full diagnostic trail in project memory: front-back-split-topology-solver-bug.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (canonical form \u2014 brief.carport_side='left'), matches the actual solved geometry:",
    "  Rear (private, one row): [master | ensuite | common | standard]",
    "  Front (public, side-by-side): [great_room (hosts entry) | kitchen]",
    "  One wall spans the FULL WIDTH, dividing the two bands.",
    "",
    "No hallway. Master and standard each door directly across the front/rear boundary wall into great_room. Ensuite is master's exclusive (wet_core with common, door only from master). Common's only door faces great_room \u2014 same circulation idiom as 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr's non-hall siblings: both bedrooms reach the bath by stepping into the shared public room and back in.",
    "",
    "Wet stack: ensuite and common share a plumbing wall (wet_core) in the middle of the row.",
    "",
    "IMPORTANT deviation from the rest of the catalog: because kitchen sits at the FRONT of the house (street side) rather than the rear, the dirty kitchen and service area can't extend into a rear_setback the way they do in every vertical-split topology \u2014 there's no rear wall behind kitchen to extend from. They're relocated to the side_setback on kitchen's side instead. Carport is pushed to the opposite side (great room's side) to avoid crowding that same setback strip.",
    "",
    "Floor band: sized against a 14x11 m lot (envelope 10x7 = 70 sqm, ratio 1.43, genuinely wide).",
    "",
    "When brief.carport_side='right', the runner mirrors the topology: great_room/carport move right, kitchen/service move left.",
    "",
    "Fallback: 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr \u2014 drops to the standard vertical-split single-bath hall topology on lots too shallow to support two full-width bands."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/1s_2br_14x10_wd_front_back_split_baths_ds_gr_ncp.json

Wide-shell 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED, genuine FRONT-BACK split, GR style — 14x10 lot, NO CARPORT. Envelope 10x6 = 60 m² floor. One wall spans the full width, dividing the front public band (great_room + kitchen, side-by-side) from the rear private row (master | ensuite | common | standard, baths clustered in the middle). Solves via ldk_horizontal=true (relaxes the solver's default LDK vertical-stacking rules) and master-only left anchoring (great_room is NOT left-anchored, letting it span freely to reach every private-row room it must share a wall with). Dirty kitchen enabled: sits in the side setback adjacent to kitchen (kitchen has no rear wall in this topology, so it can't be placed behind kitchen like every other topology), with a service door from kitchen out to it.

Lot 14×10 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 10×6 m buildable envelope — 60 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot14 × 10 m (140 m²)
Buildable envelope10 × 6 m (60 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

one wall spans the full width dividing public front from private rear · great_room and kitchen sit side-by-side across the front, not stacked · master and standard each door directly into the great room, no hall · ensuite and common bath clustered together in the middle of the private row · dirty kitchen in the side setback adjacent to kitchen, with a door from kitchen · no carport

No. 25 / 46

1s_2br_wd_quadrant_split_baths_ds_ld

Verified
1s_2br_wd_quadrant_split_baths_ds_ld · single-storey · Wide · 2 bed · 2 bath

Wide-shell 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, QUADRANT split, LD style — front band: living (left) + dining (middle) + standard bedroom (right). Rear band: master + ensuite (left), hall + common T&B stacked (hall in front, T&B behind it) in the middle, kitchen (right). Hall is the circulation hinge for the T&B and kitchen: open to kitchen and to dining in front of it, and hosts the common T&B's door. Master doors into living (its south wall, a corner swing), not the hall. Neither a clean vertical split nor a horizontal split — private and public occupy diagonal corners instead.

Definition

1s/2br/wide/1s_2br_wd_quadrant_split_baths_ds_ld.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

Wide-shell catalog topology — 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, QUADRANT split, LD style.

2026-07-21: great_room split back into separate LIVING + DINING rooms — finally matching this topology's '_ld' name (it had shipped a great_room since the 2026-07-14 revision, a name/implementation mismatch). The original living+dining version (pre-2026-07-14) was infeasible even at 34x34 m; two things unlock the split now: (1) ldk_horizontal: true disables the solver's hardcoded LDK vertical-stacking rules (living must sit in front of dining+kitchen), which contradicted the side-by-side living/dining across the front; (2) kitchen is kept NON-adjacent to dining (reached only via the hall, as the great_room version already did), avoiding the 3-way mutual adjacency (hall/kitchen/dining) that was the original blocker. Verified 0-warning across ~13x10 to 16x12; the oversized-envelope failure (25x18+) is now just the ordinary non-monotonic upper bound, not the total infeasibility the original hit. claim_dead_strips: true absorbs the interior pockets that ragged widths leave at the larger end of the band into L-alcoves.

Layout intent (canonical form, street/front at the bottom): Front band (bottom): [living (hosts entry) | dining | standard ] Rear band (top): [master + ensuite | hall / common T&B (stacked) | kitchen ]

living spans the front-left (in front of master); dining spans the front-middle (in front of the hall/common column); standard spans the front-right (in front of kitchen only). living + dining are open-plan to each other across the front.

Circulation: hall is the hinge between the T&B, the kitchen, and the public wing. dining -> hall (open mouth, no door) -> common T&B (door). hall also opens directly onto kitchen (open-plan) — kitchen's only route to/from the rest of the house besides being visually open to it. kitchen has no direct adjacency to living or dining; you reach it via hall. Master does NOT door into the hall (2026-07-22 change) — its door is on its south wall into living instead (a corner swing; see below).

standard's only door is to dining. standard<->kitchen is geometry-only (wall, no door) — deliberately no direct bedroom-to-kitchen opening.

zone_split is null here (unlike the vertical- and horizontal-split topologies) — the private/public boundary isn't a single axis, so per-room 'zone' tags carry the classification instead.

Confirmed target_shell: wide — the sketch's ~10x7 m footprint (1.43:1) matches the wide-shell catalog's proportions much more closely than squarish.

Floor band: roughly 65-85 sqm buildable, sized against the sketch's ~70 sqm footprint (10 x 7 m building on a 14x11 buildable envelope).

No fallback declared.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is master's exclusive — carved from master's own footprint via the alcove claim. Door from master only, no other access.ensuite_door
  • ensuite ↔ common — ensuite and common bath (T&B) share a plumbing wall, consolidating both wet stacks in the rear-middle band. No door — ensuite stays master-exclusive per the locked catalog convention.wet_core
  • master ↔ hall — master's east wall = hall's west wall — geometry-only wall now, NO door (was master's door before 2026-07-22). The master<->hall shared segment sits mid-wall on master's much-wider east wall, so a door there can't swing to a corner (no perpendicular wall on master's side); master's door moved to living instead, which always touches master's south-wall corners. Master still reaches the hall visually — hall is open to dining, which master opens onto.bedroom_to_public_wall
  • master ↔ living — master's door — opens south into living, hinged at master's SE corner (door_placement=high_corner) so the panel swings flat against the EAST wall (solid vs dining below y=5): a corner swing at the SE corner, next to the dining/hall junction. Living is directly in front of master (via the [living,master] stack) and ALWAYS shares master's full south wall with real corners at every lot size, unlike master<->dining/hall on the east wall, which only touch at some sizes and can't give a robust SE-corner swing (at compact lots the hall widens between master and dining). Replaced the old master<->hall door (2026-07-22). Living is public (valid bedroom access per the validator's ACCESS_FROM set), and it's one continuous open LDK with dining/kitchen anyway.bedroom_door
  • common ↔ hall — T&B's door opens into the hall — matches the catalog's standard 'common bath is hall/public-accessible, not routed through a bedroom' rule.bath_door
  • hall ↔ dining — hall's front wall is an open mouth into dining — codebase rule: hallway↔public is always open-plan, no door. Dining is the middle public room, directly in front of the hall (it took over the mouth role the merged great_room used to play). This is hall's (and by extension master's and common's) connection to the public wing.open_plan
  • kitchen ↔ hall — kitchen's west wall opens onto the hall (open-plan, no door) — hall serves as kitchen's entrance from the private/circulation side. Kitchen is NOT adjacent to dining or living: it reaches the public wing only via the hall. Keeping kitchen and dining non-adjacent is deliberate — it avoids the 3-way mutual adjacency (hall/kitchen/dining) that made the pre-2026-07-14 living+dining version infeasible.open_plan
  • standard ↔ kitchen — standard's rear wall = kitchen's front wall — geometry-only, NO door. Keeps the building outline a clean rectangle at this seam, but deliberately does not give a bedroom a direct opening onto the kitchen work area; standard's actual door is to dining instead.bedroom_to_public_wall
  • standard ↔ dining — standard's only door — direct into dining (the front-middle public room, immediately west of standard). 1.1 m floor = 0.8 m clear panel + frame + a little leeway. Took over the door role standard<->great_room played before the split.bedroom_to_public
  • living ↔ dining — living and dining sit side by side across the front (living left, dining middle) — open plan, no wall. Side-by-side (not stacked front-to-rear) requires ldk_horizontal: true.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_wd_quadrant_split_baths_ds_ld).
  • ldk_horizontal: true — disables the solver's default vertical LDK-stacking rules so great_room/kitchen can sit side-by-side.
  • ensuite_alcove_joins_master — the strip beside a narrower ensuite is folded into master as an L-shaped alcove.
1s_2br_wd_quadrant_split_baths_ds_ld.json 285 lines · 11.7 KB

    "Wide-shell catalog topology \u2014 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, QUADRANT split, LD style.",
    "",
    "2026-07-21: great_room split back into separate LIVING + DINING rooms \u2014",
    "finally matching this topology's '_ld' name (it had shipped a great_room",
    "since the 2026-07-14 revision, a name/implementation mismatch). The",
    "original living+dining version (pre-2026-07-14) was infeasible even at",
    "34x34 m; two things unlock the split now: (1) ldk_horizontal: true",
    "disables the solver's hardcoded LDK vertical-stacking rules (living must",
    "sit in front of dining+kitchen), which contradicted the side-by-side",
    "living/dining across the front; (2) kitchen is kept NON-adjacent to",
    "dining (reached only via the hall, as the great_room version already",
    "did), avoiding the 3-way mutual adjacency (hall/kitchen/dining) that was",
    "the original blocker. Verified 0-warning across ~13x10 to 16x12; the",
    "oversized-envelope failure (25x18+) is now just the ordinary",
    "non-monotonic upper bound, not the total infeasibility the original hit.",
    "claim_dead_strips: true absorbs the interior pockets that ragged widths",
    "leave at the larger end of the band into L-alcoves.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (canonical form, street/front at the bottom):",
    "  Front band (bottom): [living (hosts entry) | dining | standard ]",
    "  Rear band  (top):    [master + ensuite | hall / common T&B (stacked) | kitchen ]",
    "",
    "living spans the front-left (in front of master); dining spans the",
    "front-middle (in front of the hall/common column); standard spans the",
    "front-right (in front of kitchen only). living + dining are open-plan",
    "to each other across the front.",
    "",
    "Circulation: hall is the hinge between the T&B, the kitchen, and the",
    "public wing. dining -> hall (open mouth, no door) -> common T&B (door).",
    "hall also opens directly onto kitchen (open-plan) \u2014 kitchen's only route",
    "to/from the rest of the house besides being visually open to it. kitchen",
    "has no direct adjacency to living or dining; you reach it via hall. Master",
    "does NOT door into the hall (2026-07-22 change) \u2014 its door is on its south",
    "wall into living instead (a corner swing; see below).",
    "",
    "standard's only door is to dining. standard<->kitchen is geometry-only",
    "(wall, no door) \u2014 deliberately no direct bedroom-to-kitchen opening.",
    "",
    "zone_split is null here (unlike the vertical- and horizontal-split",
    "topologies) \u2014 the private/public boundary isn't a single axis, so",
    "per-room 'zone' tags carry the classification instead.",
    "",
    "Confirmed target_shell: wide \u2014 the sketch's ~10x7 m footprint (1.43:1)",
    "matches the wide-shell catalog's proportions much more closely than",
    "squarish.",
    "",
    "Floor band: roughly 65-85 sqm buildable, sized against the sketch's ~70",
    "sqm footprint (10 x 7 m building on a 14x11 buildable envelope).",
    "",
    "No fallback declared."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/1s_2br_12x10_wd_quadrant_split_baths_ds_ld_ncp.json

2BR / 2 baths distributed, QUADRANT split, LD style, no carport. 12x10 lot gives an 8x6 envelope = 48 m2 floor -- the clean 0-warning minimum for this topology (12x9 also solves but drops the preferred bath/kitchen sizes; 11x9 is infeasible). Private and public occupy diagonal corners: front band living (left) | dining (middle) | standard (right); rear band master+ensuite (left) | hall+T&B stacked (middle) | kitchen (right). Hall is the circulation hinge -- dining opens into it, master and T&B door into it, kitchen is open-plan to it. Living + dining are a real split (not a combined great room).

Lot 12×10 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 8×6 m buildable envelope — 48 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot12 × 10 m (120 m²)
Buildable envelope8 × 6 m (48 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

master ensuite · one common bath (T&B) accessible from the hall · central hall hinge serving master, T&B, and the kitchen · separate living and dining rooms (side by side across the front) · no carport

Private and public occupy diagonal corners: front band living (left) | dining (middle) | standard (right); rear band master+ensuite (left) | hall+T&B stacked (middle) | kitchen (right). No. 26 / 46

1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr

Verified
1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr · single-storey · Wide · 2 bed · 1 bath

Wide-shell 2BR / 1 bath — side-split with HALL in middle band and combined GREAT ROOM (gr style, living+dining merged). Bedrooms stack front-to-rear on the LEFT (master front, standard rear). Hall sits in the middle band south of common bath; both bedrooms door into the hall; common bath doors into the hall; hall opens east into the great_room. Great_room + kitchen form the LDK column on the right.

Definition

1s/2br/wide/1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

Wide-shell 2BR / 1 bath topology with mid-band HALL and combined GREAT ROOM (gr style). Replaces the older 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_gr (no hall — standard had to door directly into great_room, awkward access) and 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_gr (removed) (different bath position, also no hall).

Floor band: 50-70 sqm buildable. Designed against 14x10 m (60 sqm) as the canonical compact case.

Layout intent (canonical form — brief.carport_side='right' or 'none'): Rear band: [STANDARD | COMMON T&B | KITCHEN ] Middle band: [(master cont.) | HALL | (great cont.) ] Front band: [MASTER BEDROOM | GREAT ROOM ]

Three sub-columns: - LEFT (~4 m): [master front, hall middle, standard rear] — bedrooms stack with hall threading between them on the south side of common bath - MID (~2 m): common T&B at the rear, north of hall; standard.east = common.west wall (no door) - RIGHT (~4 m): [great_room front, kitchen rear] — the LDK column

Hall is a 4-walled circulation node: - NORTH: door to common T&B (bath's only door) - SOUTH: door to master bedroom (master's primary public access) - WEST: wall to master/standard column (no public connection on this side) - EAST: open mouth into great_room (public LDK connection) Additionally hall.north overlaps with standard.south at the bath block's east end, hosting standard's bedroom door into the hall.

Single-bath access flow: - Master: bedroom door north (or east?) into hall; hall opens east to great - Standard: bedroom door south into hall (standard sits north of hall) - Bath: south door into hall; both bedrooms reach it via the hall without transiting each other or the bath

LDK arrangement (gr style): - Living + Dining combined into one open great_room - Kitchen stacked behind great_room in the right column - Hall opens east into the public space (great_room) - Total LDK floor: great ~18-22 sqm + kitchen ~6-8 sqm

Wet-stack: common T&B shares the wet wall with kitchen (common.east = kitchen.west, 1.0 m wet_core). Combined with the dirty kitchen in the rear setback, all wet utilities land in one rear bay along the rear elevation.

Carport: not declared in the base topology — NCP form. To add a side carport, add a building_void at front_right. For front-parallel carport, brief.carport_side='front' bumps the front setback to 3 m. When brief.carport_side='left', the runner mirrors the topology so LDK ends up on the left.

No fallback declared. This IS the simplest single-bath wide-shell topology now — earlier fallbacks targeted the deprecated _side_split_gr.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ standard — master (front) and standard (rear) stack vertically in the left column — shared horizontal wall only, no door between them.bedroom_to_bedroom_wall
  • master ↔ hall — master's east wall has a door opening east into the hall — master's primary public access. The hall provides a buffer between master and the great_room (vs. master dooring directly into great).bedroom_door
  • standard ↔ hall — standard's south wall opens into the hall — standard's only public access. Standard is north of the hall in the left column.bedroom_door
  • standard ↔ common — standard's east wall touches common's west wall at the rear — shared wall only, no door. Bath block sits between standard (west) and the LDK column (east) at the rear.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • common ↔ hall — common bath's south wall opens south into the hall — the single bath's only door. Both bedrooms reach the bath via the hall. DEFAULT host of the 'common_access' door-host group.bath_door
  • common ↔ kitchen — common bath shares plumbing wall with kitchen. ALTERNATE host of the 'common_access' group: door placed here only when shared wall >= 1.5 m (clear 0.80 + frame 0.20 + guard 0.5); falls back to hall on compact lots.wet_core
  • hall ↔ great — hall's east wall is an open mouth into the great_room (no door — codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open-plan). 0.9 m mouth — PH minimum for single-person flow; the compact 14x10 envelope can't accommodate a wider mouth without breaking the y-budget.open_plan
  • great ↔ kitchen — great_room + kitchen form the open LDK (gr style) stacked front-to-rear in the right column.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_w_hall_gr_single_bath).
  • door_host_group — at least one door's host wall is chosen by the solver's auto-scorer (or overridden per-brief via door_host), not fixed in the topology.
1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr.json 230 lines · 8.6 KB

Test brief

test/1s_2br_11x9_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr_ncp.json

2BR / 1 bath, mid-band hall, combined great room, no carport. 11x9 lot gives a 7x5 envelope = 35 m2 floor -- the shallow-depth end of the wide single-bath hall program. The ld sibling (1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_ld) needs buildable depth >= 6 m for its living/dining/kitchen split and can't reach depth 5, so this gr (combined great_room + kitchen, 2-deep public stack) is the topology to use here. Single common bath at the rear next to kitchen; a hallway is tucked into the middle band -- both bedrooms and the common bath door onto the hall; hall opens east into the great room.

Lot 11×9 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 7×5 m buildable envelope — 35 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot11 × 9 m (99 m²)
Buildable envelope7 × 5 m (35 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

one common bath · common bath accessible from the hall · mid-band hallway serving both bedrooms and the common bath · open-plan great room and kitchen · no carport

1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_ld

Verified
1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_ld · single-storey · Wide · 2 bed · 1 bath

Wide-shell 2BR / 1 bath, LD style — side-split with HALL in middle band; public column is a separate living/dining/kitchen stacked front-to-rear (not a combined great room). Bedrooms stack front-to-rear on the LEFT (master front, standard rear); hall sits in the middle band south of common bath; both bedrooms and the common bath door into the hall; hall opens east into DINING (the middle-row public room).

Definition

1s/2br/wide/1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_ld.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

Created 2026-07-21 as an LDK split (living/dining/kitchen stacked front-to-rear, the solver's default LDK arrangement) of the combined- great-room 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr. This is a SIBLING, not a replacement: the gr version is kept (it is the fallback target for three other wide topologies AND it reaches shallower lots this ld version can't — see the depth floor below).

Only ONE adjacency changed role: hall's open-mouth connection moves from great_room to dining. The hall sits in the middle band, so its wall has to align with the middle-row public room (dining, between living-front and kitchen-rear). living gets hosts_entry instead of great (front-most public room, same role). Everything else (private column, hall structure, single common bath, wet-stack, lot_adjustment_profile) is unchanged.

Layout intent (canonical form — brief.carport_side='right' or 'none'): Rear band: [STANDARD | COMMON T&B | KITCHEN ] Middle band: [(master cont.) | HALL | DINING ] Front band: [MASTER BEDROOM | LIVING ]

FEASIBILITY — a broad, well-behaved 0-warning band, notably better than the clustered-bath wide ld sibling (1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_ld, which is narrow-band). Reason: single-bath, no ensuite / no clustered bath block / no match_bedroom_widths / no match_bath_widths, so the private side has far fewer competing width constraints and master's full-width front band absorbs slack. Verified feasible (0 warnings) across buildable width 7-12 m and buildable depth >= 6 m — lots roughly 11x10 up through 16x12.

DEPTH FLOOR = buildable depth 6 m (lot depth 10). Below that the living/dining/kitchen 3-room stack can't fit (14x9.5 = depth 5.5 and all depth-5 lots are infeasible, confirmed by sweep) — the same LDK-stack depth constraint documented on cl_ld and the ds_gr LDK investigation. Published minimum: 12x10 (buildable 8x6). The gr sibling covers the shallower depth-5 lots (11x9 up); a sub-depth-6 brief on this topology routes there automatically via fallback_topology (general infeasibility- triggered fallback, NOT a fallback_below_buildable_sqm area gate — the binding constraint is DEPTH, not area, so a wide-but-shallow lot must reach the solve attempt and fail rather than be gated on area).

UPPER-WIDTH LIMIT ~ buildable width 12 (17x12 = buildable 13x8 is infeasible). This is SOFT, not structural: it's the public-room aspect cap kicking in as the LDK column widens (loosening the aspect override makes 17x12 solve). Those sizes are past the 2BR floor-area band anyway, so the cap is left in place.

claim_dead_strips: true. At 12x10 the layout tiles clean on its own, but at larger sizes (14x10+) master's floating front-band width leaves small unclaimed interior pockets (up to ~2.8 m2 at 15x11). The flag runs the dead-strip claimer on the single-storey realize so living absorbs those pockets as L-alcoves — 0 dead space across the band, still 0 warnings. Realized-geometry cleanup only; never affects feasibility.

Carport / wet-stack / mirroring all unchanged from the gr sibling.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ standard — master (front) and standard (rear) stack vertically in the left column — shared horizontal wall only, no door. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bedroom_to_bedroom_wall
  • master ↔ hall — master's east wall has a door opening east into the hall — master's primary public access; the hall buffers master from the public side. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bedroom_door
  • standard ↔ hall — standard's south wall opens into the hall — standard's only public access. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bedroom_door
  • standard ↔ common — standard's east wall touches common's west wall at the rear — shared wall only, no door. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • common ↔ hall — common bath's south wall opens south into the hall — the single bath's only door; both bedrooms reach it via the hall. DEFAULT host of the 'common_access' group. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bath_door
  • common ↔ kitchen — common bath shares plumbing wall with kitchen. ALTERNATE host of the 'common_access' group. Unchanged from the gr sibling.wet_core
  • hall ↔ dining — hall's east wall is an open mouth into dining, not living — the hall sits in the MIDDLE band, so its wall aligns with whichever public room occupies that same middle row of the living/dining/kitchen stack (dining). Takes over the role hall<->great played in the gr sibling. 0.9 m mouth = PH single-person minimum on the compact envelope.open_plan
  • living ↔ dining — living and dining stack front-to-rear — open mouth, no wall (ld style).open_plan
  • dining ↔ kitchen — dining and kitchen stack front-to-rear — open pass-through, matches the solver's default kitchen+dining-must-stack rule directly.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_w_hall_ld_single_bath).
  • door_host_group — at least one door's host wall is chosen by the solver's auto-scorer (or overridden per-brief via door_host), not fixed in the topology.
  • fallback_topology — if the solver reports infeasibility on the brief's lot, retries with 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr.

Test brief

test/1s_2br_12x10_wd_side_split_bath_hall_ld_ncp.json

2BR / 1 bath, mid-band hall, LDK smoke test, no carport. 12x10 lot gives an 8x6 envelope = 48 m2 floor -- the published minimum for this topology (its living/dining/kitchen stack needs buildable depth >= 6 m; shallower lots route to the gr sibling). Single common bath at the rear next to kitchen; a hallway is tucked into the middle band -- both bedrooms and the common bath door onto the hall, never directly onto the public side. Public side is a real living/dining/kitchen split stacked front-to-rear -- the hall's open mouth connects to dining (the middle-row public room), not living.

Lot 12×10 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 8×6 m buildable envelope — 48 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot12 × 10 m (120 m²)
Buildable envelope8 × 6 m (48 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

one common bath · common bath accessible from the hall · mid-band hallway serving both bedrooms and the common bath · separate living, dining, and kitchen rooms stacked front-to-rear · no carport

1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_gr

Verified
1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_gr · single-storey · Wide · 2 bed · 2 bath

Wide-shell 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED REAR with mid-band HALL — side-split: bedrooms in left column organised as two parallel front-to-rear sub-columns, LDK in right column (great_room front, kitchen rear). Left sub-column: [master, ensuite]. Mid sub-column: [standard, hall, common T&B]. Both baths cluster at the rear sharing a wet-stack plumbing wall with the kitchen. Compact-lot sibling of 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_ld -- use this gr (combined great_room) form down to ~5 m buildable depth; switch to the ld (living/dining split) form once buildable depth reaches ~10 m lot depth (6 m envelope) or more.

Definition

1s/2br/wide/1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

RESTORED 2026-07-20 as the compact-lot sibling of 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_ld (deleted the same day, then restored the same day once testing showed the LDK split genuinely can't reach shallow-depth lots: its living/dining/kitchen stack needs at least ~5.5 m buildable depth, confirmed via isolated-column bisection). This gr (combined great_room, 2-deep public stack: great+kitchen) is the one to use down to ~5 m buildable depth -- verified feasible at 11x9 (buildable 7x5). cl_ld's fallback_topology now points here (was dangling at a file deleted 2026-06-25, fixed the same day this topology was restored). No fallback_below_buildable_sqm gate set -- the general infeasibility-triggered fallback (not the skip-straight-to-fallback area gate) is deliberately what routes shallow-depth briefs here, since the actual binding constraint is DEPTH specifically, not buildable AREA (confirmed: widening a shallow lot does NOT unlock cl_ld -- 12x9, 13x9, and 15x9 are all still infeasible on cl_ld despite far more area than the 12x10 minimum that does work -- so an area-based skip- ahead gate would misroute a wide-but-shallow lot).

Wide-shell catalog topology — 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED at the REAR. First wide-shell with rear-clustered baths AND a hallway-served bedroom wing. Distinct from 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr (single rear-bath, no ensuite, hall-served) and its own since-restored ds_gr-style siblings (mid-band common bath, no ensuite, no hall).

Floor band: this topology now covers the shallow/compact end (~50-60 sqm buildable, 11x9 through roughly 14x10) -- cl_ld picks up 12x10 and deeper. Originally designed against 14x10 m as its own low edge before cl_ld existed; both topologies solve there, so either is a valid choice at that size. Larger lots (14x12, 15x11, 16x11) let master and bedrooms breathe toward the preferred high.

Layout intent (CANONICAL form — kitchen on RIGHT, bedrooms on LEFT, matches squarish catalog convention; brief.carport_side='right' or 'none'): Rear band (1.5 m deep): [ENSUITE | COMMON T&B | KITCHEN ] Middle band (1.5 m deep): [(master | HALL | (great cont.) ] Front band (3-4 m deep): [ MASTER | STANDARD | GREAT ROOM ]

Three vertical sub-columns (canonical, kitchen-side right): - LEFT (3 m wide): [master front+middle, ensuite rear] - MID (3 m wide): [standard front, hall middle, common T&B rear] - RIGHT (4 m wide): [great_room front, kitchen rear] — the LDK stack

Hall is a 4-walled circulation node: - NORTH (rear): door to common T&B (bath's only door) - SOUTH (front): door to standard bedroom (standard's only door) - EAST: open mouth into great_room (the public connection — open-plan) - WEST: door to master bedroom (master's only public door)

Master ensuite is master's exclusive (door on master's north/rear wall into ensuite). Master accesses public space ONLY via the hall — there is no direct master↔great door. This adds a small acoustic buffer between the master and the LDK, and keeps the master's west+south+north walls available for closets, windows, and the ensuite door.

Wet-stack cluster: ensuite's east = common's west (wet_core 1.2 m), and common's east = kitchen's west (wet_core 1.0 m). All three rooms (ensuite, common, kitchen) sit on the rear band, sharing one continuous plumbing chase along the rear elevation. The dirty kitchen in the rear setback completes the wet-utility row.

Bedroom widths: match_bedroom_widths=true forces master.width = standard.width. Bath widths: match_bath_widths=true forces common.width = ensuite.width. Combined with the left-anchor on master/ensuite (left wall) and the right-anchor on great/kitchen (right wall), this naturally tiles the bedroom wing into two 3 m sub-columns when the buildable width allows it.

Hall depth: lot_adjustment_profile floors the hallway at 1.4 m on compact lots so the hall↔great open mouth (which equals great's depth that extends into the hall's y-range) stays at 1.0 m minimum.

Carport: not declared in the base topology — this is the NCP form. To add a left-side carport (mirrors the canonical), set brief.carport_side='left' — the runner mirrors the topology and LDK appears on the left. For a right-side carport, add a building_void at rear_right but be aware the rear-right corner is the kitchen+dirty-kitchen wet stack so a right-side carport doesn't fit naturally here. For a front-parallel carport, brief.carport_side='front' bumps the front setback to 3 m.

Fallback: on shells too tight for the rear-bath cluster (envelope < ~55 sqm buildable or ratio outside 1.30-1.85), the runner falls back to 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_gr (single common bath, no ensuite, no hall, simpler floor demand).

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is master's exclusive — door on master's rear (north) wall opens north into ensuite. Ensuite sits at the rear-left corner behind master (canonical kitchen-right form: master at left, ensuite directly behind).ensuite_door
  • master ↔ hall — master's east wall has a door opening east into the hall — 1.0 m floor = 0.8 m clear + 2x 0.1 m frame stops. This is master's only public access.bedroom_door
  • standard ↔ hall — standard's north wall has a door opening north into the hall — 1.0 m floor. Standard has no other public access.bedroom_door
  • common ↔ hall — common bath's south wall has a door opening south into the hall — the bath's only door. Both bedrooms reach the common bath via the hall. DEFAULT host of the 'common_access' door-host group.bath_door
  • common ↔ kitchen — common bath shares plumbing wall with kitchen. ALTERNATE host of the 'common_access' group: door placed here only when shared wall >= 1.5 m; falls back to hall on compact lots.wet_core
  • common ↔ ensuite — common bath and ensuite share a plumbing wall — common's west meets ensuite's east, completing the 3-room wet-stack cluster at the rear (ensuite + common + kitchen, all rear-band).wet_core
  • hall ↔ great — hall's east wall is an open mouth into the great_room (no door — codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open-plan). 0.9 m min mouth — looser than the 1.0 m used elsewhere in the catalog because the wide-shell side-split's stacked-column geometry forces a tight rear-band budget on compact lots. On 14x10 with kitchen pinned at 1.8 m depth and hall at 1.2 m depth, allowing 0.9 m here lets common bath cap at 2.8 × 1.5 (= 4.2 sqm). The 0.9 m mouth is still a legal PH door-clear opening for single-person flow — two-person flow loses, but a starter home rarely needs it.open_plan
  • hall ↔ kitchen — hall's east wall continues to face the kitchen for the small strip where the hall depth exceeds great_room depth (on compact lots, hall.y_end is at the bath block's south edge while great.y_end is at the kitchen's north edge, and the two y values differ by ~0.3 m when kitchen is pinned at 1.8 m). Declaring this as open_plan tells the renderer to suppress the wall there — LDK is conceptually one open space, so the hall opens onto great AND kitchen as a continuous mouth, not a mouth into great with a 0.3 m wall sliver between hall and kitchen. Min 0.1 m so this adjacency holds even when the strip is minimal.open_plan
  • great ↔ kitchen — great_room + kitchen form one open LDK (gr style) stacked front-to-rear in the right column.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_side_split_rear_baths).
  • door_host_group — at least one door's host wall is chosen by the solver's auto-scorer (or overridden per-brief via door_host), not fixed in the topology.
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • match_bath_widths — solver forces the bath rooms' widths to match.
  • fallback_topology — if the solver reports infeasibility on the brief's lot, retries with 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr.

Both baths are PINNED at 2.8 \u00d7 1.5 m = 4.2 sqm (max_least 1.5, max_greatest 2.8, max_area 4.2). This makes ensuite and common identical in shape, so the wall separating them on the column divider (x = master_east = standard_west) starts cleanly at the bath block's south edge (the T&B/hall corner) and runs straight to the rear envelope. No 0.1 m offset between bath y_start values \u2014 the bath block reads as one clean horizontal stripe. 2026-07-20 update: this topology was restored as the compact-lot sibling of 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_ld (which can't reach shallow-depth lots); the published minimum 11x9 needed both the kitchen floor AND the hall's greatest-dim RANGE loosened together -- neither alone was sufficient (confirmed by bisection: kitchen alone at any floor down to 1.5 stayed infeasible; hall's max_least_dim_m loosened alone stayed infeasible; only hall's min_greatest_dim_m specifically, down to <=2.6, combined with kitchen<=1.6, unlocks 11x9). Bath caps were NOT touched -- confirmed unnecessary via the same bisection."
    }
