  "notes": [
    "RESTORED 2026-07-20 as the compact-lot sibling of 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_ld",
    "(deleted the same day, then restored the same day once testing showed the LDK split",
    "genuinely can't reach shallow-depth lots: its living/dining/kitchen stack needs at",
    "least ~5.5 m buildable depth, confirmed via isolated-column bisection). This gr",
    "(combined great_room, 2-deep public stack: great+kitchen) is the one to use down to",
    "~5 m buildable depth -- verified feasible at 11x9 (buildable 7x5). cl_ld's",
    "fallback_topology now points here (was dangling at a file deleted 2026-06-25, fixed",
    "the same day this topology was restored). No fallback_below_buildable_sqm gate set --",
    "the general infeasibility-triggered fallback (not the skip-straight-to-fallback area",
    "gate) is deliberately what routes shallow-depth briefs here, since the actual binding",
    "constraint is DEPTH specifically, not buildable AREA (confirmed: widening a shallow",
    "lot does NOT unlock cl_ld -- 12x9, 13x9, and 15x9 are all still infeasible on cl_ld",
    "despite far more area than the 12x10 minimum that does work -- so an area-based skip-",
    "ahead gate would misroute a wide-but-shallow lot).",
    "",
    "Wide-shell catalog topology \u2014 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED at the REAR. First wide-shell with rear-clustered baths AND a hallway-served bedroom wing. Distinct from 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr (single rear-bath, no ensuite, hall-served) and its own since-restored ds_gr-style siblings (mid-band common bath, no ensuite, no hall).",
    "",
    "Floor band: this topology now covers the shallow/compact end (~50-60 sqm buildable,",
    "11x9 through roughly 14x10) -- cl_ld picks up 12x10 and deeper. Originally designed",
    "against 14x10 m as its own low edge before cl_ld existed; both topologies solve there,",
    "so either is a valid choice at that size.",
    "Larger lots (14x12, 15x11, 16x11) let master and bedrooms breathe toward the preferred high.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (CANONICAL form \u2014 kitchen on RIGHT, bedrooms on LEFT, matches squarish catalog convention; brief.carport_side='right' or 'none'):",
    "  Rear band  (1.5 m deep):    [ENSUITE | COMMON T&B | KITCHEN       ]",
    "  Middle band (1.5 m deep):   [(master | HALL       | (great cont.) ]",
    "  Front band (3-4 m deep):    [ MASTER | STANDARD   | GREAT ROOM    ]",
    "",
    "Three vertical sub-columns (canonical, kitchen-side right):",
    "  - LEFT  (3 m wide): [master front+middle, ensuite rear]",
    "  - MID   (3 m wide): [standard front, hall middle, common T&B rear]",
    "  - RIGHT (4 m wide): [great_room front, kitchen rear] \u2014 the LDK stack",
    "",
    "Hall is a 4-walled circulation node:",
    "  - NORTH (rear): door to common T&B (bath's only door)",
    "  - SOUTH (front): door to standard bedroom (standard's only door)",
    "  - EAST: open mouth into great_room (the public connection \u2014 open-plan)",
    "  - WEST: door to master bedroom (master's only public door)",
    "",
    "Master ensuite is master's exclusive (door on master's north/rear wall into ensuite). Master accesses public space ONLY via the hall \u2014 there is no direct master\u2194great door. This adds a small acoustic buffer between the master and the LDK, and keeps the master's west+south+north walls available for closets, windows, and the ensuite door.",
    "",
    "Wet-stack cluster: ensuite's east = common's west (wet_core 1.2 m), and common's east = kitchen's west (wet_core 1.0 m). All three rooms (ensuite, common, kitchen) sit on the rear band, sharing one continuous plumbing chase along the rear elevation. The dirty kitchen in the rear setback completes the wet-utility row.",
    "",
    "Bedroom widths: match_bedroom_widths=true forces master.width = standard.width. Bath widths: match_bath_widths=true forces common.width = ensuite.width. Combined with the left-anchor on master/ensuite (left wall) and the right-anchor on great/kitchen (right wall), this naturally tiles the bedroom wing into two 3 m sub-columns when the buildable width allows it.",
    "",
    "Hall depth: lot_adjustment_profile floors the hallway at 1.4 m on compact lots so the hall\u2194great open mouth (which equals great's depth that extends into the hall's y-range) stays at 1.0 m minimum.",
    "",
    "Carport: not declared in the base topology \u2014 this is the NCP form. To add a left-side carport (mirrors the canonical), set brief.carport_side='left' \u2014 the runner mirrors the topology and LDK appears on the left. For a right-side carport, add a building_void at rear_right but be aware the rear-right corner is the kitchen+dirty-kitchen wet stack so a right-side carport doesn't fit naturally here. For a front-parallel carport, brief.carport_side='front' bumps the front setback to 3 m.",
    "",
    "Fallback: on shells too tight for the rear-bath cluster (envelope < ~55 sqm buildable or ratio outside 1.30-1.85), the runner falls back to 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_gr (single common bath, no ensuite, no hall, simpler floor demand)."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/1s_2br_11x9_wd_side_split_baths_cl_gr_ncp.json

2BR / 2 baths clustered rear, mid-band hall, great_room smoke test, no carport. 11x9 lot gives a 7x5 envelope = 35 m2 floor -- the shallow-depth minimum this topology now serves as the compact-lot sibling of 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_ld (which needs >=5.5 m buildable depth for its living/dining/kitchen split and can't reach this size). Master ensuite + common bath share wet-stack plumbing walls at the rear along with kitchen; a hallway is tucked into the mid sub-column between standard (front) and common (rear) -- all bedroom and bath doors open onto the hall, never directly onto the public side. Public side is one combined great_room + kitchen (2-deep stack), not a separate living/dining split.

Lot 11×9 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 7×5 m buildable envelope — 35 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot11 × 9 m (99 m²)
Buildable envelope7 × 5 m (35 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

master ensuite · common bath accessible from the hall · clustered baths at the rear sharing plumbing with the kitchen · mid-band hallway serving both bedrooms and the common bath · open-plan great room and kitchen · no carport

No. 29 / 46

1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_ld

Verified
1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_ld · single-storey · Wide · 2 bed · 2 bath

Wide-shell 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED REAR with mid-band HALL, LD style — side-split: bedrooms in left column organised as two parallel front-to-rear sub-columns, LDK in right column (living front, dining mid, kitchen rear — stacked, not a combined great room). Left sub-column: [master, ensuite]. Mid sub-column: [standard, hall, common T&B]. Both baths cluster at the rear sharing a wet-stack plumbing wall with the kitchen.

Definition

1s/2br/wide/1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_ld.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

Created 2026-07-20 as an LDK split (living/dining/kitchen stacked front-to-rear, the solver's default LDK arrangement) of 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_gr -- gr sibling deleted the same day, this topology replaces it. Private column + hall structure entirely unchanged: two parallel front-to-rear sub-columns (master+ensuite on the left, standard+hall+common on the mid), bedroom and bath doors all open onto the hall only, never directly onto the public side.

Layout intent (CANONICAL form -- kitchen on RIGHT, bedrooms on LEFT, brief.carport_side='right' or 'none'): Rear band (1.5 m deep): [ENSUITE | COMMON T&B | KITCHEN ] Middle band (1.5 m deep): [(master | HALL | DINING ] Front band (3-4 m deep): [ MASTER | STANDARD | LIVING ]

Only ONE adjacency changed role: hall's open-mouth connection moves from great_room to dining (not living) -- the hall sits in the MIDDLE sub-row of its own column, so its wall has to align with whichever public room occupies that same middle row in the living/dining/kitchen stack. Verified this empirically the same way the squarish cl_hall_gr->cl_hall_ld conversion was: hall->dining is correct. living gets hosts_entry instead of great (front-most public room, same role).

FEASIBILITY -- this conversion is genuinely narrow-band, unlike the squarish cl_hall_gr->cl_hall_ld sibling (which worked at every size tested). Diagnostic method: isolated the cause the same way as the 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_ds_gr LDK investigation (stripped rooms/ flags one at a time against an oversized 25x18 m envelope). Removing the hall<->dining adjacency alone makes the topology solve at ANY size; keeping it, the topology solves cleanly (0 warn) from ~11.9x10 (true floor, confirmed by bisection: 11.8x10 and 12x9.7 both infeasible) through roughly 15x11.5, then goes infeasible for good at every larger size tested up to 25x18 -- including sizes well past this topology's own 60-80 sqm floor-area band. Most likely cause: hall's own width is effectively locked to the middle sub-column's width (tied to standard's bedroom width via match_bedroom_widths), which doesn't scale the way it would need to as the envelope grows for hall to stay aligned with dining's row. Confirmed the compact- shell profile isn't the cause (tested with it disabled entirely and forced to always apply -- neither changed the upper bound).

Published minimum: 12x10 (round number just above the true ~11.9x10 floor). 0-warning solve.

gr sibling restored 2026-07-20 (same day as this topology's deletion and re-creation) once shallow-depth testing (11x9, buildable 7x5) proved this ld topology genuinely can't reach lots below ~5.5 m buildable depth -- isolated to the living/dining/kitchen 3-room stack specifically (confirmed by testing that column alone: needs >=5.5 m buildable depth even with unlimited width to spread into, while the bedroom-wing mid sub-column -- standard/hall/common, also 3 rooms -- fits fine at 5 m alone). great_room's 2-deep stack (great+kitchen) doesn't have this problem, so it's the one to use for shallow lots. fallback_topology now points to 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_gr.json (the restored sibling, same room program) -- deliberately relying on the general infeasibility-triggered fallback rather than a fallback_below_buildable_sqm area gate, because the actual binding constraint is DEPTH specifically, not buildable AREA: widening a shallow lot does NOT unlock this topology (12x9, 13x9, 15x9 all still infeasible despite far more area than 12x10), so an area-based skip-ahead gate would misroute a wide-but-shallow lot straight past a solve attempt that could never have succeeded anyway, and worse, could incorrectly gate AWAY a lot that's narrow-but-deep-enough to actually work. The upper feasibility bound (~15x11.5+) is unaffected by this restoration and remains unverified against the gr sibling -- gr's own large-lot behavior wasn't tested this session.

Wet-stack cluster, bedroom/bath width matching, and carport handling all unchanged from the gr sibling.

claim_dead_strips: true (added 2026-07-20). At tight lot sizes the three mid-column private rooms (standard 2.2 / hall 2.2-2.8 / common 2.5) and the two bath widths don't share an edge, so the solve leaves small unclaimed interior pockets no edge-snap can reach -- e.g. at the 12x10 min, ~0.86 m2 in two pockets. This flag runs the multi-storey pipeline's claim_dead_strips post-process on the single-storey realize (off by default catalog-wide): each rectangular dead pocket becomes an L-alcove on an adjacent room. At 12x10 living absorbs the 0.78 m2 pocket (L-shaped living) and hall takes a 0.07 m2 sliver -> 0 dead space, still 0 warnings. Purely a realized-geometry cleanup -- never touches solver feasibility, so the feasible band and minimums above are unaffected. At generous widths (14x10+) the private rooms tile evenly on their own and the flag is inert. The gr sibling (1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_gr) does NOT set this flag yet -- it has its own, larger dead space (~2.4 m2 at 12x10) but was left as-is since only this topology's output was flagged for cleanup.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is master's exclusive — door on master's rear (north) wall opens north into ensuite. Unchanged from the gr sibling.ensuite_door
  • master ↔ hall — master's east wall has a door opening east into the hall — master's only public access. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bedroom_door
  • standard ↔ hall — standard's north wall has a door opening north into the hall — standard's only public access. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bedroom_door
  • common ↔ hall — common bath's south wall has a door opening south into the hall — the bath's only door. Both bedrooms reach the common bath via the hall. DEFAULT host of the 'common_access' door-host group. Unchanged from the gr sibling.bath_door
  • common ↔ kitchen — common bath shares plumbing wall with kitchen. ALTERNATE host of the 'common_access' group. Unchanged from the gr sibling.wet_core
  • common ↔ ensuite — common bath and ensuite share a plumbing wall — completing the 3-room wet-stack cluster at the rear. Unchanged from the gr sibling.wet_core
  • hall ↔ dining — hall's east wall is an open mouth into dining, not living — the hall sits in the MIDDLE sub-row of its own column (standard front / hall mid / common rear), so its wall has to align with whichever public room occupies that same middle row in the living/dining/kitchen stack. Takes over the role hall<->great played in the gr sibling. Verified empirically: this is a genuinely narrow-band conversion (see notes) unlike the squarish cl_hall_gr->cl_hall_ld sibling, which worked at every size tested.open_plan
  • living ↔ dining — living and dining stack front-to-rear -- open mouth, no wall (ld style).open_plan
  • dining ↔ kitchen — dining and kitchen stack front-to-rear -- open pass-through, matches the solver's default kitchen+dining-must-stack rule directly.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_side_split_rear_baths_ld).
  • door_host_group — at least one door's host wall is chosen by the solver's auto-scorer (or overridden per-brief via door_host), not fixed in the topology.
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • match_bath_widths — solver forces the bath rooms' widths to match.
  • fallback_topology — if the solver reports infeasibility on the brief's lot, retries with 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_gr.

    "Created 2026-07-20 as an LDK split (living/dining/kitchen stacked",
    "front-to-rear, the solver's default LDK arrangement) of",
    "1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_gr -- gr sibling deleted the same day,",
    "this topology replaces it. Private column + hall structure entirely",
    "unchanged: two parallel front-to-rear sub-columns (master+ensuite on",
    "the left, standard+hall+common on the mid), bedroom and bath doors all",
    "open onto the hall only, never directly onto the public side.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (CANONICAL form -- kitchen on RIGHT, bedrooms on LEFT,",
    "brief.carport_side='right' or 'none'):",
    "  Rear band  (1.5 m deep):    [ENSUITE | COMMON T&B | KITCHEN     ]",
    "  Middle band (1.5 m deep):   [(master | HALL        | DINING     ]",
    "  Front band (3-4 m deep):    [ MASTER | STANDARD    | LIVING     ]",
    "",
    "Only ONE adjacency changed role: hall's open-mouth connection moves",
    "from great_room to dining (not living) -- the hall sits in the MIDDLE",
    "sub-row of its own column, so its wall has to align with whichever",
    "public room occupies that same middle row in the living/dining/kitchen",
    "stack. Verified this empirically the same way the squarish",
    "cl_hall_gr->cl_hall_ld conversion was: hall->dining is correct.",
    "living gets hosts_entry instead of great (front-most public room,",
    "same role).",
    "",
    "FEASIBILITY -- this conversion is genuinely narrow-band, unlike the",
    "squarish cl_hall_gr->cl_hall_ld sibling (which worked at every size",
    "tested). Diagnostic method: isolated the cause the same way as the",
    "1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_ds_gr LDK investigation (stripped rooms/",
    "flags one at a time against an oversized 25x18 m envelope). Removing",
    "the hall<->dining adjacency alone makes the topology solve at ANY",
    "size; keeping it, the topology solves cleanly (0 warn) from ~11.9x10",
    "(true floor, confirmed by bisection: 11.8x10 and 12x9.7 both",
    "infeasible) through roughly 15x11.5, then goes infeasible for good at",
    "every larger size tested up to 25x18 -- including sizes well past",
    "this topology's own 60-80 sqm floor-area band. Most likely cause:",
    "hall's own width is effectively locked to the middle sub-column's",
    "width (tied to standard's bedroom width via match_bedroom_widths),",
    "which doesn't scale the way it would need to as the envelope grows",
    "for hall to stay aligned with dining's row. Confirmed the compact-",
    "shell profile isn't the cause (tested with it disabled entirely and",
    "forced to always apply -- neither changed the upper bound).",
    "",
    "Published minimum: 12x10 (round number just above the true ~11.9x10",
    "floor). 0-warning solve.",
    "",
    "gr sibling restored 2026-07-20 (same day as this topology's deletion",
    "and re-creation) once shallow-depth testing (11x9, buildable 7x5)",
    "proved this ld topology genuinely can't reach lots below ~5.5 m",
    "buildable depth -- isolated to the living/dining/kitchen 3-room stack",
    "specifically (confirmed by testing that column alone: needs >=5.5 m",
    "buildable depth even with unlimited width to spread into, while the",
    "bedroom-wing mid sub-column -- standard/hall/common, also 3 rooms --",
    "fits fine at 5 m alone). great_room's 2-deep stack (great+kitchen)",
    "doesn't have this problem, so it's the one to use for shallow lots.",
    "fallback_topology now points to 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_gr.json",
    "(the restored sibling, same room program) -- deliberately relying on",
    "the general infeasibility-triggered fallback rather than a",
    "fallback_below_buildable_sqm area gate, because the actual binding",
    "constraint is DEPTH specifically, not buildable AREA: widening a",
    "shallow lot does NOT unlock this topology (12x9, 13x9, 15x9 all still",
    "infeasible despite far more area than 12x10), so an area-based",
    "skip-ahead gate would misroute a wide-but-shallow lot straight past a",
    "solve attempt that could never have succeeded anyway, and worse, could",
    "incorrectly gate AWAY a lot that's narrow-but-deep-enough to actually",
    "work. The upper feasibility bound (~15x11.5+) is unaffected by this",
    "restoration and remains unverified against the gr sibling -- gr's own",
    "large-lot behavior wasn't tested this session.",
    "",
    "Wet-stack cluster, bedroom/bath width matching, and carport handling",
    "all unchanged from the gr sibling.",
    "",
    "claim_dead_strips: true (added 2026-07-20). At tight lot sizes the three",
    "mid-column private rooms (standard 2.2 / hall 2.2-2.8 / common 2.5) and",
    "the two bath widths don't share an edge, so the solve leaves small",
    "unclaimed interior pockets no edge-snap can reach -- e.g. at the 12x10",
    "min, ~0.86 m2 in two pockets. This flag runs the multi-storey pipeline's",
    "claim_dead_strips post-process on the single-storey realize (off by",
    "default catalog-wide): each rectangular dead pocket becomes an L-alcove",
    "on an adjacent room. At 12x10 living absorbs the 0.78 m2 pocket (L-shaped",
    "living) and hall takes a 0.07 m2 sliver -> 0 dead space, still 0 warnings.",
    "Purely a realized-geometry cleanup -- never touches solver feasibility, so",
    "the feasible band and minimums above are unaffected. At generous widths",
    "(14x10+) the private rooms tile evenly on their own and the flag is inert.",
    "The gr sibling (1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_gr) does NOT set this flag",
    "yet -- it has its own, larger dead space (~2.4 m2 at 12x10) but was left",
    "as-is since only this topology's output was flagged for cleanup."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/1s_2br_12x10_wd_side_split_baths_cl_ld_ncp.json

2BR / 2 baths clustered rear, mid-band hall, LDK smoke test, no carport. 12x10 lot gives an 8x6 envelope = 48 m2 floor -- the published minimum for this topology (true floor is ~11.9x10, confirmed by bisection). Master ensuite + common bath share wet-stack plumbing walls at the rear along with kitchen; a hallway is tucked into the mid sub-column between standard (front) and common (rear) -- all bedroom and bath doors open onto the hall, never directly onto the public side. Public side is a real living/dining/kitchen split stacked front-to-rear -- the hall's open mouth connects to dining (the middle-row public room), not living. Unlike the squarish cl_hall_gr->cl_hall_ld sibling, this conversion is narrow-band: verified feasible only from ~12x10 through roughly 15x11.5.

Lot 12×10 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 8×6 m buildable envelope — 48 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot12 × 10 m (120 m²)
Buildable envelope8 × 6 m (48 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

master ensuite · common bath accessible from the hall · clustered baths at the rear sharing plumbing with the kitchen · mid-band hallway serving both bedrooms and the common bath · separate living, dining, and kitchen rooms stacked front-to-rear · no carport

1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_ds_gr

Verified
1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_ds_gr · single-storey · Wide · 2 bed · 2 bath

Wide-shell 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, no hall — ensuite sandwiched between master (front) and standard (rear) in the bedroom column, mirroring the squarish distributed-bath pattern (1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_ds_ld). Both bedrooms door directly into the great room; common bath gets its own direct doors from standard (private route) and from the great room (guest-accessible route) instead of routing through a hall.

Definition

1s/2br/wide/1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_ds_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK

Design notes

Wide-shell catalog topology — 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, no hall. Sibling of 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_ds_hall_gr with the hall removed: both bedrooms door directly into the great room (same direct-access idiom master already used), and common bath picks up a second direct door onto the great room instead of routing through hall. Mirrors the squarish 1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_ds_ld pattern, brought into the wide shell.

Floor band: roughly 65-85 sqm buildable — same territory as the hall variant and side_split_baths_cl_ld, sized against 14x11 to 15x12 m lots.

Layout intent (canonical form — brief.carport_side='right' or 'none'): Rear band: [standard | common (wet) | kitchen ] Mid band: [ensuite | | great (cont.) ] Front band: [master | GREAT ROOM (direct doors from both bedrooms) ]

Two sub-columns (no separate mid/hall column, unlike the hall variant): - LEFT (~3-3.5 m): [master front, ensuite mid, standard rear] — bedroom column, same distributed stack as squarish ds_ld - RIGHT (~5.5-6.5 m): [great_room front+mid, kitchen rear] — LDK column, with common bath tucked at the rear boundary next to kitchen

Master and standard access: both door directly into the great room — no hall buffer for either bedroom.

Common bath access: ONE actual door, from the great room — the standard<->common adjacency above is declared (for geometry pull) but always gets suppressed to a wall by architectural_plan.py's generic bedroom<->common-bath rule, same as the squarish ds_ld sibling. So standard's route to the bath is through the great room, same as everyone else's.

Wet stack: common bath's east wall = kitchen's west wall (wet_core). Combined with the rear-setback dirty kitchen, all wet utilities land in one rear-east bay, same as every other topology in the catalog.

Carport: not declared in the base topology — NCP form, consistent with the other wide topologies. To add a side carport, add a building_void at front_right. For a front-parallel carport, brief.carport_side='front' bumps the front setback to 3 m. When brief.carport_side='left', the runner mirrors the topology so the LDK ends up on the left and the bedroom column on the right.

No fallback_topology declared — matches the squarish ds_ld sibling, which also omits one.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is master's exclusive — door on master's north wall, ensuite sits directly behind master in the bedroom column's mid band. Same distributed-bath idiom as the squarish ds catalog, brought into the wide shell.ensuite_door
  • ensuite ↔ standard — ensuite's north wall touches standard's south wall — no door, acoustic+privacy buffer between the two bedrooms.bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • master ↔ great — master's east wall = great room's west wall in the front band — a real door, no hall buffer.bedroom_to_public
  • standard ↔ great — standard's east wall = great room's west wall in the rear band — a real door, no hall buffer. With the hall removed, both bedrooms access the great room directly, same as the squarish distributed-bath sibling.bedroom_to_public
  • standard ↔ common — declared for adjacency/geometry purposes (pulls common next to standard) but architectural_plan.py's generic bedroom<->common-bath rule always suppresses this into a wall, never a door — common baths are never directly bedroom-accessible. Same no-op declaration exists on the squarish ds_ld sibling. Standard's only route to common is via the great room door below.bath_door
  • common ↔ great — common bath's ONLY actual door — opens onto the great room, mirrors the squarish distributed-bath sibling's common↔great door. With the hall removed and the standard↔common door suppressed (see above), this is the sole way in or out of common bath.bath_door
  • common ↔ kitchen — shared plumbing wall — common bath and kitchen stack at the rear.wet_core
  • great ↔ kitchen — great_room + kitchen form one open LDK (gr style) — consistent with every wide-shell topology in the catalog so far; the shell's shallower depth doesn't comfortably support a separate living+dining ('ld') split.open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_wd_side_split_baths_ds_gr).
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • ensuite_alcove_joins_master — the strip beside a narrower ensuite is folded into master as an L-shaped alcove.

      "rationale": "On compact wide lots (<80 sqm buildable), kitchen floors at 1.8 m depth (usable galley, above the PD 1096 1.5 m hard min). Both baths get an auto floor so neither collapses to its hard minimum by default; master is capped at 18 sqm so its priority weight doesn't steal depth from the ensuite that's sandwiched behind it. preferred_apply bumps ensuite to 3.0 sqm and kitchen to a more generous 2.0 m galley where the envelope allows \u2014 tried first, relaxed together on infeasibility."

    "",

So standard's route to the bath is through the great room, same as everyone else's.",
    "",
    "Wet stack: common bath's east wall = kitchen's west wall (wet_core). Combined with the rear-setback dirty kitchen, all wet utilities land in one rear-east bay, same as every other topology in the catalog.",
    "",
    "Carport: not declared in the base topology \u2014 NCP form, consistent with the other wide topologies. To add a side carport, add a building_void at front_right. For a front-parallel carport, brief.carport_side='front' bumps the front setback to 3 m. When brief.carport_side='left', the runner mirrors the topology so the LDK ends up on the left and the bedroom column on the right.",
    "",
    "No fallback_topology declared \u2014 matches the squarish ds_ld sibling, which also omits one."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/1s_2br_14x11_wd_side_split_baths_ds_gr_ncp.json

Wide-shell 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, no hall, both bedrooms direct-to-great — 14x11 lot, NO CARPORT. Envelope 10x7 = 70 m² floor, within the topology's 65-85 m² buildable band. Bedroom column (left): master front, ensuite mid (sandwiched, master's exclusive), standard rear. Both master and standard door directly east into the great room. Common bath's only door opens onto the great room (bedroom<->common-bath is always wall-only per the codebase's generic rule). Common bath and kitchen share a rear wet-stack. Great_room + kitchen form one open LDK on the right.

Lot 14×11 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 10×7 m buildable envelope — 70 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot14 × 11 m (154 m²)
Buildable envelope10 × 7 m (70 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

master ensuite sandwiched between master and standard (distributed, not clustered) · both master and standard door directly into the great room, no hall · common bath accessible from the great room · open-plan great_room + kitchen (gr style) · wet-stack between common bath and kitchen · no carport

No. 31 / 46

2s_2br_wd_rear_stair_bath_gr

Verified
2s_2br_wd_rear_stair_bath_gr · 2-storey · Wide · 2 bed · 2 bath

Wide-shell 2-STOREY 2BR / 2 baths, REAR SIDEWAYS STAIR, hall-less ground floor — the catalog's first HORIZONTAL stair run: the flight lies along the rear band (3.5 m run sideways), leaving the full 5 m depth for a full-width great room across the front (entry). Kitchen + T&B complete the rear band beside the flight. Upstairs, both bedrooms sit street-facing at the front with the landing hall as a bounded center notch between them (acoustic separation) and the second T&B stacked directly over the first. Canonical 11x9 lot (7x5 buildable per floor, 70 m² gross — in the 65-80 band, on wide-frontage lots the squarish 10x10 can't serve).

Definition

2s/2br/wide/2s_2br_wd_rear_stair_bath_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

2-STOREY wide catalog topology #1 — REAR SIDEWAYS STAIR, hall-less GF (authored 2026-07-19; third 2s topology, first on the wide shell, and the first topology in the whole catalog whose stair runs HORIZONTALLY — the solver's run-axis decision picks the sideways orientation on this shell unprompted, exercising the horizontal branch of the stair_boarding/stair_arrival end constraints).

Why this parti is the WIDE take on a 2-storey 2BR: a 5 m-deep shell can't afford a 3.5 m front-to-rear stair run plus a front living band, so the flight lies SIDEWAYS along the rear, and the entire depth goes to a full-width great room (~24 m² at canonical — the largest GF public room of the 2s catalog). The ~7 m width then gives both bedrooms street-facing front positions upstairs, separated by the landing hall as a bounded center notch (acoustic privacy between bedrooms).

Canonical layout (11 x 9 lot, 2 m setbacks, 7 x 5 buildable per floor): GF: Front: [great room (entry, FULL width, counter at rear edge) ] Rear: [stair (sideways) -> | kitchen | T&B ] 2F: Front: [master | hall2 | br2 ] Rear: [stairwell | hall2 | T&B (over GF T&B) ] Stair: straight, 0.9-1.2 m wide, >= 3.5 m sideways run, 15 risers @ 200 mm / 250 mm treads, left-anchored; boards off the great room at one end of the run, arrives at hall2 at the other (stair_boarding / stair_arrival hold the mouths to opposite ends — orientation-general).

Fit rule: the binding constraint is WIDTH, not area — the rear band must host stair run + counter-width kitchen + min bath side by side: 3.5 + 2.1 + 1.4 = 7.0 m buildable width. Verified: 11x8.5 (63 m² gross) solves; 10.5x9 does NOT (only 6.5 m wide despite more area). Feasible band 11x8.5 through 13x9.5+ (99 m² gross), 0 warnings throughout after the kitchen mechanical_vent flag (its rear wall is counter-width and service-door-dominated at canonical, same rationale as the narrow 2s sibling).

Structure/override choices (per established conventions): - zone_split is the 2F-only horizontal partition pinning both bedrooms to the front band (street windows) with bath2 behind — the same device as the squarish 2s sibling; only the stair is anchored (single-member left_anchored, the safe pattern). - zone_balance_rooms counts the whole upper floor minus stairwell as private — bedrooms alone can never outweigh the full-width great room + kitchen. - soft proximity bath2->stairwell lands the upper T&B directly over the GF T&B at canonical (verified) — the interim pattern while cross-floor wet-stack alignment stays soft-only in v2. - hallway max_area_sqm 4.5 in the always-on profile: at canonical the 2F hall is a full-depth 0.9 m center notch between the bedrooms; the cap keeps it from snap-growing past 4.5 m² (it absorbed 6.6 m² uncapped). On deeper/wider lots (12x9 up) the solver returns a compact rear-band hall instead.

Known quirk: the strip behind the sideways flight (between stair and rear wall, when the solver floats the flight off the rear wall) is claimed as an alcove by an adjacent room (kitchen pantry shelf on GF, hall ledge on 2F) — honest under/behind-stair space, suggestion-level.

Naming note: the `bath` token undercounts the program (two common baths, one per floor) — kept consistent with the other 2s topologies pending the 2s naming-convention pass. No carport variant yet (ncp briefs only), matching the rest of the 2s catalog.

Key adjacencies

  • great ↔ kitchen — GF: kitchen opens directly off the great room — dining counter on the shared edge (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass = 2.1 m min); band + stools live on the great-room side to keep the galley aisle clear. Render-only (counter_divider) — briefs may set dining_counter: false to suppress it without touching this topology.open_plan
  • great ↔ bath1 — GF: T&B doors into the great room — hall-less GF, the great room IS the circulation; door-placement scoring keeps it off the entry sightlinebath_door
  • great ↔ stair — GF: open stair boards straight off the great room; the boarding constraint holds this edge to the flight's bottom endstair_boarding
  • kitchen ↔ bath1 — GF: T&B shares a plumbing wall with the kitchen — single GF wet bay at the rear; kitchen keeps its rear service doorwet_core
  • stair ↔ stairwell — VERTICAL circulation edge: flight and stairwell opening occupy the identical rectangle on their floors (rect-equality in the solver, graph connectivity for validation)stair_vertical
  • stairwell ↔ hall2 — 2F: the landing hall must meet the stairwell at the TOP end of the flight — step off into circulation, never a bedroom wallstair_arrival
  • hall2 ↔ master — 2F: master door off the landing hallbedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ br2 — 2F: second bedroom door off the landing hallbedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ bath2 — 2F: second T&B doors off the landing hallbath_door

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (stair_run_geometry).
  • zone_balance_rooms — custom room grouping overrides the default public/private area split (private: master, br2, bath2, hall2; public: great, kitchen).
  • aspect_overrides — per-room aspect-ratio (w:h) floor for stairs (4.0:1).
  • Two-storey: GF flight and 2F stairwell pinned to the identical rectangle (stair_vertical); ascent direction forced via stair_boarding/stair_arrival so the flight can't top out against a wall.

    "axis": "horizontal",
    "private_side": "front",
    "private_rooms": [
      "master",
      "br2"
    ],
    "public_rooms": [
      "bath2"
    ],
    "note": "2F-only geometric partition (all listed rooms are storey 2, per the multi-storey zone_split rule): both bedrooms hold the FRONT band \u2014 the squarish signature, side-by-side street-facing windows \u2014 with the T&B strictly behind the split line. 'public_rooms' here is the mechanical 'other side of the split' list, not a zoning claim; hall2 and stairwell float."
  },
  "zone_balance_rooms": {
    "private": [
      "master",
      "br2",
      "bath2",
      "hall2"
    ],
    "public": [
      "great",
      "kitchen"
    ]
  },
  "aspect_overrides": {
    "stairs": 4.0
  },
  "left_anchored": [
    "stair"
  ],
  "lot_adjustment_profiles": [
    {
      "name": "stair_run_geometry",
      "when": {
        "buildable_area_lt_sqm": 99999
      },
      "auto_apply": {
        "stairs": {
          "min_greatest_dim_m": 3.5,
          "max_least_dim_m": 1.2
        },
        "common_bath": {
          "max_greatest_dim_m": 2.6,
          "max_area_sqm": 3.9
        },
        "hallway": {
          "max_area_sqm": 4.5
        }
      },
      "rationale": "ALWAYS-ON (the 99999 trigger): a straight stair's run is riser math the solver cannot derive \u2014 3.0 m floor-to-floor at 15 x 200 mm risers needs 14 x 250 mm treads = 3.5 m; min_greatest_dim_m pins the run, max_least_dim_m 1.2 keeps the flight a flight (orientation stays the solver's choice \u2014 on this shell it picks the sideways run). common_bath caps stop post-snap gap-filling from stretching a bath into a corridor. hallway max_area_sqm 4.5 bounds the 2F landing hall: post-snap growth is area-capped, so on the compact canonical shell the center-notch hall stays at 4.5 m\u00b2 instead of absorbing 6.6 m\u00b2 that belongs to the bedrooms (dimension caps were tried and rejected \u2014 they made every 5 m-deep shell infeasible)."
    }
  ],
  "front_to_rear_stacks": [
    [
      "great",
      "kitchen"
    ],
    [
      "great",
      "bath1"
    ],
    [
      "great",
      "stair"
    ]
  ],
  "setback_elements": [
    {
      "type": "service_area",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false
    }
  ],
  "notes": [
    "2-STOREY wide catalog topology #1 \u2014 REAR SIDEWAYS STAIR, hall-less GF",
    "(authored 2026-07-19; third 2s topology, first on the wide shell, and",
    "the first topology in the whole catalog whose stair runs HORIZONTALLY \u2014",
    "the solver's run-axis decision picks the sideways orientation on this",
    "shell unprompted, exercising the horizontal branch of the",
    "stair_boarding/stair_arrival end constraints).",
    "",
    "Why this parti is the WIDE take on a 2-storey 2BR: a 5 m-deep shell",
    "can't afford a 3.5 m front-to-rear stair run plus a front living band,",
    "so the flight lies SIDEWAYS along the rear, and the entire depth goes",
    "to a full-width great room (~24 m\u00b2 at canonical \u2014 the largest GF public",
    "room of the 2s catalog). The ~7 m width then gives both bedrooms",
    "street-facing front positions upstairs, separated by the landing hall",
    "as a bounded center notch (acoustic privacy between bedrooms).",
    "",
    "Canonical layout (11 x 9 lot, 2 m setbacks, 7 x 5 buildable per floor):",
    "  GF:  Front: [great room (entry, FULL width, counter at rear edge) ]",
    "       Rear:  [stair (sideways) -> | kitchen | T&B                  ]",
    "  2F:  Front: [master        | hall2 | br2                          ]",
    "       Rear:  [stairwell     | hall2 | T&B (over GF T&B)            ]",
    "Stair: straight, 0.9-1.2 m wide, >= 3.5 m sideways run, 15 risers @",
    "200 mm / 250 mm treads, left-anchored; boards off the great room at one",
    "end of the run, arrives at hall2 at the other (stair_boarding /",
    "stair_arrival hold the mouths to opposite ends \u2014 orientation-general).",
    "",
    "Fit rule: the binding constraint is WIDTH, not area \u2014 the rear band",
    "must host stair run + counter-width kitchen + min bath side by side:",
    "3.5 + 2.1 + 1.4 = 7.0 m buildable width. Verified: 11x8.5 (63 m\u00b2",
    "gross) solves; 10.5x9 does NOT (only 6.5 m wide despite more area).",
    "Feasible band 11x8.5 through 13x9.5+ (99 m\u00b2 gross), 0 warnings",
    "throughout after the kitchen mechanical_vent flag (its rear wall is",
    "counter-width and service-door-dominated at canonical, same rationale",
    "as the narrow 2s sibling).",
    "",
    "Structure/override choices (per established conventions):",
    "  - zone_split is the 2F-only horizontal partition pinning both",
    "    bedrooms to the front band (street windows) with bath2 behind \u2014",
    "    the same device as the squarish 2s sibling; only the stair is",
    "    anchored (single-member left_anchored, the safe pattern).",
    "  - zone_balance_rooms counts the whole upper floor minus stairwell as",
    "    private \u2014 bedrooms alone can never outweigh the full-width great",
    "    room + kitchen.",
    "  - soft proximity bath2->stairwell lands the upper T&B directly over",
    "    the GF T&B at canonical (verified) \u2014 the interim pattern while",
    "    cross-floor wet-stack alignment stays soft-only in v2.",
    "  - hallway max_area_sqm 4.5 in the always-on profile: at canonical the",
    "    2F hall is a full-depth 0.9 m center notch between the bedrooms;",
    "    the cap keeps it from snap-growing past 4.5 m\u00b2 (it absorbed 6.6 m\u00b2",
    "    uncapped). On deeper/wider lots (12x9 up) the solver returns a",
    "    compact rear-band hall instead.",
    "",
    "Known quirk: the strip behind the sideways flight (between stair and",
    "rear wall, when the solver floats the flight off the rear wall) is",
    "claimed as an alcove by an adjacent room (kitchen pantry shelf on GF,",
    "hall ledge on 2F) \u2014 honest under/behind-stair space, suggestion-level.",
    "",
    "Naming note: the `bath` token undercounts the program (two common",
    "baths, one per floor) \u2014 kept consistent with the other 2s topologies",
    "pending the 2s naming-convention pass. No carport variant yet (ncp",
    "briefs only), matching the rest of the 2s catalog."
  ]
}

Test brief

test/2s_2br_11x9_wd_rear_stair_bath_gr_ncp.json

2-STOREY 2BR / 2 bath wide rear-sideways-stair smoke test on the canonical 11x9 lot, no carport. 7x5 envelope per floor (35 m² footprint, 70 m² gross) — in the 65-80 m² 2BR/2-bath band, on a wide-frontage lot. Exercises the catalog's first HORIZONTAL stair run (the solver's run-axis decision picks the sideways orientation on this shell), the horizontal branch of the stair_boarding/stair_arrival end constraints, the full-width front great room, both bedrooms street-facing with the hall as a bounded center notch (hallway max_area cap), and bath2 stacked directly over bath1.

Lot 11×9 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 7×5 m buildable envelope — 35 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot11 × 9 m (99 m²)
Buildable envelope7 × 5 m (35 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested2
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

sideways stair along the rear band · full-width great room across the front · both bedrooms upstairs, street-facing · dining counter between great room and kitchen · upper T&B stacked over the ground-floor T&B · no carport

Exercises the catalog's first HORIZONTAL stair run (the solver's run-axis decision picks the sideways orientation on this shell), the horizontal branch of the stair_boarding/stair_arrival end constraints, the full-width front great room, both bedrooms street-facing with the hall as a bounded center notch (hallway max_area cap), and bath2 stacked directly over bath1.",

No. 32 / 46

1s_3br_sq_front_back_split_baths_cl_hall_lk

Verified
1s_3br_sq_front_back_split_baths_cl_hall_lk · single-storey · Squarish · 3 bed · 2 bath

Squarish 3BR / baths CLUSTERED, FRONT_BACK split with mid-band HALL — full-width public band at front (living-kitchen, lk style, dining folded into living), full-width private band at rear with all three bedrooms; wet core (common + ensuite back-to-back) in the middle band beside the hall. The most compact of the 3BR squarish family.

Definition

1s/3br/squarish/1s_3br_sq_front_back_split_baths_cl_hall_lk.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

3BR squarish catalog topology #3 — FRONT_BACK split, clustered wet stack. The most compact of the four: natural floor band ~84-96 m²; minimum feasible shell ~9.3 x 9.0 m (width is the binding constraint, not area). Verified 2026-07-18 end-to-end (solve + validate COMPLIANT) for ncp, ccp right, ccp left (mirror) and fcp, down to a true 9.0 x 9.0 m buildable (ncp).

Layout intent (canonical, brief.carport_side='right'): Rear: [br2 | br3 | master ] Mid: [HALL | common | ensuite ] Front: [living (entry) | kitchen ]

Program: clean two-band parti. Full-width public band at front — living hosts entry, kitchen at front-right, lk open plan (dining folds into living; the lanai or dirty-kitchen side can host overflow dining). Full-width private band at rear holds all three bedrooms. The thin middle band carries the east-west hall plus the entire wet stack: kitchen, common and ensuite form one contiguous plumbing bay (kitchen fronts it, common backs the kitchen, ensuite backs common and doors into master).

Ventilation: all three bedrooms hold the rear wall (windows to the rear setback / service area), living + kitchen hold the front — no landlocked room. Baths and hall vent via high windows or mechanical, per PD 1096 allowance for baths.

Fit rule: rear band needs >= 8.6 m internal width (2.8 + 2.8 + 3.0) — shell width >= ~9.3 m with wall allowances. Depth can compress to ~9.0 m because the mid band is only bath-depth (~1.8-2.0 m).

Single wet stack = cheapest plumbing of the family; pick this topology first for BP 220 / economic-housing briefs. When brief.carport_side='left', mirror.

Solver notes (2026-07-18): left_anchored/right_anchored were REMOVED — as authored (living+br2 left, kitchen+ensuite+master right) the topology was infeasible at every lot size (confirmed up to 20x20 buildable). Root cause: any anchor-list combination beyond a single list alone contradicts the front_to_rear_stacks + full adjacency graph (hall alone touches 5 other rooms while chained front-to-rear between living and br2) — pinning kitchen/ensuite/master flush to the right wall was the specific trigger, but left+rear together (no right at all) also failed. rear_anchored alone is sufficient: kitchen_side='right' (the solver's softer center-based symmetry break, untouched) still reliably keeps living left / kitchen right in practice, but br2/br3 left-right order within the rear band is no longer pinned — solver's choice, sometimes br3|br2|master instead of the diagram's br2|br3|master. Functionally equivalent (both are plain standard bedrooms); treat the ASCII diagram above as intent, not a geometry guarantee.

Key adjacencies

  • living ↔ kitchen — lk style — living and kitchen are one open front band, dining folds into the living sideopen_plan
  • hall ↔ living — hall's south mouth opens into the living room — no dooropen_plan
  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite sits in the mid band directly in front of master — interior door on master's front wallensuite_door
  • ensuite ↔ common — back-to-back baths share one plumbing wallwet_core
  • kitchen ↔ common — common bath backs onto the kitchen wall — all three wet rooms in one contiguous stackwet_core
  • master ↔ hall — master door at the hall's east endbedroom_door
  • br2 ↔ hall — br2 door opens north into the hallbedroom_door
  • br3 ↔ hall — br3 door opens north into the hallbedroom_door
  • common ↔ hall — common bath door opens west into the hallbath_door

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_sq_front_back_split_baths_cl_hall_lk).
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • match_bath_widths — solver forces the bath rooms' widths to match.
  • bedroom_band_fills_width — the bedroom band is forced to fill the full envelope width.

Test brief

test/1s_3br_13.3x13_sq_front_back_split_baths_cl_hall_lk_ncp.json

3BR / 2 bath front-back split, clustered wet stack, MINIMUM-boundary smoke test, no carport. 13.3x13 lot gives a 9.3x9.0 envelope = 83.7 m² floor — the verified minimum (width is the binding constraint). Full-width public band front (living + kitchen, lk style), full-width private band rear (all three bedrooms), single contiguous wet stack (kitchen-common-ensuite) beside the hall in the mid band.

Lot 13.3×13 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 9.3×9 m buildable envelope — 83.7 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot13.3 × 13 m (172.9 m²)
Buildable envelope9.3 × 9 m (83.7 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested3
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

clustered baths (ensuite + common) in one wet stack · central hall · open-plan living + kitchen (lk style) · no carport

1s_3br_sq_hall_core_baths_ds_hall_gr

Verified
1s_3br_sq_hall_core_baths_ds_hall_gr · single-storey · Squarish · 3 bed · 2 bath

Squarish 3BR / baths DISTRIBUTED with mid-band HALL-CORE (pinwheel) — master+ensuite and br3 across the rear band, br2 at front-right off the great_room; short central hall serves master, br3 and common T&B. Open-plan great_room + kitchen (gr style).

Definition

1s/3br/squarish/1s_3br_sq_hall_core_baths_ds_hall_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

3BR squarish catalog topology #1 — HALL-CORE (pinwheel). Natural floor band ~90-102 m²; minimum feasible shell 9.0 x 9.5 m buildable (13 x 13.5 lot, ncp/rectangular envelope) or 9.5 x 10.5 m buildable (13.5 x 14.5 lot, ccp; re-tuned 2026-07-19 when the master-bigger rule was extended to ALL standard bedrooms — the front-right carport notch costs ~0.5 m of both dimensions). Verified 2026-07-18 end-to-end (solve + validate COMPLIANT) for ncp, ccp right, ccp left (mirror) and fcp.

Layout intent (canonical, brief.carport_side='right'): Rear: [ensuite : master | br3 ] Mid: [kitchen | HALL | common T&B ] Front: [great_room (entry) | br2 ]

Program: public band at front-left (great_room hosts entry, kitchen behind it), private band across the rear (master with ensuite alcove at the rear-left corner, br3 rear-right), br2 tucked at front-right. A short mid-band hall (~1.2 x 2.5 m) between kitchen and common bath serves master, br3 and the common T&B; its south mouth is open to the great_room.

Ventilation: all three bedrooms and the great_room hold exterior walls (master/br3 rear, br2 front) — NBC Rule 7/8 light-and-vent satisfied with no landlocked habitable room, the usual failure mode of squarish 3BR layouts.

Plumbing: two short runs — ensuite backs onto the kitchen head wall (stack 1), common T&B sits one bay east off the hall (stack 2), pulled toward the kitchen by a soft proximity. Dirty kitchen + laundry land in the rear setback behind the kitchen.

Trade-off vs the side_split sibling: br2's door opens off the great_room (bedroom_to_public) instead of the hall — shorter circulation, slightly less bedroom privacy. When brief.carport_side='left', mirror the whole topology.

Solver notes (2026-07-18): kitchen_side_pin=false is REQUIRED — the kitchen column is left_anchored (non-carport side), which contradicts the default canonical-orientation pin outright and made the topology infeasible at every lot size. `common` is deliberately NOT in right_anchored: pinning it to the right wall while it must also reach hall (west door) and buffer br2 was a combinatorial contradiction; the bath-at-exterior-wall soft bonus still pulls common toward the right wall when geometry allows.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is an alcove in master's rear-left corner — interior door from masterensuite_door
  • ensuite ↔ kitchen — ensuite sits directly behind the kitchen head wall — first wet stackwet_core
  • master ↔ hall — master door opens south into the hall — 1.0 m floor = 0.8 m clear + frame stopsbedroom_door
  • br3 ↔ hall — br3 door opens south into the hallbedroom_door
  • common ↔ hall — common T&B door opens west into the hall — second wet stack, kept one bay from the kitchen runbath_door
  • common ↔ br2 — bath block is the acoustic buffer between hall/wet zone and br2bath_to_bedroom_wall
  • br2 ↔ great — br2 door opens directly off the great_room (front-right) — saves ~1 m of hall; the privacy trade-off of this familybedroom_to_public
  • hall ↔ great — hall's south end is an open mouth into the great_room (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)open_plan
  • great ↔ kitchen — great_room + kitchen are one open LDK (gr style)open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_sq_hall_core_baths_ds_hall_gr).
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • kitchen_side_pin: false — the default mirror-symmetry kitchen-side pin is disabled (kitchen legitimately sits on the non-carport side).

Test brief

test/1s_3br_13x13.5_sq_hall_core_baths_ds_hall_gr_ncp.json

3BR / 2 bath hall-core (pinwheel) MINIMUM-boundary smoke test, no carport. 13x13.5 lot gives a 9x9.5 envelope = 85.5 m² floor — the verified minimum for the rectangular (ncp) envelope. Public band front-left (great_room hosts entry, kitchen behind), master+ensuite and br3 across the rear band, br2 front-right off the great_room, short mid-band hall serving master, br3 and the common T&B. Kitchen column is on the LEFT (non-carport side) — exercises kitchen_side_pin: false.

Lot 13×13.5 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 9×9.5 m buildable envelope — 85.5 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot13 × 13.5 m (175.5 m²)
Buildable envelope9 × 9.5 m (85.5 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested3
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

distributed baths (ensuite + common) · central hall core · open-plan great room + kitchen · no carport

1s_3br_sq_l_wrap_baths_ds_hall_gr

Not regression-tested
1s_3br_sq_l_wrap_baths_ds_hall_gr · single-storey · Squarish · 3 bed · 2 bath

Squarish 3BR / baths DISTRIBUTED, private L-WRAP with west-flank HALL — standard pair stacked on the west flank (br2 front, br3 rear), master wraps the rear-center with ensuite against the kitchen wall; great_room + kitchen hold the front-right/rear-right corner (gr style). Maximum bedroom separation; suits corner lots.

Not yet regression-tested. No test brief in briefs/test/ currently exercises this topology through the solver (or every brief that references it falls back to a different topology before solving). Static structural check only — load_topology() loads cleanly — every room ID is unique, every adjacency references a real room, and every habitable room is reachable from the entry point. No test brief, rendered output, or solver-derived room sizing exists to show for this entry; nothing below is fabricated to fill the gap.

Definition

1s/3br/squarish/1s_3br_sq_l_wrap_baths_ds_hall_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

3BR squarish catalog topology #4 — private L-WRAP. Natural floor band ~94-108 m²; minimum feasible shell ~9.5 x 10.0 m. The roomiest parti of the four — pick it when the brief values bedroom separation over floor economy.

Layout intent (canonical, brief.carport_side='right'): Rear: [br3 | master : ensuite | kitchen ] Mid: [HALL | common | great_room ] Front: [br2 | great_room (entry) ]

Program: the private zone forms an L — standard pair stacked on the west flank (br2 front-left, br3 rear-left) with the hall between them, master wrapping the rear-center. The public gr corner (great_room hosting entry, kitchen behind it) fills the front-right/rear-right. The hall runs north-south between the standards, mouths east into the great_room, and elbows east at its rear end to pick up master's door and the common T&B.

Separation: master shares NO wall with either standard bedroom — the widest acoustic split available in a squarish 3BR shell (multi-generation briefs, rentable-room briefs).

Corner-lot fit: bedrooms hold the west + rear walls, public holds front + east — on a corner lot orient the public corner to the street intersection and every habitable room still gets an outdoor wall. No landlocked rooms.

Plumbing: distributed — ensuite rides the kitchen wall (stack 1), common sits mid-band off the hall (stack 2, softly pulled toward the kitchen).

Watch-outs: longest hall of the family (~3.5-4.5 m incl. elbow) and the elbow constraint in the compact profile. When brief.carport_side='left', mirror the topology.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is an alcove on master's east side — interior door from masterensuite_door
  • ensuite ↔ kitchen — ensuite shares the kitchen's west wall — first wet stack at the rear-rightwet_core
  • br2 ↔ hall — br2 door opens south into the hallbedroom_door
  • br3 ↔ hall — br3 door opens north into the hallbedroom_door
  • master ↔ hall — the hall elbows east at its rear end to reach master's front wall — master's door does NOT face the great_roombedroom_door
  • common ↔ hall — common T&B sits east of the hall in the middle band — second wet stackbath_door
  • common ↔ br2 — bath block buffers br2 from the great_roombath_to_bedroom_wall
  • hall ↔ great — hall's east face is an open mouth into the great_room (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)open_plan
  • great ↔ kitchen — great_room + kitchen are one open LDK (gr style); kitchen at the rear of the public corneropen_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_sq_l_wrap_baths_ds_hall_gr).
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • ensuite_alcove_joins_master — the strip beside a narrower ensuite is folded into master as an L-shaped alcove.

    "3BR squarish catalog topology #4 \u2014 private L-WRAP. Natural floor band",
    "~94-108 m\u00b2; minimum feasible shell ~9.5 x 10.0 m. The roomiest parti of the",
    "four \u2014 pick it when the brief values bedroom separation over floor economy.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (canonical, brief.carport_side='right'):",
    "  Rear:  [br3   | master : ensuite | kitchen ]",
    "  Mid:   [HALL | common  | great_room        ]",
    "  Front: [br2   | great_room (entry)         ]",
    "",
    "Program: the private zone forms an L \u2014 standard pair stacked on the west",
    "flank (br2 front-left, br3 rear-left) with the hall between them, master",
    "wrapping the rear-center. The public gr corner (great_room hosting entry,",
    "kitchen behind it) fills the front-right/rear-right. The hall runs",
    "north-south between the standards, mouths east into the great_room, and",
    "elbows east at its rear end to pick up master's door and the common T&B.",
    "",
    "Separation: master shares NO wall with either standard bedroom \u2014 the widest",
    "acoustic split available in a squarish 3BR shell (multi-generation briefs,",
    "rentable-room briefs).",
    "",
    "Corner-lot fit: bedrooms hold the west + rear walls, public holds front +",
    "east \u2014 on a corner lot orient the public corner to the street intersection",
    "and every habitable room still gets an outdoor wall. No landlocked rooms.",
    "",
    "Plumbing: distributed \u2014 ensuite rides the kitchen wall (stack 1), common sits",
    "mid-band off the hall (stack 2, softly pulled toward the kitchen).",
    "",
    "Watch-outs: longest hall of the family (~3.5-4.5 m incl. elbow) and the",
    "elbow constraint in the compact profile. When brief.carport_side='left',",
    "mirror the topology."
  ]
}

No. 35 / 46

1s_3br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr

Not regression-tested
1s_3br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr · single-storey · Squarish · 3 bed · 2 bath

Squarish 3BR / baths CLUSTERED with mid-band HALL — 3BR extension of the 2BR side_split_cl_hall: master at front of the private column, ensuite+common wet core and hall in the middle band, br3 and br2 paired across the rear; kitchen migrates to the rear of the public column. Open-plan great_room + kitchen.

Not yet regression-tested. No test brief in briefs/test/ currently exercises this topology through the solver (or every brief that references it falls back to a different topology before solving). Static structural check only — load_topology() loads cleanly — every room ID is unique, every adjacency references a real room, and every habitable room is reachable from the entry point. No test brief, rendered output, or solver-derived room sizing exists to show for this entry; nothing below is fabricated to fill the gap.

Definition

1s/3br/squarish/1s_3br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

3BR squarish catalog topology #2 — SIDE_SPLIT with clustered wet core. Direct 3BR extension of 1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr. Natural floor band ~92-106 m²; minimum feasible shell ~9.3 x 9.8 m.

Layout intent (canonical, brief.carport_side='right'): Front: [master | great_room (entry) ] Mid: [ensuite|common| HALL | great_room ] Rear: [br3 | br2 | kitchen ]

Program: identical clustered bath block (ensuite + common share a wet_core) and front-master position as the 2BR parent. The changes: (1) the rear of the private column now holds a PAIR of standard bedrooms instead of one, so the private column widens to ~6.0-6.4 m (2 x 3.0-3.2 m bedroom widths); (2) the hall stretches east-west along the pair's front walls so BOTH standards door into it; (3) the kitchen migrates from the public column's midpoint to its rear, keeping the gr open plan and putting the dirty kitchen directly behind.

Ventilation: master fronts the street, br3/br2 hold the rear wall, great_room holds front + side — no landlocked habitable room.

Privacy: best-in-class of the four 3BR squarish topologies — every bedroom door faces the hall, no bedroom shares a wall with the great_room except via the bath block buffer.

Fit warning: needs the widest shell of the family. If frontage < ~9.3 m the rear pair collapses below 2.8 m widths — fall back to hall_core (#1) or front_back_split (#3). When brief.carport_side='left', mirror the topology.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is master's exclusive — interior door from master, west side of the bath blockensuite_door
  • ensuite ↔ common — shared plumbing wall — both wet stacks in one bay, single vent runwet_core
  • master ↔ hall — master door opens north into the hallbedroom_door
  • br2 ↔ hall — br2 door opens south into the hallbedroom_door
  • br3 ↔ hall — br3 door opens south into the hall — the hall runs east-west along the rear pair's front walls so both standards reach itbedroom_door
  • common ↔ hall — common bath door opens east into the hallbath_door
  • hall ↔ great — hall's east wall is an open mouth into the great_room (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)open_plan
  • great ↔ kitchen — great_room + kitchen are one open LDK (gr style); kitchen sits at the rear of the public columnopen_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr_3br).
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • match_bath_widths — solver forces the bath rooms' widths to match.

No. 36 / 46

2s_3br_sq_rear_stair_baths_ds_gr

Verified
2s_3br_sq_rear_stair_baths_ds_gr · 2-storey · Squarish · 3 bed · 2 bath

Squarish-shell 2-STOREY 3BR / 2 baths, REAR STAIR, hall-less ground floor, distributed baths (one per floor) — completes the 2s 3BR shell set. Ground floor: great room across the front (entry), stair + kitchen + T&B tiling the rear zone. Upstairs: master + second bedroom street-facing at the front, third bedroom at the rear, landing hall as a compact core serving all five doors. ORIENTATION-ADAPTIVE stair: the solver lays the flight sideways along the rear band on 7 m-wide floors and flips it to a vertical rear-corner run at 6.5 m and below. Canonical 10.5x10.5 lot (6.5x6.5 buildable per floor, 84 m² gross — 3BR/2-bath band 80-120).

Definition

2s/3br/squarish/2s_3br_sq_rear_stair_baths_ds_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

2-STOREY squarish catalog topology #2 — the squarish take on the 2s 3BR (authored 2026-07-19; completes 2s coverage of {2BR,3BR} x {narrow, squarish, wide}). Same room graph as the wide 3BR sibling; the shell does the differentiating.

Program: 3BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED one per floor (baths_ds) — GF common T&B off the great room, upper common bath off the hall-core. All three bedrooms upstairs; master + br2 street-facing at the front, br3 at the rear.

ORIENTATION-ADAPTIVE STAIR (the finding this topology surfaced): the solver's run-axis decision flips with lot size — sideways along the rear band on 7x7 floors (band fits stair 3.5 + kitchen + bath), a vertical rear-corner column at 6.5x6.5 and below (band too narrow). Both orientations verified COMPLIANT; nothing in this file forces either.

Canonical layout (10.5 x 10.5 lot, 2 m setbacks, 6.5 x 6.5 buildable per floor — vertical-stair form): GF: Front: [great room (entry, full width, counter at rear edge)] Rear: [stair | kitchen -> rear service door | T&B ] 2F: Front: [br2 | master ] Mid: [stairwell | hall-core | br3 ] Rear: [stairwell | T&B | br3 ]

Verified sizes (all COMPLIANT, 0 warnings, deterministic): - 10.5x10.5 canonical: 84 m² gross, 100% coverage both floors; master 12.0 / br2 7.5 / br3 10.5 / bath2 2.4 / hall 6.0. - 11x11 (98): sideways stair; a ~5 m² strip behind the flight goes unclaimed on 2F (its only neighbors are excluded claimant types) — suggestion-level, honest behind-stair space. - 12x11 (112) and 12x12 (128 — above band): solve cleanly. - 10x10 (72 — below band): solves; treat the 80 m² band, not solver feasibility, as the practical floor when matching briefs.

This topology's authoring exposed and fixed a THREE-layer leak of the locked 'master is always larger' rule (see project memory): (1) the solver constrained master against only the FIRST standard bedroom — now against ALL of them; (2) snap_gaps could grow a standard past the master — now capped at master's current area minus half the margin; (3) the dead-strip claimer could hand a standard an alcove that outgrew the master — now guarded. Post-snap margins land at >= 0.5 m² (solver enforces 1.0 on the pre-snap rects).

Structure/override choices: identical to the wide 3BR sibling — only the stair is anchored (single-member left_anchored), two gentle stacks ([master, hall2], [br2, hall2]), zone_balance_rooms counts the whole upper floor minus stairwell as private, no zone_split, kitchen mechanical_vent, soft proximity bath2->stairwell for the wet stack.

Naming: `baths_ds` = distributed baths, `gr` = open great-room style, no `_hall` token (GROUND floor is hall-less; the 2F landing hall is mandatory in every 2s topology and is not tokened).

Key adjacencies

  • great ↔ kitchen — GF: kitchen opens directly off the great room — dining counter on the shared edge (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass = 2.1 m min); band + stools live on the great-room side to keep the galley aisle clear. Render-only (counter_divider) — briefs may set dining_counter: false to suppress it without touching this topology.open_plan
  • great ↔ bath1 — GF: T&B doors into the great room — hall-less GF, the great room IS the circulation; door-placement scoring keeps it off the entry sightlinebath_door
  • great ↔ stair — GF: open stair boards straight off the great room; the boarding constraint holds this edge to the flight's bottom endstair_boarding
  • kitchen ↔ bath1 — GF: T&B shares a plumbing wall with the kitchen — single GF wet bay at the rear; kitchen keeps its rear service doorwet_core
  • stair ↔ stairwell — VERTICAL circulation edge: flight and stairwell opening occupy the identical rectangle on their floors (rect-equality in the solver, graph connectivity for validation)stair_vertical
  • stairwell ↔ hall2 — 2F: the landing hall must meet the stairwell at the TOP end of the flight — step off into circulation, never a bedroom wallstair_arrival
  • hall2 ↔ master — 2F: master door off the landing hallbedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ br2 — 2F: second bedroom door off the landing hallbedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ br3 — 2F: third bedroom door off the landing hall-corebedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ bath2 — 2F: second T&B doors off the landing hallbath_door

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (stair_run_geometry).
  • zone_balance_rooms — custom room grouping overrides the default public/private area split (private: master, br2, br3, bath2, hall2; public: great, kitchen).
  • aspect_overrides — per-room aspect-ratio (w:h) floor for stairs (4.0:1).
  • Two-storey: GF flight and 2F stairwell pinned to the identical rectangle (stair_vertical); ascent direction forced via stair_boarding/stair_arrival so the flight can't top out against a wall.

Upstairs: master + second bedroom street-facing at the front, third bedroom at the rear, landing hall as a compact core serving all five doors. ORIENTATION-ADAPTIVE stair: the solver lays the flight sideways along the rear band on 7 m-wide floors and flips it to a vertical rear-corner run at 6.5 m and below.   "lot_adjustment_profiles": [
    {
      "name": "stair_run_geometry",
      "when": {
        "buildable_area_lt_sqm": 99999
      },
      "auto_apply": {
        "stairs": {
          "min_greatest_dim_m": 3.5,
          "max_least_dim_m": 1.2
        },
        "common_bath": {
          "max_greatest_dim_m": 2.6,
          "max_area_sqm": 3.9
        },
        "hallway": {
          "max_area_sqm": 6.0
        }
      },
      "rationale": "ALWAYS-ON (the 99999 trigger): a straight stair's run is riser math the solver cannot derive \u2014 3.0 m floor-to-floor at 15 x 200 mm risers needs 14 x 250 mm treads = 3.5 m; min_greatest_dim_m pins the run, max_least_dim_m 1.2 keeps the flight a flight; ORIENTATION stays the solver's choice, and on this squarish shell it genuinely varies by lot size (sideways at 7 m wide, vertical at 6.5 m and below \u2014 a 6.5 m rear band can't fit stair 3.5 + counter-width kitchen 2.1 + bath side by side). common_bath caps stop post-snap bath stretching. hallway max_area_sqm 6.0: the five-door hall-core earns more than the 2BR siblings' 4.5 but stays bounded."
    }
  ],

Test brief

test/2s_3br_10.5x10.5_sq_rear_stair_baths_ds_gr_ncp.json

2-STOREY 3BR squarish rear-stair smoke test on the canonical 10.5x10.5 lot, no carport. 6.5x6.5 envelope per floor (42.25 m² footprint, 84.5 m² gross — in the 3BR/2-bath 80-120 band). Completes the 2s {2BR,3BR} x {narrow,squarish,wide} matrix. Exercises the orientation-adaptive stair (vertical rear-corner run at this width; the same topology lays it sideways on 7 m-wide floors), master + br2 street-facing front with br3 rear, the five-door hall-core, and the three-layer master-supremacy guards (solver all-standards rule, snap cap, claimer guard) that this topology's authoring surfaced.

Lot 10.5×10.5 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 6.5×6.5 m buildable envelope — 42.25 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot10.5 × 10.5 m (110.25 m²)
Buildable envelope6.5 × 6.5 m (42.25 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested3
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

all three bedrooms upstairs · master strictly the largest bedroom · master and second bedroom street-facing at the front · compact hall-core upstairs, not a corridor · full-width great room with dining counter · one T&B per floor · no carport

1s_3br_nw_front_back_split_baths_ds_hall_gr

Not regression-tested
1s_3br_nw_front_back_split_baths_ds_hall_gr · single-storey · Narrow · 3 bed · 2 bath

Narrow-shell 3BR / baths DISTRIBUTED with transverse MID-HALL + rear spur — full-width bands front-to-rear: great_room (entry), wet band (kitchen + common + passage), transverse hall, master + ensuite band, standard pair at the rear. Canonical-size parti with paired bedrooms instead of a full corridor. gr style.

Not yet regression-tested. No test brief in briefs/test/ currently exercises this topology through the solver (or every brief that references it falls back to a different topology before solving). Static structural check only — load_topology() loads cleanly — every room ID is unique, every adjacency references a real room, and every habitable room is reachable from the entry point. No test brief, rendered output, or solver-derived room sizing exists to show for this entry; nothing below is fabricated to fill the gap.

Definition

1s/3br/narrow/1s_3br_nw_front_back_split_baths_ds_hall_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

3BR narrow catalog topology #3 — FRONT_BACK split with transverse mid-hall and rear spur. Natural floor band ~78-90 m²; designed against the canonical test: 10.5 x 17.0 m lot (2.0 m setbacks) → 6.5 x 13.0 m shell (84.5 m²). Regime: single-detached, no firewalls — this parti DEPENDS on it (see below).

Layout intent (west = left, front = bottom): Rear: [br2 | br3 ] .. [master | ensuite | SPUR ] .. [---- transverse HALL ---- ] .. [kitchen | common | passage ] Front: [great_room (entry, full width) ]

Program: five full-width bands, no long corridor. Great_room fronts the street; the wet band behind it packs kitchen (west, open to great) and common T&B, with a 1.0 m passage on the east linking great_room to the transverse hall. The hall serves common, master and the east spur, which runs past the ensuite to the rear standard pair. Master sits mid-depth — buffered from both the street and the kids' rooms.

Why no-firewall matters: master and ensuite occupy a MID-DEPTH band with no front or rear wall — they vent through the 2.0 m side yards (legal courts for a single-storey under PD 1096 Rule 7/8). In the firewall regime this band would be landlocked and the parti dies.

Plumbing: kitchen+common share stack 1; ensuite is nominally ds but sits directly across the 1.0 m hall from common (soft weight 25) — in practice one under-slab run. Note the dirty kitchen lands behind br3, not the kitchen — the service path runs along the east side yard.

Differentiator vs side_corridor (#2): bedrooms pair in bands instead of stacking on a column, so the standard pair gets rear-yard windows and the master gets a quiet mid position; the cost is the dog-leg circulation (passage → hall → spur). Prefer this on briefs valuing rear-facing kids' rooms; prefer #2 when the kitchen must own the service edge.

Carport policy (deferred): one side setback 2.0 → 3.0 m later (lot +1.0 m); mirror so the passage/spur side faces the carport.

Key adjacencies

  • great ↔ kitchen — kitchen fills the west of the wet band directly behind the great_room — open connection (gr style)open_plan
  • hall ↔ great — the hall's east passage rises past the wet band to mouth into the great_room's rear-east corner (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)open_plan
  • common ↔ kitchen — kitchen (west) and common T&B (east) share the wet band's dividing wall — stack 1wet_core
  • common ↔ hall — common doors south into the transverse hallbath_door
  • master ↔ hall — master (west of its band) doors north into the transverse hallbedroom_door
  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite sits center-east of master's band, vertically aligned with common across the hall — interior door from masterensuite_door
  • br2 ↔ hall — br2 (rear-west) doors onto the spur terminus — the hall's east spur runs past ensuite to the rear bandbedroom_door
  • br3 ↔ hall — br3 (rear-east) doors onto the spur terminus beside br2bedroom_door
  • br2 ↔ br3 — the standard pair shares the rear band side-by-side — wall onlybedroom_to_bedroom_wall

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_nw_front_back_split_baths_ds_hall_gr).
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • bedroom_band_fills_width — the bedroom band is forced to fill the full envelope width.
  • fallback_topology — if the solver reports infeasibility on the brief's lot, retries with 1s_3br_nw_split_columns_baths_cl_lk.
