    },
    {
      "id": "br3",
      "type": "bedroom_standard",
      "zone": "private",
      "size_priority": "other_bedrooms"
    },
    {
      "id": "common",
      "type": "common_bath",
      "zone": "service",
      "size_priority": "service_and_baths"
    },
    {
      "id": "hall",
      "type": "hallway",
      "zone": "circulation",
      "size_priority": "service_and_baths"
    },
    {
      "id": "great",
      "type": "great_room",
      "zone": "public",
      "size_priority": "public_LDK",
      "hosts_entry": true
    },
    {
      "id": "kitchen",
      "type": "kitchen",
      "zone": "public",
      "size_priority": "public_LDK"
    }
  ],
  "adjacencies": [
    {
      "a": "great",
      "b": "kitchen",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 1.5,
      "kind": "open_plan",
      "note": "kitchen fills the west of the wet band directly behind the great_room \u2014 open connection (gr style)"
    },
    {
      "a": "hall",
      "b": "great",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 0.9,
      "kind": "open_plan",
      "note": "the hall's east passage rises past the wet band to mouth into the great_room's rear-east corner (codebase rule: hallway\u2194great_room is always open)"
    },
    {
      "a": "common",
      "b": "kitchen",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 1.0,
      "kind": "wet_core",
      "note": "kitchen (west) and common T&B (east) share the wet band's dividing wall \u2014 stack 1"
    },
    {
      "a": "common",
      "b": "hall",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 1.0,
      "kind": "bath_door",
      "note": "common doors south into the transverse hall"
    },
    {
      "a": "master",
      "b": "hall",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 1.0,
      "kind": "bedroom_door",
      "note": "master (west of its band) doors north into the transverse hall"
    },
    {
      "a": "master",
      "b": "ensuite",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 0.8,
      "kind": "ensuite_door",
      "note": "ensuite sits center-east of master's band, vertically aligned with common across the hall \u2014 interior door from master"
    },
    {
      "a": "br2",
      "b": "hall",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 1.0,
      "kind": "bedroom_door",
      "note": "br2 (rear-west) doors onto the spur terminus \u2014 the hall's east spur runs past ensuite to the rear band"
    },
    {
      "a": "br3",
      "b": "hall",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 1.0,
      "kind": "bedroom_door",
      "note": "br3 (rear-east) doors onto the spur terminus beside br2"
    },
    {
      "a": "br2",
      "b": "br3",
      "min_shared_wall_m": 2.0,
      "kind": "bedroom_to_bedroom_wall",
      "note": "the standard pair shares the rear band side-by-side \u2014 wall only"
    }
  ],
  "soft_proximities": [
    {
      "a": "ensuite",
      "b": "common",
      "weight": 25,
      "note": "ensuite and common are separated only by the 1.0 m hall \u2014 keep them stacked so both baths ride one under-slab run despite the ds designation"
    }
  ],
  "entry_point": "great",
  "zone_split": {
    "axis": "horizontal",
    "private_side": "rear",
    "private_rooms": [
      "master",
      "ensuite",
      "br2",
      "br3"
    ],
    "public_rooms": [
      "great",
      "kitchen"
    ]
  },
  "fallback_topology": "1s/3br/narrow/1s_3br_nw_split_columns_baths_cl_lk.json",
  "match_bedroom_widths": true,
  "bedroom_band_fills_width": true,
  "left_anchored": [
    "kitchen",
    "master",
    "br2"
  ],
  "right_anchored": [
    "common",
    "br3"
  ],
  "rear_anchored": [
    "br2",
    "br3"
  ],
  "front_to_rear_stacks": [
    [
      "great",
      "kitchen",
      "hall",
      "master",
      "br2"
    ],
    [
      "great",
      "common",
      "hall",
      "ensuite",
      "br3"
    ]
  ],
  "lot_adjustment_profiles": [
    {
      "name": "compact_nw_front_back_split_baths_ds_hall_gr",
      "when": {
        "buildable_area_lt_sqm": 85
      },
      "auto_apply": {
        "kitchen": {
          "min_least_dim_m": 1.8
        },
        "hallway": {
          "min_least_dim_m": 0.95
        },
        "ensuite_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 2.5,
          "max_area_sqm": 3.2
        },
        "common_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 3.0,
          "max_area_sqm": 4.0
        },
        "master_bedroom": {
          "max_area_sqm": 13.5
        }
      },
      "preferred_apply": {
        "ensuite_bath": {
          "min_area_sqm": 3.0
        },
        "kitchen": {
          "min_least_dim_m": 2.0
        }
      },
      "rationale": "Depth is the budget here: 3.2 (great) + 2.1 (wet band) + 1.0 (hall) + 3.5 (master band) + 3.1 (rear pair) = ~12.9 m. The bands can shave to ~12.3 m total before rooms hit hard mins; below that, or below ~6.2 m width (rear pair 3.0+3.2), fall back to split_columns."
    }
  ],
  "setback_elements": [
    {
      "type": "dirty_kitchen",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false,
      "behind": "br3"
    },
    {
      "type": "service_area",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false
    },
    {
      "type": "lanai",
      "location": "side_setback",
      "covered": false
    }
  ],
  "building_voids": [],
  "notes": [
    "3BR narrow catalog topology #3 \u2014 FRONT_BACK split with transverse mid-hall",
    "and rear spur. Natural floor band ~78-90 m\u00b2; designed against the canonical",
    "test: 10.5 x 17.0 m lot (2.0 m setbacks) \u2192 6.5 x 13.0 m shell (84.5 m\u00b2).",
    "Regime: single-detached, no firewalls \u2014 this parti DEPENDS on it (see below).",
    "",
    "Layout intent (west = left, front = bottom):",
    "  Rear:  [br2          | br3               ]",
    "  ..     [master       | ensuite | SPUR    ]",
    "  ..     [---- transverse HALL ----        ]",
    "  ..     [kitchen      | common  | passage ]",
    "  Front: [great_room (entry, full width)   ]",
    "",
    "Program: five full-width bands, no long corridor. Great_room fronts the",
    "street; the wet band behind it packs kitchen (west, open to great) and",
    "common T&B, with a 1.0 m passage on the east linking great_room to the",
    "transverse hall. The hall serves common, master and the east spur, which",
    "runs past the ensuite to the rear standard pair. Master sits mid-depth \u2014",
    "buffered from both the street and the kids' rooms.",
    "",
    "Why no-firewall matters: master and ensuite occupy a MID-DEPTH band with no",
    "front or rear wall \u2014 they vent through the 2.0 m side yards (legal courts",
    "for a single-storey under PD 1096 Rule 7/8). In the firewall regime this",
    "band would be landlocked and the parti dies.",
    "",
    "Plumbing: kitchen+common share stack 1; ensuite is nominally ds but sits",
    "directly across the 1.0 m hall from common (soft weight 25) \u2014 in practice",
    "one under-slab run. Note the dirty kitchen lands behind br3, not the",
    "kitchen \u2014 the service path runs along the east side yard.",
    "",
    "Differentiator vs side_corridor (#2): bedrooms pair in bands instead of",
    "stacking on a column, so the standard pair gets rear-yard windows and the",
    "master gets a quiet mid position; the cost is the dog-leg circulation",
    "(passage \u2192 hall \u2192 spur). Prefer this on briefs valuing rear-facing kids'",
    "rooms; prefer #2 when the kitchen must own the service edge.",
    "",
    "Carport policy (deferred): one side setback 2.0 \u2192 3.0 m later (lot +1.0 m);",
    "mirror so the passage/spur side faces the carport."
  ]
}

No. 38 / 46

1s_3br_nw_side_corridor_baths_cl_hall_gr

Not regression-tested
1s_3br_nw_side_corridor_baths_cl_hall_gr · single-storey · Narrow · 3 bed · 2 bath

Narrow-shell 3BR / baths CLUSTERED with SIDE CORRIDOR and full wet strip — the GENEROUS sibling of the side_corridor family: kitchen, common T&B and ensuite form one contiguous east strip; master owns the full rear band as a retreat opening to a rear lanai. Open great_room + kitchen (gr style). Tuned for 7.0 x 14.0-15.0 m shells.

Not yet regression-tested. No test brief in briefs/test/ currently exercises this topology through the solver (or every brief that references it falls back to a different topology before solving). Static structural check only — load_topology() loads cleanly — every room ID is unique, every adjacency references a real room, and every habitable room is reachable from the entry point. No test brief, rendered output, or solver-derived room sizing exists to show for this entry; nothing below is fabricated to fill the gap.

Definition

1s/3br/narrow/1s_3br_nw_side_corridor_baths_cl_hall_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

3BR narrow catalog topology #4 — SIDE CORRIDOR, CLUSTERED strip: the GENEROUS sibling of #2. Natural floor band ~86-100 m²; designed against the generous test: 11.0 x 19.0 m lot (2.0 m setbacks) → 7.0 x 15.0 m shell (105 m²); happiest at 7.0 x 14.0-15.0. Regime: single-detached, no firewalls.

Layout intent (west = left, front = bottom): Rear: [master (full width, rear lanai) ] .. [br3 | HALL | ensuite ] .. [br2 | HALL | common T&B ] .. [ | | kitchen ] Front: [great_room (entry, full width) ]

Program: same three-sub-column parti as #2 but the east strip runs the full depth as one wet bay — kitchen, common T&B, then ensuite, each sharing a plumbing wall (single stack + vent for the entire house, the cheapest wet layout of all twelve 3BR topologies). Master owns the full rear band and opens through the rear wall to a lanai in the 2.0 m rear setback — the deep-lot reward move. At 7.0 m width the west column holds 3.3 m bedrooms, the corridor 1.0 m, the strip 2.2 m.

Ventilation: west column vents west, strip vents east, master vents rear + both sides. The generous depth also buys a linen/storage run along the corridor at 14 m+ shells.

Ensuite access: entered from master's band on the strip's south face — the strip's rear cell belongs to the master suite even though it plumbs with the service strip.

Downscale behavior: below ~88 m² the three-cell strip stops fitting — fallback to #2 (ds sibling), which drops the ensuite out of the strip into master's alcove. Chain continues #2 → split_columns for compact shells.

Carport policy (deferred): one side setback 2.0 → 3.0 m later (lot +1.0 m); mirror so the strip faces the carport for the shortest service path.

Key adjacencies

  • great ↔ kitchen — kitchen heads the east strip behind the great_room, open over a counter (gr style) — at generous widths it earns a breakfast ledgeopen_plan
  • hall ↔ great — corridor mouths into the great_room (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)open_plan
  • br2 ↔ hall — br2 doors east into the corridorbedroom_door
  • br3 ↔ hall — br3 doors east into the corridorbedroom_door
  • br2 ↔ br3 — standards stack front-to-rear in the west column — wall onlybedroom_to_bedroom_wall
  • master ↔ hall — master's door terminates the corridor — the full rear band is the retreatbedroom_door
  • common ↔ hall — common doors west into the corridor, midway between the standards and the great_roombath_door
  • common ↔ kitchen — kitchen → common on the east strip, first shared plumbing wallwet_core
  • common ↔ ensuite — common → ensuite continues the strip: kitchen + common + ensuite form ONE contiguous wet run — the clustered signature of this siblingwet_core
  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is the strip's rear cell, entered on its south wall from master's bandensuite_door

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_nw_side_corridor_baths_cl_hall_gr).
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • match_bath_widths — solver forces the bath rooms' widths to match.
  • fallback_topology — if the solver reports infeasibility on the brief's lot, retries with 1s_3br_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_hall_gr.

No. 39 / 46

1s_3br_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_hall_gr

Not regression-tested
1s_3br_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_hall_gr · single-storey · Narrow · 3 bed · 2 bath

Narrow-shell 3BR / baths DISTRIBUTED with SIDE CORRIDOR and service strip — the canonical narrow parti: bedroom column west, 1.0 m corridor center, wet/service strip east; great_room (entry) spans the full front, master spans the full rear. Open great_room + kitchen (gr style). Tuned for the 6.5 x 13.0 m canonical shell.

Not yet regression-tested. No test brief in briefs/test/ currently exercises this topology through the solver (or every brief that references it falls back to a different topology before solving). Static structural check only — load_topology() loads cleanly — every room ID is unique, every adjacency references a real room, and every habitable room is reachable from the entry point. No test brief, rendered output, or solver-derived room sizing exists to show for this entry; nothing below is fabricated to fill the gap.

Definition

1s/3br/narrow/1s_3br_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_hall_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

3BR narrow catalog topology #2 — SIDE CORRIDOR with service strip, the canonical narrow parti. Natural floor band ~80-92 m²; designed against the canonical test: 10.5 x 17.0 m lot (2.0 m setbacks) → 6.5 x 13.0 m shell (84.5 m²). Regime: single-detached, no firewalls.

Layout intent (west = left, front = bottom): Rear: [ensuite : master (full width) ] .. [br3 | HALL | common T&B ] .. [br2 | HALL | kitchen ] Front: [great_room (entry, full width) ]

Program: three sub-columns behind the full-width great_room — bedroom column west (~3.2 m: br2, br3), corridor center (~1.0 m), service strip east (~1.9 m: kitchen open to great over the counter, then common T&B). The corridor dies into master's door; master rewards the walk with the full 6.5 m rear band and rear-yard windows. Dirty kitchen + service sit in the 2.0 m rear setback behind the strip.

Plumbing: kitchen+common share the east strip wall (stack 1); ensuite is master's rear-west alcove (stack 2) — distributed, with a soft pull to shorten the branch. Ventilation: west column vents the west yard, strip vents east, master vents rear — no landlocked rooms (no-firewall premise).

Scaling: at the generous shell (7.0 x 14+) the strip widens to a real galley and the corridor gains storage; at compact it loses to split_columns — fallback declared.

Carport policy (deferred): widening one side setback 2.0 → 3.0 m (lot +1.0 m) adds the carport later; mirror so the strip faces the carport side and the kitchen/dirty-kitchen service path stays on the driveway.

Key adjacencies

  • great ↔ kitchen — kitchen occupies the front of the east strip, open to the great_room over a counter (gr style)open_plan
  • hall ↔ great — the corridor's front end mouths into the great_room (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)open_plan
  • br2 ↔ hall — br2 (west column, first bedroom) doors east into the corridorbedroom_door
  • br3 ↔ hall — br3 (west column, behind br2) doors east into the corridorbedroom_door
  • br2 ↔ br3 — standards stack front-to-rear in the west column — wall onlybedroom_to_bedroom_wall
  • master ↔ hall — master spans the full rear band — its door sits at the corridor's rear terminusbedroom_door
  • common ↔ hall — common T&B (east strip, behind kitchen) doors west into the corridorbath_door
  • common ↔ kitchen — kitchen and common stack on the east strip sharing the plumbing wall — stack 1wet_core
  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is an alcove at master's rear-west corner (west + rear yard venting) — stack 2, interior door from masterensuite_door

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_nw_side_corridor_baths_ds_hall_gr).
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • ensuite_alcove_joins_master — the strip beside a narrower ensuite is folded into master as an L-shaped alcove.
  • fallback_topology — if the solver reports infeasibility on the brief's lot, retries with 1s_3br_nw_split_columns_baths_cl_lk.

No. 40 / 46

1s_3br_nw_split_columns_baths_cl_lk

Not regression-tested
1s_3br_nw_split_columns_baths_cl_lk · single-storey · Narrow · 3 bed · 2 bath

Narrow-shell 3BR / baths CLUSTERED, SPLIT COLUMNS (zero-corridor) — the COMPACT CHAMPION of the narrow family: public spine runs down the west column (living, dining-kitchen, hall node, master), bedrooms + clustered bath block stack in the east column. lk style, no full corridor. Designed to solve 6.0 x 12.0 m (72 m²).

Not yet regression-tested. No test brief in briefs/test/ currently exercises this topology through the solver (or every brief that references it falls back to a different topology before solving). Static structural check only — load_topology() loads cleanly — every room ID is unique, every adjacency references a real room, and every habitable room is reachable from the entry point. No test brief, rendered output, or solver-derived room sizing exists to show for this entry; nothing below is fabricated to fill the gap.

Definition

1s/3br/narrow/1s_3br_nw_split_columns_baths_cl_lk.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

3BR narrow catalog topology #1 — SPLIT COLUMNS, the COMPACT CHAMPION and the declared fallback of the whole narrow family. Natural floor band ~72-84 m². Designed against the compact test: 10.0 x 16.0 m lot (2.0 m setbacks all sides) → 6.0 x 12.0 m shell (72 m²). Solves canonical 6.5 x 13.0 with room to spare. Regime: single-detached, NO firewalls — both 2.0 m side yards are code courts, so BOTH columns side-vent along the full depth. This parti is impossible in the firewall/rowhouse regime.

Layout intent (canonical, west = left, front = bottom): Rear: [master | ensuite ] .. [HALL node | common T&B ] .. [dining-kitchen| br3 ] .. [living | br2 ] Front: [ (entry) | ]

Program: no corridor at all. The west column is the public spine — living (entry) flowing into dining-kitchen (lk), ending in a 1.0 m hall node, with master at the rear-west corner. The east column stacks br2, br3, the common T&B and the ensuite front-to-rear. Every circulation meter does double duty as living space; that is what buys 3BR at 72 m².

Doors: br2 off the living room (bedroom_to_public — the one concession), br3 + common + master off the hall node. Ensuite through the column wall from master.

Plumbing: common+ensuite clustered on one wall (stack 1) at the rear-east; kitchen sits diagonally across at mid-west, softly aligned so one transverse run serves both. Dirty kitchen + service in the 2.0 m rear setback.

Ventilation: west rooms vent west yard, east rooms vent east yard, master + ensuite also rear — every habitable room has an exterior wall (the no-firewall premise makes the whole east column legal).

Carport policy (per brief, deferred): adding a carport later widens ONE side setback 2.0 → 3.0 m (lot width +1.0 m). Topology unchanged — mirror so the public column faces the carport side.

Key adjacencies

  • living ↔ kitchen — lk style — living (front-west, hosts entry) flows rear into the dining-kitchen; together they are the west public spineopen_plan
  • hall ↔ kitchen — the hall node sits at the rear end of the dining-kitchen — open mouth, no door (public spine continues into it)open_plan
  • br2 ↔ living — br2 (front-east) doors west off the living room — the compact concession of this parti, same move as the squarish hall_corebedroom_to_public
  • br3 ↔ hall — br3 (mid-east) doors west into the hall nodebedroom_door
  • master ↔ hall — master (rear-west) doors north into the hall nodebedroom_door
  • common ↔ hall — common T&B (east, beside the node) doors west into the hall — guests reach it without crossing bedroomsbath_door
  • common ↔ ensuite — clustered bath block in the east column — common (front) and ensuite (rear) share one plumbing wallwet_core
  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite sits east of master's rear position — interior door through the column wallensuite_door
  • br2 ↔ br3 — standards stack front-to-rear in the east column — wall only, no doorbedroom_to_bedroom_wall

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_nw_split_columns_baths_cl_lk).
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.

    "run serves both. Dirty kitchen + service in the 2.0 m rear setback.",
    "",
    "Ventilation: west rooms vent west yard, east rooms vent east yard, master +",
    "ensuite also rear \u2014 every habitable room has an exterior wall (the",
    "no-firewall premise makes the whole east column legal).",
    "",
    "Carport policy (per brief, deferred): adding a carport later widens ONE side",
    "setback 2.0 \u2192 3.0 m (lot width +1.0 m). Topology unchanged \u2014 mirror so the",
    "public column faces the carport side."
  ]
}
← Back to catalog
No. 41 / 46

2s_3br_nw_side_spine_stair_baths_ds_gr

Verified
2s_3br_nw_side_spine_stair_baths_ds_gr · 2-storey · Narrow · 3 bed · 2 bath

Narrow-shell 2-STOREY 3BR / 2 baths, SIDE-SPINE STAIR, hall-less ground floor, distributed baths (one per floor) — three bedrooms above a ~5 m-wide floor. Ground floor: great room across the front (entry, GF circulation), kitchen as a rear-right COLUMN touching the great room's rear edge (>= 2.1 m dining-counter seam) with the T&B tucked beside it, stair as the left-wall spine. Upstairs: master full-width at the front (street windows), then the narrow signature — stairwell + landing hall as a left/mid circulation spine serving br2 mid-right, br3 at the rear, and the second T&B. Canonical 9x13 lot (5x9 buildable per floor, 90 m² gross — in the 3BR/2-bath 80-120 band).

Definition

2s/3br/narrow/2s_3br_nw_side_spine_stair_baths_ds_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

2-STOREY narrow catalog topology #3 — the narrow take on the 2s 3BR (authored 2026-07-19; sibling of 2s_3br_wd_rear_stair_baths_ds_gr, same room program, re-partied for a deep ~5 m-wide shell).

Program: 3BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED one per floor (baths_ds) — GF common T&B off the great room, upper common bath off the spine hall. All three bedrooms upstairs.

Why the parti differs from the wide sibling: on a wide floor the hall is a compact mid-band CORE; on a deep narrow floor the circulation stretches into a left/mid SPINE — stairwell against the left wall (vertical run, like the 2s narrow 2BR pair) with the landing hall running beside it, master full-width at the front, br2 against the right wall mid-plan, br3 at the rear. Every bedroom keeps an exterior wall (front / right / rear) for windows.

GF kitchen-as-column trick: a deep hall-less GF cannot put a wet band between great room and kitchen (the kitchen would become unreachable except through the bath). Instead the kitchen is a rear-right COLUMN whose head touches the great room's rear edge — preserving the >= 2.1 m open-plan counter seam AND the rear-wall pin/service door — with the T&B beside the column's head, doors into the great room.

Canonical layout (9 x 13 lot, 2 m setbacks, 5 x 9 buildable/floor): GF: Front: [great room (entry, full width, counter at rear) ] Rear: [stair | T&B | kitchen column -> rear service door ] 2F: Front: [master (full width) ] Mid: [stairwell | hall spine | br2 ] Rear: [T&B | hall spine | br3 ]

Verified sizes (all COMPLIANT, 0 warnings unless noted, deterministic): - 9x13 canonical: 90 m² gross; master 15.0 / br2 9.0 / br3 9.0 / bath2 3.3; GF 96.7% filled (the kitchen claims the rear-left rect as an L-pantry via the dead-strip decomposition added for this topology; a ~1.5 m² behind-stair sliver remains — its only neighbors are stair and bath, both excluded claimants). - 9x12 (80 gross — band floor): 100% both floors, but bath2 1.3 m² and br3 6.2 m² sit at hard minimums — the economic edge. - 8x14 (80 gross, 4 m-wide TRUE-narrow): solves, br2/br3 at 6.0. - 9x14 (100) and 10x13 (108) also solve; 10x13 carries the size-dependent kitchen-window soft warning (10%-of-floor rule outgrows the rear wall — same class as the other 2s topologies).

Structure/override choices: minimal — single-member left_anchored [stair]; stacks [great,kitchen] [great,bath1] [great,stair] + [master,hall2] only (the wide sibling's [br2,hall2] stack is dropped: br2 sits BESIDE the spine, not in front of it). zone_balance_rooms counts the whole upper floor minus stairwell as private. No zone_split (the spine geometry emerges from adjacencies alone). rear-anchoring bath1 was tried and is provably infeasible — bath1 must TOUCH the great room for its door, so it can never sit on the rear wall.

Naming: `baths_ds` = distributed baths, `gr` = open great-room style, no `_hall` token (GROUND floor is hall-less; the 2F landing hall is mandatory in every 2s topology and is not tokened).

Key adjacencies

  • great ↔ kitchen — GF: kitchen opens directly off the great room — dining counter on the shared edge (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass = 2.1 m min); band + stools live on the great-room side to keep the galley aisle clear. Render-only (counter_divider) — briefs may set dining_counter: false to suppress it without touching this topology.open_plan
  • great ↔ bath1 — GF: T&B doors into the great room — hall-less GF, the great room IS the circulation; door-placement scoring keeps it off the entry sightlinebath_door
  • great ↔ stair — GF: open stair boards straight off the great room; the boarding constraint holds this edge to the flight's bottom endstair_boarding
  • kitchen ↔ bath1 — GF: T&B shares a plumbing wall with the kitchen — single GF wet bay at the rear; kitchen keeps its rear service doorwet_core
  • stair ↔ stairwell — VERTICAL circulation edge: flight and stairwell opening occupy the identical rectangle on their floors (rect-equality in the solver, graph connectivity for validation)stair_vertical
  • stairwell ↔ hall2 — 2F: the landing hall must meet the stairwell at the TOP end of the flight — step off into circulation, never a bedroom wallstair_arrival
  • hall2 ↔ master — 2F: master door off the landing hallbedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ br2 — 2F: second bedroom door off the landing hallbedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ br3 — 2F: third bedroom door off the landing hall-corebedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ bath2 — 2F: second T&B doors off the landing hallbath_door

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (stair_run_geometry).
  • zone_balance_rooms — custom room grouping overrides the default public/private area split (private: master, br2, br3, bath2, hall2; public: great, kitchen).
  • aspect_overrides — per-room aspect-ratio (w:h) floor for stairs (4.0:1).
  • Two-storey: GF flight and 2F stairwell pinned to the identical rectangle (stair_vertical); ascent direction forced via stair_boarding/stair_arrival so the flight can't top out against a wall.

    {
      "name": "stair_run_geometry",
      "when": {
        "buildable_area_lt_sqm": 99999
      },
      "auto_apply": {
        "stairs": {
          "min_greatest_dim_m": 3.5,
          "max_least_dim_m": 1.2
        },
        "common_bath": {
          "max_greatest_dim_m": 2.6,
          "max_area_sqm": 3.9
        },
        "hallway": {
          "max_area_sqm": 6.0
        }
      },
      "rationale": "ALWAYS-ON (the 99999 trigger): a straight stair's run is riser math the solver cannot derive \u2014 3.0 m floor-to-floor at 15 x 200 mm risers needs 14 x 250 mm treads = 3.5 m; min_greatest_dim_m pins the run, max_least_dim_m 1.2 keeps the flight a flight (on this deep shell the solver picks the vertical front-to-rear run). common_bath caps stop post-snap gap-filling from stretching a bath into a corridor. hallway max_area_sqm 6.0: the 2F spine hall hosts five doors (three bedrooms, bath, stair arrival) so it earns more area than the 2BR siblings' 4.5, but stays bounded so spare m\u00b2 goes to bedrooms."
    }
  ],

      "kitchen"
    ],
    [

      "bath1"
    ],
    [
      "great",
      "stair"
    ],
    [
      "master",
      "hall2"
    ]
  ],

      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false
    }
  ],

Test brief

test/2s_3br_9x13_nw_side_spine_stair_baths_ds_gr_ncp.json

2-STOREY 3BR narrow side-spine-stair smoke test on the canonical 9x13 lot, no carport. 5x9 envelope per floor (45 m² footprint, 90 m² gross — in the 3BR/2-bath 80-120 band). Exercises three bedrooms above a 5 m-wide floor: master full-width front, stairwell + landing hall as the left/mid circulation spine, br2 mid-right, br3 rear; the GF kitchen-as-rear-column trick preserving the dining-counter seam on a deep hall-less floor; and the dead-strip rectangle decomposition (the kitchen claims the rear-left rect as an L-pantry).

Lot 9×13 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 5×9 m buildable envelope — 45 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot9 × 13 m (117 m²)
Buildable envelope5 × 9 m (45 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested3
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

all three bedrooms upstairs, each with an exterior wall · master full-width at the front · circulation as a compact side spine, not a full-width corridor · kitchen column at the rear keeping the dining counter seam · one T&B per floor · no carport

No. 42 / 46

1s_3br_wd_center_spine_baths_ds_hall_gr

Not regression-tested
1s_3br_wd_center_spine_baths_ds_hall_gr · single-storey · Wide · 3 bed · 2 bath

Wide-shell 3BR / baths DISTRIBUTED with east-west CENTER SPINE hall — the classic PH wide bungalow parti: two room bands separated by a spine corridor. Great_room (entry) + master across the front, kitchen + common T&B + standard pair across the rear. Open-plan great_room + kitchen (gr style).

Not yet regression-tested. No test brief in briefs/test/ currently exercises this topology through the solver (or every brief that references it falls back to a different topology before solving). Static structural check only — load_topology() loads cleanly — every room ID is unique, every adjacency references a real room, and every habitable room is reachable from the entry point. No test brief, rendered output, or solver-derived room sizing exists to show for this entry; nothing below is fabricated to fill the gap.

Definition

1s/3br/wide/1s_3br_wd_center_spine_baths_ds_hall_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

3BR wide catalog topology #1 — CENTER SPINE, the classic PH wide bungalow. Natural floor band ~88-100 m²; canonical test shell 12.0 x 8.0 m (96 m²) on a 16.5 x 14.5 m lot; minimum feasible ~11.4 x 7.8 m before fallback.

Layout intent (canonical, west = left): Rear: [kitchen | common T&B | br2 | br3 ] Spine: [ ---- HALL (1.0 m deep) ------------- ] Front: [great_room (entry) | master : ensuite ]

Program: two full-width bands. Front band: great_room hosts entry on the west, master + ensuite alcove on the east (street-facing master — the traditional poblacion arrangement). Rear band west-to-east: kitchen, common T&B, then the standard pair. The east-west spine threads between the bands, starting east of the great_room↔kitchen open-plan connection, so the public west half stays spine-free.

Why depth 7.5-8.5 m is the sweet band: 3.3 m room + 1.0 m spine + 3.3 m room + walls. Deeper drifts squarish; shallower breaks the two-band section.

Ventilation: every habitable room holds either the front or rear wall; the spine borrows light from the great_room mouth. No landlocked rooms.

Plumbing: distributed — kitchen+common share stack 1 (with the door_host_group alternate letting common door off the kitchen wall on generous shells); ensuite on the east gable is stack 2, softly pulled toward br3's wall.

Carport: NCP base form like the 2BR wide series — declare via brief (side_setback carport on the east, or front-parallel with 3.0 m front setback). Mirror when brief.carport_side='left'.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is an alcove at master's front-east corner (east gable wall for venting) — interior door from masterensuite_door
  • master ↔ hall — master's rear wall doors north into the spine — master is the only bedroom in the front bandbedroom_door
  • br2 ↔ hall — br2 doors south into the spinebedroom_door
  • br3 ↔ hall — br3 doors south into the spine at its east endbedroom_door
  • br2 ↔ br3 — standard pair sits side-by-side in the rear band — shared wall only, no doorbedroom_to_bedroom_wall
  • common ↔ hall — common T&B doors south into the spine — DEFAULT host of the 'common_access' groupbath_door
  • common ↔ kitchen — common shares the wet wall with kitchen (stack 1). ALTERNATE door host: only when shared wall >= 1.5 m; falls back to the spine on compact shellswet_core
  • hall ↔ great — the spine's west end mouths into the great_room (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)open_plan
  • great ↔ kitchen — great_room (front-west) and kitchen (rear-west) connect open-plan west of the spine's start (gr style)open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_wd_center_spine_baths_ds_hall_gr).
  • door_host_group — at least one door's host wall is chosen by the solver's auto-scorer (or overridden per-brief via door_host), not fixed in the topology.
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • ensuite_alcove_joins_master — the strip beside a narrower ensuite is folded into master as an L-shaped alcove.
  • fallback_topology — if the solver reports infeasibility on the brief's lot, retries with 1s_3br_wd_half_spine_baths_ds_hall_lk.

Spine depth floors at 1.0 m; kitchen galley at 1.8 m; master capped at 15 m\u00b2 so the rear pair holds >= 2.9 m widths. Below ~88 m\u00b2 the spine cannot pay for itself \u2014 fall back to half_spine."

  "notes": [
    "3BR wide catalog topology #1 \u2014 CENTER SPINE, the classic PH wide bungalow.",
    "Natural floor band ~88-100 m\u00b2; canonical test shell 12.0 x 8.0 m (96 m\u00b2) on a",
    "16.5 x 14.5 m lot; minimum feasible ~11.4 x 7.8 m before fallback.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (canonical, west = left):",
    "  Rear:  [kitchen | common T&B | br2   | br3           ]",
    "  Spine: [        ---- HALL (1.0 m deep) ------------- ]",
    "  Front: [great_room (entry)  | master      : ensuite  ]",
    "",
    "Program: two full-width bands. Front band: great_room hosts entry on the",
    "west, master + ensuite alcove on the east (street-facing master \u2014 the",
    "traditional poblacion arrangement). Rear band west-to-east: kitchen, common",
    "T&B, then the standard pair. The east-west spine threads between the bands,",
    "starting east of the great_room\u2194kitchen open-plan connection, so the public",
    "west half stays spine-free.",
    "",
    "Why depth 7.5-8.5 m is the sweet band: 3.3 m room + 1.0 m spine + 3.3 m room",
    "+ walls. Deeper drifts squarish; shallower breaks the two-band section.",
    "",
    "Ventilation: every habitable room holds either the front or rear wall; the",
    "spine borrows light from the great_room mouth. No landlocked rooms.",
    "",
    "Plumbing: distributed \u2014 kitchen+common share stack 1 (with the door_host_group",
    "alternate letting common door off the kitchen wall on generous shells);",
    "ensuite on the east gable is stack 2, softly pulled toward br3's wall.",
    "",
    "Carport: NCP base form like the 2BR wide series \u2014 declare via brief",
    "(side_setback carport on the east, or front-parallel with 3.0 m front",
    "setback). Mirror when brief.carport_side='left'."
  ]
}

No. 43 / 46

1s_3br_wd_half_spine_baths_ds_hall_lk

Not regression-tested
1s_3br_wd_half_spine_baths_ds_hall_lk · single-storey · Wide · 3 bed · 2 bath

Wide-shell 3BR / baths DISTRIBUTED with HALF-SPINE hall, living-kitchen public band (lk style) — the COMPACT CHAMPION of the wide family: spine runs only over the private east half, public west half is one open lk band. Designed to solve the 11.0 x 7.5 m BP 220 shell and down to ~10.5 x 7.2 m (76 m²).

Not yet regression-tested. No test brief in briefs/test/ currently exercises this topology through the solver (or every brief that references it falls back to a different topology before solving). Static structural check only — load_topology() loads cleanly — every room ID is unique, every adjacency references a real room, and every habitable room is reachable from the entry point. No test brief, rendered output, or solver-derived room sizing exists to show for this entry; nothing below is fabricated to fill the gap.

Definition

1s/3br/wide/1s_3br_wd_half_spine_baths_ds_hall_lk.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

3BR wide catalog topology #3 — HALF-SPINE, the COMPACT CHAMPION and the declared fallback of #1 (center_spine) and #2 (side_split). Natural floor band ~76-92 m². Designed against the BP 220 test case: 14.0 x 11.0 m lot (1.5 m sides/front, 2.0 m rear) → 11.0 x 7.5 m shell (82.5 m²). Solves down to ~10.5 x 7.2 m (76 m²). No fallback — this is the floor of the family.

Layout intent (canonical, west = left): Rear: [kitchen | common T&B | br2 | br3 ] Spine: [ (open) | -- HALL (0.95 m, east half) ---- ] Front: [living (entry) | master : ensuite ]

Program: same two-band section as center_spine but the corridor only spans the private east half — the west half is a full-depth open lk band (living front, kitchen rear, dining folded into living). Cutting the spine's west run saves ~3.5 m² of circulation, which is exactly what lets this parti clear 76-82 m² shells where center_spine fails (~88 m² floor).

Circulation: spine mouths west into the living room and serves common, br2, br3 (north side) and master (south side). Every bedroom still doors onto circulation, not the LDK — no privacy concession despite the compact size.

Ventilation: all bedrooms + living on front/rear walls; common vents high window over the service area; NBC Rule 7/8 clean.

Plumbing: kitchen+common wet wall is stack 1; master's ensuite alcove sits directly across the spine from common (soft proximity keeps them aligned) so both baths ride one under-slab branch — effectively 1.5 stacks, cheapest after front_back_split's single stack in the squarish family.

Fit rule: frontage >= 10.5 m (living 4.3 + master 3.3 + br widths behind), depth >= 7.2 m (3.1 + 0.95 + 3.0 + walls). Carport: NCP base form; on the 14 x 11 test lot use a front-parallel carport in the 1.5 m + street verge or widen one side setback to 3.0 m and drop shell width to 10.5 m. Mirror when brief.carport_side='left'.

Key adjacencies

  • living ↔ kitchen — lk style — living (front-west, hosts entry) and kitchen (rear-west) are one open band; dining folds into the living sideopen_plan
  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite is an alcove on master's rear edge — interior door from masterensuite_door
  • master ↔ hall — master (front-east) doors north into the half-spinebedroom_door
  • br2 ↔ hall — br2 doors south into the half-spinebedroom_door
  • br3 ↔ hall — br3 doors south into the half-spine at its east endbedroom_door
  • br2 ↔ br3 — standard pair side-by-side in the rear band — wall only, no doorbedroom_to_bedroom_wall
  • common ↔ hall — common T&B doors south into the half-spine at its west endbath_door
  • common ↔ kitchen — common backs onto the kitchen — stack 1, one shared plumbing wallwet_core
  • hall ↔ living — the half-spine's west mouth opens into the living room — 0.9 m minimum single-person mouth (compact envelope cannot afford wider)open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_wd_half_spine_baths_ds_hall_lk).
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • ensuite_alcove_joins_master — the strip beside a narrower ensuite is folded into master as an L-shaped alcove.

    "directly across the spine from common (soft proximity keeps them aligned) so",
    "both baths ride one under-slab branch \u2014 effectively 1.5 stacks, cheapest",
    "after front_back_split's single stack in the squarish family.",
    "",
    "Fit rule: frontage >= 10.5 m (living 4.3 + master 3.3 + br widths behind),",
    "depth >= 7.2 m (3.1 + 0.95 + 3.0 + walls). Carport: NCP base form; on the",
    "14 x 11 test lot use a front-parallel carport in the 1.5 m + street verge or",
    "widen one side setback to 3.0 m and drop shell width to 10.5 m. Mirror when",
    "brief.carport_side='left'."
  ]
}
← Back to catalog
No. 44 / 46

1s_3br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr

Not regression-tested
1s_3br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr · single-storey · Wide · 3 bed · 2 bath

Wide-shell 3BR / baths CLUSTERED with wing HALL node — 3BR extension of the 2BR wd side_split family: a 2x2 private wing on the west (master + br2 front, ensuite+common wet block + br3 rear) with a central hall node, LDK column on the east. Open-plan great_room + kitchen (gr style).

Not yet regression-tested. No test brief in briefs/test/ currently exercises this topology through the solver (or every brief that references it falls back to a different topology before solving). Static structural check only — load_topology() loads cleanly — every room ID is unique, every adjacency references a real room, and every habitable room is reachable from the entry point. No test brief, rendered output, or solver-derived room sizing exists to show for this entry; nothing below is fabricated to fill the gap.

Definition

1s/3br/wide/1s_3br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

3BR wide catalog topology #2 — SIDE_SPLIT with 2x2 wing and clustered wet block. Direct 3BR extension of 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr (the wing gains a second column; the single bath becomes an ensuite+common block). Natural floor band ~90-104 m²; canonical test shell 12.0 x 8.0 m; needs frontage >= ~11.5 m.

Layout intent (canonical, west = left): Rear: [ensuite | common | br3 | kitchen ] Middle: [(master)| HALL node | (great cont.) ] Front: [master | br2 | great (entry) ]

Program: the private wing is a 2x2 grid — front row [master | br2], rear row [wet block | br3] — with a hall node carved at its center-east, mouthing east into the great_room. Every bedroom door and the common bath door face the hall node: best privacy of the wide family (no bedroom doors off the LDK). The east column is the public LDK: great_room front (hosts entry), kitchen rear.

Ventilation: master + br2 front wall, ensuite + br3 rear wall, common vents via high window to the rear (behind the wet block) or mechanical per PD 1096.

Plumbing: single clustered stack (ensuite+common share the wet wall), softly pulled toward the kitchen column so all wet services and the rear-setback dirty kitchen land within one bay.

Carport: NCP base form; declare via brief. Mirror when brief.carport_side='left'.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — ensuite sits directly behind master (rear-west of the wing) — interior door on master's rear wallensuite_door
  • ensuite ↔ common — clustered wet block — ensuite (west) and common (east) share one plumbing wall in the wing's rear rowwet_core
  • master ↔ br2 — master (front-west) and br2 (front-east of the wing) share the front row — wall only, no doorbedroom_to_bedroom_wall
  • master ↔ hall — master doors east into the hall nodebedroom_door
  • br2 ↔ hall — br2 doors south into the hall nodebedroom_door
  • br3 ↔ hall — br3 (rear-east of the wing) doors south-west into the hall nodebedroom_door
  • common ↔ hall — common bath's only door opens south into the hall — both standards and guests reach it via the hallbath_door
  • hall ↔ great — hall's east face is an open mouth into the great_room (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open). 0.9 m minimum single-person mouthopen_plan
  • great ↔ kitchen — great_room (front) + kitchen (rear) stack in the east LDK column (gr style)open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_wd_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr).
  • match_bath_widths — solver forces the bath rooms' widths to match.
  • fallback_topology — if the solver reports infeasibility on the brief's lot, retries with 1s_3br_wd_half_spine_baths_ds_hall_lk.

No. 45 / 46

1s_3br_wd_split_wings_baths_ds_hall_gr

Not regression-tested
1s_3br_wd_split_wings_baths_ds_hall_gr · single-storey · Wide · 3 bed · 2 bath

Wide-shell 3BR / baths DISTRIBUTED, SPLIT WINGS — public LDK in the center, standard pair + common bath in the west wing (with hall node), master + ensuite in the east wing. Maximum master separation; all wet rooms on one contiguous rear run. Open-plan great_room + kitchen (gr style).

Not yet regression-tested. No test brief in briefs/test/ currently exercises this topology through the solver (or every brief that references it falls back to a different topology before solving). Static structural check only — load_topology() loads cleanly — every room ID is unique, every adjacency references a real room, and every habitable room is reachable from the entry point. No test brief, rendered output, or solver-derived room sizing exists to show for this entry; nothing below is fabricated to fill the gap.

Definition

1s/3br/wide/1s_3br_wd_split_wings_baths_ds_hall_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

3BR wide catalog topology #4 — SPLIT WINGS (public center). Natural floor band ~92-106 m²; canonical test shell 12.0 x 8.0 m at the tight end, happiest at 13.0-13.5 x 8.0-8.5 m; needs frontage >= ~12.0 m or falls back to center_spine.

Layout intent (canonical, west = left): Rear: [br3 | common | kitchen | ensuite ] Mid: [ HALL node | (great cont.) ] Front: [br2 | great_room (entry) | master ]

Program: the public LDK holds the center of the shell — great_room fronts the street and hosts entry, kitchen behind it. Private zone splits into two wings: WEST wing carries the standard pair stacked front-to-rear with a small hall node between them (br2, br3 and the common bath all door onto it); EAST wing carries master + ensuite. Master doors off the great_room's east side (bedroom_to_public) — the one privacy concession, softened on generous shells by migrating the door into a stretched hall mouth.

Separation: master shares no wall with either standard — the two bedroom wings are a full LDK apart. Best parti for multi-generation households, rentable-room setups, or parents-vs-teens acoustic separation.

Plumbing: the signature move — kitchen sits BETWEEN the baths (common on its west wall, ensuite on its east wall), so all three wet rooms plus the rear-setback dirty kitchen form one contiguous rear plumbing bay. Cheapest wet infrastructure of the wide family despite 'ds' bath placement.

Corner lots: with bedrooms on both gables and public in the middle, either gable can face the side street; orient the great_room's front to the main frontage. Ventilation: every habitable room holds an exterior wall.

Carport: NCP base form; declare via brief on either flank. Mirror when brief.carport_side='left'.

Key adjacencies

  • master ↔ ensuite — east wing stacks master (front) over ensuite (rear) — interior door on master's rear wallensuite_door
  • ensuite ↔ kitchen — ensuite shares the kitchen's east wall — joins the contiguous rear wet runwet_core
  • common ↔ kitchen — common shares the kitchen's west wall — kitchen sits between the two baths: one continuous rear plumbing baywet_core
  • master ↔ great — master doors west off the great_room — the split-wings trade-off: no hall reaches the east wing. Acceptable per PH bungalow practice; add a vestibule on generous shellsbedroom_to_public
  • br2 ↔ hall — br2 (front-west) doors south into the wing hall nodebedroom_door
  • br3 ↔ hall — br3 (rear-west) doors north into the wing hall nodebedroom_door
  • br2 ↔ br3 — the standard pair stacks front-to-rear in the west wing — wall only, no doorbedroom_to_bedroom_wall
  • common ↔ hall — common bath doors west into the wing hall node — guests reach it through the hall, not through a bedroombath_door
  • hall ↔ great — the wing hall's east face is an open mouth into the great_room (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)open_plan
  • great ↔ kitchen — great_room (front-center, hosts entry) and kitchen (rear-center) stack open-plan (gr style)open_plan

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (compact_wd_split_wings_baths_ds_hall_gr).
  • match_bedroom_widths — solver forces master.width == standard.width.
  • fallback_topology — if the solver reports infeasibility on the brief's lot, retries with 1s_3br_wd_center_spine_baths_ds_hall_gr.

    {
      "type": "service_area",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false
    },
    {
      "type": "lanai",
      "location": "side_setback",
      "covered": false
    }
  ],
  "building_voids": [],
  "notes": [
    "3BR wide catalog topology #4 \u2014 SPLIT WINGS (public center). Natural floor",
    "band ~92-106 m\u00b2; canonical test shell 12.0 x 8.0 m at the tight end, happiest",
    "at 13.0-13.5 x 8.0-8.5 m; needs frontage >= ~12.0 m or falls back to",
    "center_spine.",
    "",
    "Layout intent (canonical, west = left):",
    "  Rear:  [br3   | common | kitchen | ensuite ]",
    "  Mid:   [ HALL node    | (great cont.)     ]",
    "  Front: [br2   | great_room (entry) | master ]",
    "",
    "Program: the public LDK holds the center of the shell \u2014 great_room fronts",
    "the street and hosts entry, kitchen behind it. Private zone splits into two",
    "wings: WEST wing carries the standard pair stacked front-to-rear with a",
    "small hall node between them (br2, br3 and the common bath all door onto",
    "it); EAST wing carries master + ensuite. Master doors off the great_room's",
    "east side (bedroom_to_public) \u2014 the one privacy concession, softened on",
    "generous shells by migrating the door into a stretched hall mouth.",
    "",
    "Separation: master shares no wall with either standard \u2014 the two bedroom",
    "wings are a full LDK apart. Best parti for multi-generation households,",
    "rentable-room setups, or parents-vs-teens acoustic separation.",
    "",
    "Plumbing: the signature move \u2014 kitchen sits BETWEEN the baths (common on its",
    "west wall, ensuite on its east wall), so all three wet rooms plus the",
    "rear-setback dirty kitchen form one contiguous rear plumbing bay. Cheapest",
    "wet infrastructure of the wide family despite 'ds' bath placement.",
    "",
    "Corner lots: with bedrooms on both gables and public in the middle, either",
    "gable can face the side street; orient the great_room's front to the main",
    "frontage. Ventilation: every habitable room holds an exterior wall.",
    "",
    "Carport: NCP base form; declare via brief on either flank. Mirror when",
    "brief.carport_side='left'."
  ]
}
← Back to catalog
No. 46 / 46

2s_3br_wd_rear_stair_baths_ds_gr

Verified
2s_3br_wd_rear_stair_baths_ds_gr · 2-storey · Wide · 3 bed · 2 bath

Wide-shell 2-STOREY 3BR / 2 baths, REAR SIDEWAYS STAIR, hall-less ground floor, distributed baths (one per floor) — the first 2-storey 3BR. Ground floor: full-width great room across the front (entry), sideways 3.5 m stair + kitchen + T&B tiling the rear band. Upstairs: master and second bedroom street-facing at the front, third bedroom at the rear, and the landing hall as a compact mid-band CORE serving all five doors (three bedrooms, bath, stair arrival) — the 2-storey cousin of the 1s 3BR hall_core pinwheel. Canonical 12x10 lot (8x6 buildable per floor, 96 m² gross — mid-band for 3BR/2-bath 80-120 m²).

Definition

2s/3br/wide/2s_3br_wd_rear_stair_baths_ds_gr.json

Room program

Bedroom
Bath (any zone)
Public / LDK
Circulation

Design notes

2-STOREY wide catalog topology #2 — the FIRST 2-storey 3BR (authored 2026-07-19; scaled up from 2s_2br_wd_rear_stair_bath_gr, keeping its sideways rear stair and full-width front great room).

Program: 3BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED one per floor (baths_ds) — GF common T&B off the great room, upper common bath off the landing hall. All three bedrooms upstairs. Bath count matches the locked 3BR/2-bath floor band (80-120 m² gross).

Why the hall is a CORE, not a corridor: putting all three bedrooms across the front (the tempting wide move) forces a back corridor spanning all three door walls — ~6.5 m of hallway, past the 4:1 hallway aspect cap and against the minimal-notch convention. Instead master + br2 take the street front, br3 takes the rear beside the bath, and the hall sits as a compact mid-band node (~5-6 m²) with all five doors radiating from it — the 2-storey cousin of the 1s 1s_3br_sq_hall_core pinwheel.

Canonical layout (12 x 10 lot, 2 m setbacks, 8 x 6 buildable/floor): GF: Front: [great room (entry, FULL width, counter at rear edge)] Rear: [stair (sideways) -> | kitchen | T&B ] 2F: Front: [br2 | master ] Mid: [hall-core (5 doors) | br3 ] Rear: [stairwell | T&B | br3 ] Stair: straight, 0.9-1.2 m wide, >= 3.5 m sideways run, 15 risers @ 200 mm / 250 mm treads, left-anchored; boards off the great room, arrives at the hall-core (stair_boarding / stair_arrival end constraints, horizontal-run branch).

Verified sizes (all COMPLIANT, 0 warnings, deterministic): 12x10 canonical (96 m² gross), 13x10 (108), 11.5x9.5 (82 — band floor), 14x10.5 (130 — above band, solver-fine, offer 4BR instead per the floor-band convention). Technically solves down to 11x9 (70 m² gross) — BELOW the 3BR band floor; treat the 80 m² band, not solver feasibility, as the practical minimum when matching briefs.

Structure/override choices: deliberately minimal — only the stair is anchored (single-member left_anchored) and only two gentle stacks ([master, hall2], [br2, hall2]) pull those two bedrooms in front of the hall; br3/bath2/stairwell arrange freely via adjacencies (no zone_split — br3 belongs in the rear band, unlike the 2BR siblings whose split pins both bedrooms front). zone_balance_rooms counts the whole upper floor minus stairwell as private. Kitchen mechanical_vent per the wide-2BR rationale (counter-width, service-door-dominated rear wall at compact sizes). Soft proximity bath2->stairwell keeps the upper bath near the GF wet bay (cross-floor wet-stack alignment is soft-only in v2).

Known quirk (shared with the 2BR wide sibling): when the solver floats the sideways flight off the rear wall, the strip behind it is claimed as an alcove where an eligible room abuts it, or stays a small suggestion-level hole where only baths/stairs adjoin (both are excluded claimants) — honest behind-stair space either way.

Naming: `baths_ds` = distributed baths (one per floor), `gr` = open great-room style, no `_hall` token since the GROUND floor is hall-less (the 2F landing hall is mandatory in every 2s topology and is not tokened, consistent with the 2s 2BR family).

Key adjacencies

  • great ↔ kitchen — GF: kitchen opens directly off the great room — dining counter on the shared edge (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass = 2.1 m min); band + stools live on the great-room side to keep the galley aisle clear. Render-only (counter_divider) — briefs may set dining_counter: false to suppress it without touching this topology.open_plan
  • great ↔ bath1 — GF: T&B doors into the great room — hall-less GF, the great room IS the circulation; door-placement scoring keeps it off the entry sightlinebath_door
  • great ↔ stair — GF: open stair boards straight off the great room; the boarding constraint holds this edge to the flight's bottom endstair_boarding
  • kitchen ↔ bath1 — GF: T&B shares a plumbing wall with the kitchen — single GF wet bay at the rear; kitchen keeps its rear service doorwet_core
  • stair ↔ stairwell — VERTICAL circulation edge: flight and stairwell opening occupy the identical rectangle on their floors (rect-equality in the solver, graph connectivity for validation)stair_vertical
  • stairwell ↔ hall2 — 2F: the landing hall must meet the stairwell at the TOP end of the flight — step off into circulation, never a bedroom wallstair_arrival
  • hall2 ↔ master — 2F: master door off the landing hallbedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ br2 — 2F: second bedroom door off the landing hallbedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ br3 — 2F: third bedroom door off the landing hall-corebedroom_door
  • hall2 ↔ bath2 — 2F: second T&B doors off the landing hallbath_door

Notable overrides

  • lot_adjustment_profiles — size-conditional room-size overrides (stair_run_geometry).
  • zone_balance_rooms — custom room grouping overrides the default public/private area split (private: master, br2, br3, bath2, hall2; public: great, kitchen).
  • aspect_overrides — per-room aspect-ratio (w:h) floor for stairs (4.0:1).
  • Two-storey: GF flight and 2F stairwell pinned to the identical rectangle (stair_vertical); ascent direction forced via stair_boarding/stair_arrival so the flight can't top out against a wall.

      "a": "bath2",
      "b": "stairwell",
      "weight": 15,
      "note": "2F: pull the upper T&B toward the rear stair corner \u2014 lands it near the GF wet bay (kitchen + bath1 share the rear band), shortening the vertical plumbing run. Soft only: cross-floor wet-stack alignment isn't a hard v2 constraint yet."

  "entry_point": "great",

    "private": [
      "master",
      "br2",
      "br3",
      "bath2",
      "hall2"
    ],
    "public": [
      "great",

    ]

  "aspect_overrides": {
    "stairs": 4.0
  },
  "left_anchored": [
    "stair"
  ],
  "lot_adjustment_profiles": [
    {
      "name": "stair_run_geometry",
      "when": {
        "buildable_area_lt_sqm": 99999
      },
      "auto_apply": {
        "stairs": {
          "min_greatest_dim_m": 3.5,
          "max_least_dim_m": 1.2
        },
        "common_bath": {
          "max_greatest_dim_m": 2.6,
          "max_area_sqm": 3.9
        },
        "hallway": {
          "max_area_sqm": 6.0
        }
      },
      "rationale": "ALWAYS-ON (the 99999 trigger): a straight stair's run is riser math the solver cannot derive \u2014 3.0 m floor-to-floor at 15 x 200 mm risers needs 14 x 250 mm treads = 3.5 m; min_greatest_dim_m pins the run, max_least_dim_m 1.2 keeps the flight a flight (orientation stays the solver's choice \u2014 it picks the sideways run on this shell). common_bath caps stop post-snap gap-filling from stretching a bath into a corridor. hallway max_area_sqm 6.0 (vs 4.5 on the 2BR siblings): this hall-core hosts FIVE doors \u2014 three bedrooms, the bath, and the stair arrival \u2014 so it earns more area, but stays bounded so snap growth returns spare m\u00b2 to bedrooms instead."
    }
  ],
  "front_to_rear_stacks": [
    [
      "great",
      "kitchen"
    ],
    [
      "great",
      "bath1"
    ],
    [
      "great",
      "stair"
    ],
    [
      "master",
      "hall2"
    ],
    [
      "br2",
      "hall2"
    ]
  ],
  "setback_elements": [
    {
      "type": "service_area",
      "location": "rear_setback",
      "covered": false
    }
  ],

Test brief

test/2s_3br_12x10_wd_rear_stair_baths_ds_gr_ncp.json

First 2-STOREY 3BR smoke test — wide rear-sideways-stair, distributed baths, canonical 12x10 lot, no carport. 8x6 envelope per floor (48 m² footprint, 96 m² gross — mid-band for 3BR/2-bath 80-120 m²). Exercises three bedrooms on one upper floor: master + br2 street-facing at the front, br3 at the rear, and the landing hall as a compact mid-band CORE with all five doors radiating from it (three bedrooms, bath, stair arrival) — plus the sideways stair run, full-width front great room with dining counter, and the upper bath near the GF wet bay.

Lot 12×10 m with 2 m front / 2 m rear / 2 m side setbacks gives a 8×6 m buildable envelope — 48 m² floor, requesting no carport.

Lot12 × 10 m (120 m²)
Buildable envelope8 × 6 m (48 m²)
Setbacks2 / 2 / 2 / 2 m (front/rear/left/right)
Bedrooms requested3
CarportNone requested
Occupancy classR-1

Must-haves

all three bedrooms upstairs · master and second bedroom street-facing at the front · compact hall-core upstairs, not a long corridor · sideways stair along the rear band · full-width great room across the front with dining counter · one T&B per floor · no carport
